```{.r .cell-code .code-overflow-wrap  code-fold="true"}
geom_vaf_landscape <- function(data = NULL, mut_file = NULL, vcf_file = NULL, window_of_interest = NULL,
    flank = 0, point_size = 3, contrast = T, x_axis_theme = "full") {
    # TODO: multiple forms of input, like point to diff file types and use
    # readers (VCF, MAF, etc)

    # First, subset to mutations of interest plus flank on both ends of genomic
    # interval of WoI
    moi_query <- gr_refactor_seqs(dt2gr(data)) %&% (gUtils::parse.gr(window_of_interest) +
        flank)

    # Convert RoI query GR to DT
    moi_query_dt <- gUtils::gr2dt(moi_query)

    # Get the settings for the locus view x-axis
    locus_x_axis <- get_locus_axis(input_window = window_of_interest, input_flank = flank)

    # Create plot
    gg_vaf_track <- ggplot2::ggplot(moi_query_dt) + ggpointdensity::stat_pointdensity(aes(x = start,
        y = VAF), method = "kde2d", size = point_size) + ggplot2::scale_color_gradientn(name = "Density",
        colours = c(paletteer::paletteer_c(palette = "grDevices::Lajolla", n = 15))) +
        ggplot2::labs(y = "VAF") + ggplot2::scale_x_continuous(name = locus_x_axis$name,
        labels = locus_x_axis$labels, limits = locus_x_axis$limits, expand = c(0.01,
            0.01)) + ggplot2::scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 1), expand = c(0.01,
        0.01), breaks = scales::pretty_breaks(n = 3)) + ggplot2::theme(panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
        panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(), axis.line.y = element_line(linewidth = 0.3),
        axis.title.y = element_text(size = 12.5, angle = 0, vjust = 0.5), axis.ticks.y = element_line(linewidth = 1.25,
            color = "black"), axis.ticks.length.y = unit(1.25, units = "mm"), plot.margin = unit(c(0.1,
            0.1, 0.1, 0.1), "mm"))

    # Add background
    if (contrast == T) {
        gg_vaf_track <- gg_vaf_track + theme(panel.background = element_rect(fill = "gray50"))

    } else if (contrast == F) {
        gg_vaf_track <- gg_vaf_track + theme(panel.background = element_rect(fill = NA))
    }

    # Add x-axis theme
    if (x_axis_theme == "full") {
        gg_vaf_track <- gg_vaf_track + theme(axis.line.x = element_line(linewidth = 0.3),
            axis.title.x = element_blank(), axis.ticks.x = element_line(color = "black"),
            axis.ticks.length.x = unit(1.25, units = "mm"))

    } else if (x_axis_theme == "ticks") {
        gg_vaf_track <- gg_vaf_track + theme(axis.title.x = element_blank(), axis.text.x = element_blank(),
            axis.line.x = element_blank(), axis.ticks.x = element_line(color = "black"),
            axis.ticks.length.x = unit(1.25, units = "mm"))

    } else if (x_axis_theme == "blank") {
        gg_vaf_track <- gg_vaf_track + theme(axis.title.x = element_blank(), axis.text.x = element_blank(),
            axis.line.x = element_blank(), axis.ticks.x = element_blank())
    }

    # Output plot
    return(gg_vaf_track)
}

geom_epilogo_col <- function(data = NULL, epilogo_file = NULL, window_of_interest = NULL,
    flank = 0, x_axis_theme = "full", contrast = T) {
    # TODO: # For alternate route of reading files from scratch and not already
    # as obj mm_epilogos <- aggregate_these(path_to_files =
    # '~/morganLab/mgp1000/development/nonCoding/epiLogo/results/',
    # pattern_to_grab = '*_mm_epilogos.*.txt.gz', delim = '\t', has_header = F)
    # chromhmm_metadata <- fread(file = 'nonCoding/epiLogo/metadata.tsv', sep =
    # '\t', header = T) colnames(mm_epilogos) <- c('chrom', 'start', 'end',
    # chromhmm_metadata$long_name)

    # First, subset to epilogos of interest plus flank on both ends of genomic
    # interval of WoI
    eoi_query <- gr_refactor_seqs(dt2gr(data)) %&% (gUtils::parse.gr(window_of_interest) +
        flank)

    # Restrict the beginning and end of the GoI window if the genes extend out
    # of the window If GoI start is less than the WoI start (i.e.
    # out-of-bounds), rrestrict start
    if (sum(GenomicRanges::start(eoi_query) < GenomicRanges::start(gUtils::parse.gr(window_of_interest) +
        flank)) > 0) {
        eoi_query <- GenomicRanges::restrict(x = eoi_query, start = GenomicRanges::start(gUtils::parse.gr(window_of_interest) +
            flank))
    }
    # If GoI end is greater than the WoI end (i.e. out-of-bounds), restrict end
    if (sum(GenomicRanges::end(eoi_query) > GenomicRanges::end(gUtils::parse.gr(window_of_interest) +
        flank)) > 0) {
        eoi_query <- GenomicRanges::restrict(x = eoi_query, end = GenomicRanges::end(gUtils::parse.gr(window_of_interest) +
            flank))
    }

    # Get midpoint of EoI query GR ranges then convert to DT
    eoi_query_dt <- gUtils::gr2dt(gUtils::gr.mid(eoi_query))

    # Melt the EoI DT
    eoi_query_melt <- data.table::melt(eoi_query_dt, id.vars = c("seqnames", "start",
        "end"), measure.vars = colnames(eoi_query_dt)[6:length(colnames(eoi_query_dt))],
        variable.name = "chromhmm_state", variable.factor = T, value.name = "bits",
        value.factor = F)

    # Get the settings for the locus view x-axis
    locus_x_axis <- get_locus_axis(input_window = window_of_interest, input_flank = flank)

    # Remove interactions that are outside the window
    eoi_query_melt_bound <- eoi_query_melt %>%
        dplyr::filter(start > locus_x_axis$limits[1] & end < locus_x_axis$limits[2])

    # Order the ChromHMM states
    eoi_query_melt_bound$chromhmm_state <- stringr::str_replace_all(string = eoi_query_melt_bound$chromhmm_state,
        pattern = "_", replacement = " ")
    eoi_query_melt_bound$chromhmm_state <- factor(eoi_query_melt_bound$chromhmm_state,
        levels = rev(c("Heterochromatin Low Signal", "H3K27me3 Repressed", "H3K9me3 Repressed",
            "Weak Transcription", "Transcription Elongation", "Transcription Transition",
            "Weak Enhancer", "Strong Enhancer2", "Strong Enhancer1", "Poised Promoter",
            "Weak Promoter", "Active Promoter")))

    # Color blind friendly
    chromhmm_state_colors <- c("#ff6db6", "#ffb6db", "#b66dff", "#920000", "#924900",
        "#db6d00", "#006ddb", "#6db6ff", "#b6dbff", "#009292", "#004949", "#000000")

    # c('#260F99','#6551CC','#BFB2FF', '#0F6B99','#51A3CC','#B2E5FF',
    # '#6B990F','#A3CC51','#E5FFB2', '#99540F','#CC8E51','#FFD8B2')

    # Create plot
    gg_epilogo_track <- ggplot(eoi_query_melt_bound) + ggplot2::geom_col(aes(x = start,
        y = bits, fill = chromhmm_state), na.rm = T, width = 200) + ggplot2::labs(y = "Epilogos") +
        scale_fill_manual(name = "ChromHMM\nStates", values = chromhmm_state_colors) +
        ggplot2::scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0.01, 0.01)) + ggplot2::scale_x_continuous(name = locus_x_axis$name,
        labels = locus_x_axis$labels, limits = locus_x_axis$limits, expand = c(0.01,
            0.01)) + ggplot2::theme(panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(), panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
        axis.line.y = element_line(linewidth = 0.3), axis.title.y = element_text(size = 12.5,
            angle = 0, vjust = 0.5), axis.ticks.y = element_blank(), axis.text.y = element_blank(),
        plot.margin = unit(c(0.051, 0.05, 0.05, 0.05), "mm"))

    # Add background
    if (contrast == T) {
        gg_epilogo_track <- gg_epilogo_track + theme(panel.background = element_rect(fill = "black"))

    } else if (contrast == F) {
        gg_epilogo_track <- gg_epilogo_track + theme(panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white"))
    }

    # Add x-axis theme
    if (x_axis_theme == "full") {
        gg_epilogo_track <- gg_epilogo_track + theme(axis.line.x = element_line(linewidth = 0.3),
            axis.title.x = element_blank(), axis.ticks.x = element_line(color = "black"),
            axis.ticks.length.x = unit(1.25, units = "mm"))

    } else if (x_axis_theme == "ticks") {
        gg_epilogo_track <- gg_epilogo_track + theme(axis.title.x = element_blank(),
            axis.text.x = element_blank(), axis.line.x = element_blank(), axis.ticks.x = element_line(color = "black"),
            axis.ticks.length.x = unit(1.25, units = "mm"))

    } else if (x_axis_theme == "blank") {
        gg_epilogo_track <- gg_epilogo_track + theme(axis.title.x = element_blank(),
            axis.text.x = element_blank(), axis.line.x = element_blank(), axis.ticks.x = element_blank())
    }

    # Output plot
    return(gg_epilogo_track)
}

geom_interactions <- function(data = NULL, bedpe_file = NULL, window_of_interest = NULL,
    flank = 0, aes_start_column, aes_end_column, aes_thickness_column, link_curvature = -0.33,
    x_axis_theme = "full") {
    # TODO: multiple forms of input, like point to diff file types and use
    # readers (VCF, MAF, etc)

    # First, subset to interactions of interest plus flank on both ends of
    # genomic interval of WoI
    ioi_query <- gr_refactor_seqs(dt2gr(data)) %&% (gUtils::parse.gr(window_of_interest) +
        flank)

    # Convert RoI query GR to DT
    ioi_query_dt <- gUtils::gr2dt(ioi_query)

    # Create a DT of necessary columns as input for MintChip
    ioi_mintchip_dt <- ioi_query_dt %>%
        dplyr::select(all_of(c(aes_start_column, aes_end_column, aes_thickness_column))) %>%
        dplyr::rename(start = all_of(aes_start_column), end = all_of(aes_end_column),
            thickness = all_of(aes_thickness_column))

    # Need to convert integer format to numeric
    ioi_mintchip_dt$start <- as.numeric(ioi_mintchip_dt$start)
    ioi_mintchip_dt$end <- as.numeric(ioi_mintchip_dt$end)

    # Get the settings for the locus view x-axis
    locus_x_axis <- get_locus_axis(input_window = window_of_interest, input_flank = flank)

    # Remove interactions that are outside the window
    ioi_mintchip_dt <- ioi_mintchip_dt %>%
        dplyr::filter(start > locus_x_axis$limits[1] & end < locus_x_axis$limits[2])

    # Create plot
    gg_interaction_track <- ggplot(data = ioi_mintchip_dt) + ggplot2::geom_curve(aes(x = start,
        xend = end, y = 0, yend = 0, linewidth = thickness, color = thickness), lineend = "round",
        alpha = 0.8, curvature = link_curvature, ncp = 20) + ggplot2::scale_color_gradientn(name = "EPIraction\nScore",
        colours = c(paletteer::paletteer_c("grDevices::Berlin", 30)[15:30])) + ggplot2::scale_linewidth(guide = NULL) +
        ggplot2::scale_x_continuous(name = locus_x_axis$name, labels = locus_x_axis$labels,
            limits = locus_x_axis$limits, expand = c(0.01, 0.01)) + ggplot2::scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0,
        0.01), expand = c(0, 0)) + ggplot2::labs(y = "Interactions") + ggplot2::theme(panel.background = element_blank(),
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank(), panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
        axis.line.y = element_line(linewidth = 0.3), axis.title.y = element_text(size = 14),
        axis.ticks.y = element_blank(), axis.text.y = element_blank(), plot.margin = unit(c(0.1,
            0.1, 0.1, 0.1), "mm"))

    # Add x-axis theme
    if (x_axis_theme == "full") {
        gg_interaction_track <- gg_interaction_track + theme(axis.line.y = element_line(linewidth = 0.3),
            axis.title.x = element_blank(), axis.ticks.x = element_line(color = "black"),
            axis.ticks.length.x = unit(1.25, units = "mm"))

    } else if (x_axis_theme == "ticks") {
        gg_interaction_track <- gg_interaction_track + theme(axis.title.x = element_blank(),
            axis.text.x = element_blank(), axis.line.x = element_blank(), axis.ticks.x = element_line(color = "black"),
            axis.ticks.length.x = unit(1.25, units = "mm"))

    } else if (x_axis_theme == "blank") {
        gg_interaction_track <- gg_interaction_track + theme(axis.title.x = element_blank(),
            axis.text.x = element_blank(), axis.line.x = element_blank(), axis.ticks.x = element_blank())
    }

    # Output plot
    return(gg_interaction_track)
}

# TODO: take bigwig/bedgraph-like/extended BED like designed to take files that
# look like: chr start end MM59300 MM58698 ... MM54 chr1 10052 10447
# 3.36224889424292 5.15650463271808 chr1 180676 181211 3.77952639507682
# 5.03959938
geom_ridge_track <- function(data = NULL, file = NULL, window_of_interest = NULL,
    flank = 0, aes_y_column = NULL, aes_facet_y_column = NULL, fill_color = "darkred",
    x_axis_theme = "full") {
    # TODO: multiple forms of input, like point to diff file types and use
    # readers (bigWig, bedGraph, etc)

    # TODO: this requies loading from scratch each time, too slow for now, let
    # user load GTF as GRanges obj anyway if(!is.null(data)) { # Quickset the
    # data like a regular ggplot geom bed_gr <- data } else if(is.null(data) &
    # !is.null(bed_file)) { # Read in the GTF data bed_gr <-
    # devgru::read_bed_file(gtf_file_path = bed_file) }

    # First, subset to region of interest plus flank on both ends of genomic
    # interval of WoI
    roi_query <- data %&% (gUtils::parse.gr(window_of_interest) + flank)

    # Restrict the beginning and end of the GoI window if the genes extend out
    # of the window If GoI start is less than the WoI start (i.e.
    # out-of-bounds), rrestrict start
    if (sum(GenomicRanges::start(roi_query) < GenomicRanges::start(gUtils::parse.gr(window_of_interest) +
        flank)) > 0) {
        roi_query <- GenomicRanges::restrict(x = roi_query, start = GenomicRanges::start(gUtils::parse.gr(window_of_interest) +
            flank))
    }
    # If GoI end is greater than the WoI end (i.e. out-of-bounds), restrict end
    if (sum(GenomicRanges::end(roi_query) > GenomicRanges::end(gUtils::parse.gr(window_of_interest) +
        flank)) > 0) {
        roi_query <- GenomicRanges::restrict(x = roi_query, end = GenomicRanges::end(gUtils::parse.gr(window_of_interest) +
            flank))
    }

    # Find midpoint of range for each value
    roi_mid_query <- gUtils::gr.mid(roi_query)

    # Convert RoI query GR to DT
    roi_query_dt <- gUtils::gr2dt(roi_mid_query)

    # Get the settings for the locus view x-axis
    locus_x_axis <- get_locus_axis(input_window = window_of_interest, input_flank = flank)

    # Need to melt the RoI query DT get into ggplot format with multiple
    # samples with columns for scores
    if (!is.null(aes_facet_y_column)) {
        melt_roi_query_dt <- data.table::melt(data = roi_query_dt[, -c("strand",
            "end", "width")], id.vars = c("seqnames", "start"), value.name = eval(aes_y_column),
            variable.name = aes_facet_y_column)
    } else if (is.null(aes_facet_y_column)) {
        melt_roi_query_dt <- roi_query_dt %>%
            dplyr::select(seqnames, start, eval(aes_y_column))
    }

    gg_ridge_plot <- ggplot2::ggplot(data = melt_roi_query_dt, aes(x = start, y = .data[[aes_y_column]])) +
        ggplot2::geom_col(fill = fill_color, width = 150) + ggplot2::scale_x_continuous(name = locus_x_axis$name,
        labels = locus_x_axis$labels, limits = locus_x_axis$limits, expand = c(0.01,
            0.01)) + ggplot2::scale_y_continuous(breaks = scales::pretty_breaks(n = 3),
        expand = c(0.02, 0.02)) + ggplot2::theme(panel.background = element_blank(),
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank(), panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
        axis.line.y = element_line(linewidth = 0.3), axis.title.y = element_text(size = 12.5,
            angle = 0, vjust = 0.5), axis.ticks.y = element_line(linewidth = 1.25,
            color = "black"), axis.ticks.length.y = unit(1.25, units = "mm"), plot.margin = unit(c(0.1,
            0.1, 0.1, 0.1), "mm"))
    # Add x-axis theme
    if (x_axis_theme == "full") {
        gg_ridge_plot <- gg_ridge_plot + theme(axis.line.x = element_line(linewidth = 0.3),
            axis.title.x = element_blanks(), axis.ticks.x = element_line(color = "black"),
            axis.ticks.length.x = unit(1.25, units = "mm"))

    } else if (x_axis_theme == "ticks") {
        gg_ridge_plot <- gg_ridge_plot + theme(axis.title.x = element_blank(), axis.text.x = element_blank(),
            axis.line.x = element_blank(), axis.ticks.x = element_line(color = "black"),
            axis.ticks.length.x = unit(1.25, units = "mm"))

    } else if (x_axis_theme == "blank") {
        gg_ridge_plot <- gg_ridge_plot + theme(axis.title.x = element_blank(), axis.text.x = element_blank(),
            axis.line.x = element_blank(), axis.ticks.x = element_blank())
    }

    # first, zoom to region provided TODO: can take input DT or GR zoom_bed_gr
    # <- data %&% (gUtils::parse.gr(window) + pad) Find midpoint of range for
    # each value zoom_mid_bed_gr <- gUtils::gr.mid(zoom_bed_gr) Convert to
    # data.table zoom_mid_bed_dt <- gUtils::gr2dt(zoom_mid_bed_gr)

    # Need to melt to get into ggplot format melt_zoom_bed_dt <-
    # data.table::melt(data = zoom_mid_bed_dt[,-c('strand', 'width')], id.vars
    # = c('seqnames', 'start', 'end'), value.name = aes_y_axis_column, # TODO:
    # PARAMETERIZE variable.name = aes_facet_y_column) # TODO: PARAMETRIZE

    # gg_track_plot <- ggplot(data = melt_zoom_bed_dt) +
    # ggHoriPlot::geom_horizon(aes(x = start, y = .data[[aes_y_axis_column]],
    # fill = ..Cutpoints..), origin = 0, horizonscale = c(0,0.01,
    # round(max(melt_zoom_bed_dt[[aes_y_axis_column]]), digits = 0))) +
    # scale_fill_manual(values = c(aes_fill_color, 'transparent')) +

    # 0.001), limits = c(get_window_limits(input_window = window)$left - pad,
    # get_window_limits(input_window = window)$right + pad), expand = c(0.01,
    # 0.01)) + scale_y_continuous(n.breaks = 3, labels =
    # scales::label_number(scale =
    # round(max(melt_zoom_bed_dt[[aes_y_axis_column]]) + 1, digits = 0),
    # accuracy = 1)) + facet_grid(.data[[aes_facet_y_column]] ~ ., switch =
    # 'y') + theme(legend.position = 'none', panel.background =
    # element_rect(fill = NA), panel.grid.major.y = element_line(color =

#' Convert GInteractions to BEDPE format
#'
#' @param gi GInteractions object
#' @param include_metadata Logical, whether to include metadata columns (default: TRUE)
#' @param zero_based Logical, whether to use 0-based coordinates (default: TRUE)
#' @param sort_output Logical, whether to sort output (default: TRUE)
#' @return data.table in BEDPE format
gi_to_bedpe <- function(gi, include_metadata = TRUE, zero_based = TRUE, sort_output = TRUE) {

    require(plyinteractions)
    require(InteractionSet)
    require(data.table)

    if (!is(gi, "GInteractions")) {
        stop("Input must be a GInteractions object")
    }

    # Convert to data.table using plyinteractions
    bedpe_dt <- plyinteractions::as_tibble(gi) %>%
        as.data.table()

    # Standardize column names to BEDPE format
    col_mapping <- c(seqnames1 = "chr1", start1 = "start1", end1 = "end1", seqnames2 = "chr2",
        start2 = "start2", end2 = "end2", strand1 = "strand1", strand2 = "strand2")

    # Rename columns that exist
    for (old_name in names(col_mapping)) {
        if (old_name %in% colnames(bedpe_dt)) {
            setnames(bedpe_dt, old_name, col_mapping[old_name], skip_absent = TRUE)
        }
    }

    # Convert chromosomes to character
    bedpe_dt[, chr1 := as.character(chr1)]
    bedpe_dt[, chr2 := as.character(chr2)]

    # Convert to 0-based if requested
    if (zero_based) {
        bedpe_dt[, start1 := start1 - 1]
        bedpe_dt[, start2 := start2 - 1]
    }

    # Add type and score placeholders if not present
    if (!"type" %in% colnames(bedpe_dt)) {
        bedpe_dt[, type := "."]
    }
    if (!"score" %in% colnames(bedpe_dt)) {
        bedpe_dt[, score := "."]
    }

    # Handle strand conversion
    bedpe_dt[strand1 == "*", strand1 := "."]
    bedpe_dt[strand2 == "*", strand2 := "."]

    # Reorder columns - core BEDPE columns first
    core_cols <- c("chr1", "start1", "end1", "chr2", "start2", "end2", "type", "score",
        "strand1", "strand2")
    other_cols <- setdiff(colnames(bedpe_dt), core_cols)
    bedpe_dt <- bedpe_dt[, c(intersect(core_cols, colnames(bedpe_dt)), other_cols),
        with = FALSE]

    # Sort if requested
    if (sort_output) {
        bedpe_dt <- sort_bedpe(bedpe_dt)
    }

    return(bedpe_dt)
}

#' Convert BEDPE to GInteractions
#'
#' @param bedpe_file Path to BEDPE file or data.table/data.frame
#' @param zero_based Logical, whether input is 0-based (default: TRUE)
#' @param keep_metadata Logical, whether to preserve metadata (default: TRUE)
#' @return GInteractions object
bedpe_to_gi <- function(bedpe_file, zero_based = TRUE, keep_metadata = TRUE) {

    require(plyinteractions)
    require(InteractionSet)
    require(data.table)

    # Read file
    if (is.character(bedpe_file)) {
        message("Reading BEDPE file: ", bedpe_file)
        bedpe_dt <- fread(bedpe_file, header = TRUE)
    } else {
        bedpe_dt <- as.data.table(bedpe_file)
    }

    # Dynamic column detection for breakpoint 1
    chr1_col <- grep("^([Cc]hr1)|([Cc]hrom1)|(seqnames1)|(#chr1)", colnames(bedpe_dt),
        value = TRUE)[1]
    start1_col <- grep("^[Ss]tart1", colnames(bedpe_dt), value = TRUE)[1]
    end1_col <- grep("^[Ee]nd1", colnames(bedpe_dt), value = TRUE)[1]
    strand1_col <- grep("^[Ss]trand1", colnames(bedpe_dt), value = TRUE)[1]

    # Dynamic column detection for breakpoint 2
    chr2_col <- grep("^([Cc]hr2)|([Cc]hrom2)|(seqnames2)", colnames(bedpe_dt), value = TRUE)[1]
    start2_col <- grep("^[Ss]tart2", colnames(bedpe_dt), value = TRUE)[1]
    end2_col <- grep("^[Ee]nd2", colnames(bedpe_dt), value = TRUE)[1]
    strand2_col <- grep("^[Ss]trand2", colnames(bedpe_dt), value = TRUE)[1]

    # Check required columns
    missing_cols <- character()
    if (is.na(chr1_col))
        missing_cols <- c(missing_cols, "chr1/chrom1/seqnames1")
    if (is.na(start1_col))
        missing_cols <- c(missing_cols, "start1")
    if (is.na(end1_col))
        missing_cols <- c(missing_cols, "end1")
    if (is.na(chr2_col))
        missing_cols <- c(missing_cols, "chr2/chrom2/seqnames2")
    if (is.na(start2_col))
        missing_cols <- c(missing_cols, "start2")
    if (is.na(end2_col))
        missing_cols <- c(missing_cols, "end2")

    if (length(missing_cols) > 0) {
        stop("Missing required columns: ", paste(missing_cols, collapse = ", "))
    }

    # Standardize column names for plyinteractions
    setnames(bedpe_dt, c(chr1_col, start1_col, end1_col, chr2_col, start2_col, end2_col),
        c("seqnames1", "start1", "end1", "seqnames2", "start2", "end2"), skip_absent = TRUE)

    # Handle strand columns
    if (!is.na(strand1_col)) {
        setnames(bedpe_dt, strand1_col, "strand1", skip_absent = TRUE)
    } else {
        bedpe_dt[, strand1 := "*"]
    }

    if (!is.na(strand2_col)) {
        setnames(bedpe_dt, strand2_col, "strand2", skip_absent = TRUE)
    } else {
        bedpe_dt[, strand2 := "*"]
    }

    # Convert '.' to '*' for strand
    bedpe_dt[strand1 == ".", strand1 := "*"]
    bedpe_dt[strand2 == ".", strand2 := "*"]

    # Convert to 1-based if input is 0-based
    if (zero_based) {
        bedpe_dt[, start1 := start1 + 1]
        bedpe_dt[, start2 := start2 + 1]
    }

    # Convert to character
    bedpe_dt[, seqnames1 := as.character(seqnames1)]
    bedpe_dt[, seqnames2 := as.character(seqnames2)]

    # Identify metadata columns
    core_cols <- c("seqnames1", "start1", "end1", "strand1", "seqnames2", "start2",
        "end2", "strand2")
    metadata_cols <- setdiff(colnames(bedpe_dt), core_cols)

    # Create GInteractions using plyinteractions
    if (keep_metadata && length(metadata_cols) > 0) {
        gi <- bedpe_dt %>%
            plyinteractions::as_ginteractions(seqnames1 = seqnames1, start1 = start1,
                end1 = end1, strand1 = strand1, seqnames2 = seqnames2, start2 = start2,
                end2 = end2, strand2 = strand2, keep.extra.columns = TRUE)
    } else {
        gi <- bedpe_dt %>%
            dplyr::select(all_of(core_cols)) %>%
            plyinteractions::as_ginteractions(seqnames1 = seqnames1, start1 = start1,
                end1 = end1, strand1 = strand1, seqnames2 = seqnames2, start2 = start2,
                end2 = end2, strand2 = strand2)
    }

    message("Created GInteractions with ", length(gi), " interactions")

    return(gi)
}

#' Sort BEDPE data.table by genomic coordinates
#'
#' @param bedpe_dt data.table in BEDPE format
#' @return Sorted data.table
sort_bedpe <- function(bedpe_dt) {

    # Detect chromosome column names
    chr1_col <- grep("^([Cc]hr1)|([Cc]hrom1)", colnames(bedpe_dt), value = TRUE)[1]
    chr2_col <- grep("^([Cc]hr2)|([Cc]hrom2)", colnames(bedpe_dt), value = TRUE)[1]

    if (is.na(chr1_col) || is.na(chr2_col)) {
        warning("Cannot find chromosome columns for sorting")
        return(bedpe_dt)
    }

    # Helper function to extract numeric chromosome value
    extract_chr_number <- function(chr_vec) {
        chr_clean <- stringr::str_remove(chr_vec, "^chr")
        chr_num <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(chr_clean))

        chr_num[chr_clean == "X"] <- 23
        chr_num[chr_clean == "Y"] <- 24
        chr_num[chr_clean %in% c("M", "MT")] <- 25

        remaining_na <- is.na(chr_num)
        if (any(remaining_na)) {
            unique_other <- unique(chr_clean[remaining_na])
            chr_num[remaining_na] <- 26 + match(chr_clean[remaining_na], unique_other)
        }

        return(chr_num)
    }

    # Add sorting columns
    bedpe_dt[, chr1_sort := extract_chr_number(get(chr1_col))]
    bedpe_dt[, chr2_sort := extract_chr_number(get(chr2_col))]

    # Sort
    setorderv(bedpe_dt, c("chr1_sort", "start1", "end1", "chr2_sort", "start2", "end2"))

    # Remove sorting columns
    bedpe_dt[, c("chr1_sort", "chr2_sort") := NULL]

    return(bedpe_dt)
}


#' Convert GInteractions to GenomicRangesList
#'
#' @param gi GInteractions object
#' @param keep_metadata Logical, whether to preserve metadata (default: TRUE)
#' @return GenomicRangesList where each element contains a pair of breakpoints
gi_to_grl <- function(gi, keep_metadata = TRUE) {

    require(InteractionSet)
    require(GenomicRanges)

    if (!is(gi, "GInteractions")) {
        stop("Input must be a GInteractions object")
    }

    # Extract anchors (breakpoints)
    anchor1 <- anchors(gi, type = "first")
    anchor2 <- anchors(gi, type = "second")

    # Add metadata to anchors if requested
    if (keep_metadata && length(mcols(gi)) > 0) {
        mcols(anchor1) <- mcols(gi)
        mcols(anchor2) <- mcols(gi)
    }

    # Create list of GRanges pairs
    grl_list <- vector("list", length(gi))

    for (i in seq_along(gi)) {
        grl_list[[i]] <- c(anchor1[i], anchor2[i])
        if (i%%100 == 0) {
            cli::cli_alert_info("Done ... {i}")
        }
    }

    # Convert to GRangesList
    grl <- GRangesList(grl_list)

    # Preserve names if present
    if (!is.null(names(gi))) {
        names(grl) <- names(gi)
    } else {
        names(grl) <- paste0("pair_", seq_along(grl))
    }

    message("Created GRangesList with ", length(grl), " breakpoint pairs")

    return(grl)
}

#' Convert GenomicRangesList to GInteractions
#'
#' @param grl GenomicRangesList where each element contains exactly 2 ranges
#' @param keep_metadata Logical, whether to preserve metadata (default: TRUE)
#' @return GInteractions object
grl_to_gi <- function(grl, keep_metadata = TRUE) {

    require(GenomicRanges)
    require(InteractionSet)

    if (!is(grl, "GRangesList")) {
        stop("Input must be a GenomicRangesList object")
    }

    # Check that all elements have exactly 2 ranges
    pair_lengths <- lengths(grl)
    if (!all(pair_lengths == 2)) {
        warning("Some elements do not contain exactly 2 ranges. These will be skipped.")
        grl <- grl[pair_lengths == 2]
    }

    if (length(grl) == 0) {
        stop("No valid breakpoint pairs found (each element must contain exactly 2 ranges)")
    }

    # Extract first and second breakpoints
    anchor1_list <- vector("list", length(grl))
    anchor2_list <- vector("list", length(grl))

    for (i in seq_along(grl)) {
        gr_pair <- grl[[i]]
        anchor1_list[[i]] <- gr_pair[1]
        anchor2_list[[i]] <- gr_pair[2]
    }

    # Combine into GRanges objects
    anchor1 <- do.call(c, anchor1_list)
    anchor2 <- do.call(c, anchor2_list)

    # Create GInteractions
    gi <- GInteractions(anchor1, anchor2)

    # Add metadata from first anchor if requested (assumes metadata is shared
    # between both anchors)
    if (keep_metadata && length(mcols(anchor1)) > 0) {
        mcols(gi) <- mcols(anchor1)
    }

    # Preserve names
    if (!is.null(names(grl))) {
        names(gi) <- names(grl)
    }

    message("Created GInteractions with ", length(gi), " interactions")

    return(gi)
}

#' Complete conversion: BEDPE -> GInteractions -> GRangesList
#'
#' @param bedpe_file Path to BEDPE file or data.table/data.frame
#' @param zero_based Logical, whether input is 0-based (default: TRUE)
#' @param keep_metadata Logical, whether to preserve metadata (default: TRUE)
#' @return GenomicRangesList
bedpe_to_grl <- function(bedpe_file, zero_based = TRUE, keep_metadata = TRUE) {

    # First convert to GInteractions
    gi <- bedpe_to_gi(bedpe_file, zero_based = zero_based, keep_metadata = keep_metadata)

    # Then convert to GRangesList
    grl <- gi_to_grl(gi, keep_metadata = keep_metadata)

    return(grl)
}

#' Complete conversion: GRangesList -> GInteractions -> BEDPE
#'
#' @param grl GenomicRangesList object
#' @param include_metadata Logical, whether to include metadata (default: TRUE)
#' @param zero_based Logical, whether to use 0-based coordinates (default: TRUE)
#' @param sort_output Logical, whether to sort output (default: TRUE)
#' @return data.table in BEDPE format
grl_to_bedpe <- function(grl, include_metadata = TRUE, zero_based = TRUE, sort_output = TRUE) {

    # First convert to GInteractions
    gi <- grl_to_gi(grl, keep_metadata = include_metadata)

    # Then convert to BEDPE
    bedpe <- gi_to_bedpe(gi, include_metadata = include_metadata, zero_based = zero_based,
        sort_output = sort_output)

    return(bedpe)
}



############################################################################### Non-chrom
############################################################################### based
############################################################################### parallelization

#' @name chunkpar
#' @title Scaffold function to run workflows in parallel by chunk size
#'
#' @description
#' A generalizable scaffold function that provides easy API to `easypar` functionality
#' and executes a function in parallel by splitting input iterations into chunks.
#'
#' This function is useful for parallelizing any iterative workflow where you want to
#' process a large number of items (e.g., rows, samples, files) in parallel batches.
#'
#' @param par_function The function that will be executed in parallel, written
#'  as the function name without the `()` at the end. This function should accept
#'  a vector of indices as its first argument.
#' @param n_iterations Total number of iterations to process (e.g., number of rows,
#'  samples, or files)
#' @param chunk_size Number of iterations to process per parallel job. If NULL,
#'  will automatically determine optimal chunk size based on n_iterations and par_cpus
#' @param ... Additional input parameters that will be passed to `par_function` for
#'  execution (after the index vector)
#' @param run_in_par Run the jobs in parallel and not sequentially, default: TRUE
#' @param par_cpus Number of CPUs to use for parallel execution. Default NULL will
#'  use 80% of available cores
#' @param par_packages Vector of character strings designating special packages needed
#'  for executing the `par_function`
#' @param output_type Type of output to expect: 'data.table', 'granges', 'list', or 'custom'
#'  Default: 'data.table'
#' @param combine_function Custom function to combine outputs if output_type = 'custom'
#'
#' @details
#' The function splits n_iterations into chunks and passes a vector of indices to
#' par_function for each chunk. Your par_function should be written to accept an
#' index vector as its first argument and process those indices.
#'
#' @examples
#' # Example 1: Process mutation records in chunks
#' # Assuming you have a function that processes a subset of rows:
#' # process_mutations <- function(row_indices, mutation_dt, ...) {
#' #   result <- mutation_dt[row_indices, ]
#' #   # ... do processing ...
#' #   return(result)
#' # }
#' #
#' # results <- chunkpar(
#' #   par_function = process_mutations,
#' #   n_iterations = nrow(mutation_dt),
#' #   chunk_size = 1000,
#' #   par_cpus = 8,
#' #   mutation_dt = mutation_dt
#' # )
#'
#' # Example 2: Process VCF files in chunks
#' # vcf_results <- chunkpar(
#' #   par_function = process_vcf_batch,
#' #   n_iterations = length(vcf_files),
#' #   chunk_size = 50,
#' #   par_cpus = 4,
#' #   vcf_files = vcf_files,
#' #   output_type = 'granges'
#' # )
#'
#' @returns Combined output based on output_type: data.table, GRanges, or custom
#' @export
#' @keywords core
chunkpar <- function(par_function, n_iterations, chunk_size = NULL, ..., run_in_par = TRUE,
    par_cpus = NULL, par_packages = NULL, output_type = c("data.table", "granges",
        "list", "custom"), combine_function = NULL) {

    require(easypar)
    require(data.table)
    require(cli)

    # Match output type
    output_type <- match.arg(output_type)

    # Determine number of cores
    available_cores <- parallel::detectCores()
    if (!is.null(par_cpus)) {
        cores_to_use <- min(par_cpus, available_cores)
        par_cores_ratio <- cores_to_use/available_cores
    } else {
        par_cores_ratio <- 0.8
        cores_to_use <- floor(available_cores * par_cores_ratio)
    }

    # Auto-determine chunk size if not provided
    if (is.null(chunk_size)) {
        # Aim for at least 2 chunks per core, but no more than 100 chunks total
        optimal_chunks <- min(cores_to_use * 2, 100)
        chunk_size <- max(1, ceiling(n_iterations/optimal_chunks))
    }

    # Create chunks
    chunk_starts <- seq(1, n_iterations, by = chunk_size)
    chunk_ends <- c(chunk_starts[-1] - 1, n_iterations)
    n_chunks <- length(chunk_starts)

    # Function CLI
    cli::cli_alert_info(text = "Launching {.emph {.pkg {as.character(substitute(par_function))}()}} in parallel")
    cli::cli_text("  Total iterations: {.strong {n_iterations}}")
    cli::cli_text("  Chunk size: {.strong {chunk_size}}")
    cli::cli_text("  Number of chunks: {.strong {n_chunks}}")
    cli::cli_text("  Cores to use: {.strong {cores_to_use}}")
    cat("\n")
    cli::cli_text("****************")
    cli::cli_ul(paste0("Chunk ", 1:n_chunks, ": indices ", chunk_starts, "-", chunk_ends),
        .close = TRUE)
    cli::cli_text("****************")
    cat("\n")

    # Build the parameters for the parallel run Each parameter set includes the
    # index range for that chunk
    par_params <- list()
    for (i in 1:n_chunks) {
        chunk_indices <- chunk_starts[i]:chunk_ends[i]
        par_params[[i]] <- c(list(chunk_indices), list(...))
    }

    # Run easypar by chunks
    cli::cli_alert_info("Executing parallel jobs {crayon::white(clisymbols::symbol$ellipsis)}")
    par_out_list <- easypar::run(FUN = par_function, PARAMS = par_params, parallel = run_in_par,
        cores.ratio = par_cores_ratio, outfile = NULL, progress_bar = FALSE, silent = FALSE,
        packages = par_packages, filter_errors = FALSE)

    # Combine the output based on type
    cat("\n")
    cli::cli_alert_success("Parallel jobs completed. Combining {n_chunks} chunk outputs {crayon::white(clisymbols::symbol$ellipsis)}")

    par_out <- switch(output_type, data.table = {
        combined <- data.table::rbindlist(par_out_list, fill = TRUE)
        cli::cli_alert_success("Combined into data.table with {nrow(combined)} rows")
        combined
    }, granges = {
        require(GenomicRanges)
        combined_dt <- data.table::rbindlist(par_out_list, fill = TRUE)
        par_out_gr <- dt_to_gr(combined_dt)
        par_out_gr <- GenomicRanges::sort(par_out_gr)
        cli::cli_alert_success("Combined into sorted GRanges with {length(par_out_gr)} ranges")
        par_out_gr
    }, list = {
        cli::cli_alert_success("Returning list of {length(par_out_list)} chunk outputs")
        par_out_list
    }, custom = {
        if (is.null(combine_function)) {
            cli::cli_alert_warning("No combine_function provided. Returning raw list.")
            par_out_list
        } else {
            combined <- do.call(combine_function, list(par_out_list))
            cli::cli_alert_success("Combined using custom function")
            combined
        }
    })

    return(par_out)
}



# ============================================================================
# SigProfilerExtractor Decomposition Visualization Workflow
# ============================================================================

parse_decomposition_csv <- function(csv_file, sig_type = NULL) {
    if (is.null(sig_type) || !sig_type %in% c("SBS", "ID")) {
        stop(cli::cli_alert_danger("Signature type must be either: `SBS` or `ID`"))
    }

    # Read the CSV file
    decomp_data <- read.csv(csv_file, header = TRUE, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

    # Extract signature name
    if (sig_type == "SBS") {
        sig_name <- stringr::str_replace(string = decomp_data$De.novo.extracted,
            pattern = "96-", replacement = "SBS96")
    } else if (sig_type == "ID") {
        sig_name <- stringr::str_replace(string = decomp_data$De.novo.extracted,
            pattern = "83-", replacement = "ID83")
    }

    # Extract contributing COSMIC signatures and percentages
    global_sigs_line <- decomp_data$Global.NMF.Signatures

    # Parse individual signatures (format: 'Signature ID1 (19.56%)' | Signature
    # SBS1 (19.56%))
    sig_matches <- stringr::str_match_all(string = global_sigs_line, pattern = "Signature (\\w+\\d+) \\(([0-9.]+)%\\)")[[1]]

    cosmic_signatures <- data.frame(signature = sig_matches[, 2], percentage = as.numeric(sig_matches[,
        3]), stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

    # Extract quality metrics
    metrics <- list(l1_error = as.numeric(decomp_data$L1.Error..), l2_error = as.numeric(decomp_data$L2.Error..),
        kl_divergence = as.numeric(decomp_data$KL.Divergence), cosine_similarity = as.numeric(decomp_data$Cosine.Similarity),
        correlation = as.numeric(decomp_data$Correlation))

    return(list(sig_name = sig_name, cosmic_signatures = cosmic_signatures, metrics = metrics))
}

# ============================================================================
# 2. Parse De Novo Signature Data (ID83 format)
# ============================================================================

parse_denovo_signature <- function(txt_file) {
    sig_data <- read.delim(txt_file, header = TRUE, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

    # Ensure proper column naming
    colnames(sig_data) <- c("MutationType", "proportion")

    # Set factor levels to maintain order
    sig_data$MutationType <- factor(sig_data$MutationType, levels = sig_data$MutationType)

    return(sig_data)
}

# ============================================================================
# 3. Define ID83 Color Scheme
# ============================================================================

get_signature_colors <- function(sig_type = NULL) {
    if (is.null(sig_type) || !sig_type %in% c("SBS", "ID")) {
        stop(cli::cli_alert_danger("Signature type must be either: `SBS` or `ID`"))
    }

    # SBS96 Single-Base Substitution Colors
    if (sig_type == "SBS") {
        colors <- list(`C>A` = "#5ABCEB", `C>G` = "#050708", `C>T` = "#D33C32", `T>A` = "#CBCACB",
            `T>C` = "#ABCD72", `T>G` = "#E7C9C6")
    }

    # ID83 mutation categories and their color-blind adjusted colors
    if (sig_type == "ID") {
        colors <- list(`1:Del:C` = "#FF50FF", `1:Del:T` = "#860086", `1:Ins:C` = "#AACF8E",
            `1:Ins:T` = "#35A02E", `2:Del:R` = "#E5B17E", `3:Del:R` = "#CC9B7A",
            `4:Del:R` = "#996035", `5:Del:R` = "#662F00", `2:Ins:R` = "#CFE0F0",
            `3:Ins:R` = "#94C3DF", `4:Ins:R` = "#4A98C8", `5:Ins:R` = "#1665AA",
            `2:Del:M` = "#E1E1EE", `3:Del:M` = "#B5B5D8", `4:Del:M` = "#8683BC",
            `5:Del:M` = "#624099")
    }

    return(colors)
}


assign_signature_colors <- function(mutation_types, sig_type = NULL) {
    if (is.null(sig_type) || !sig_type %in% c("SBS", "ID")) {
        stop(cli::cli_alert_danger("Signature type must be either: `SBS` or `ID`"))
    }

    # Get the appropriate color map
    color_map <- get_signature_colors(sig_type = sig_type)

    # SBS96 Single Base Substitution Color Assignment
    if (sig_type == "SBS") {
        # Extract substitution type from trinucleotide context Format:
        # 'A[C>A]A' -> 'C>A' or 'ACA>AAA' -> extract middle base change
        categories <- sapply(mutation_types, function(mt) {
            mt_str <- as.character(mt)
            match <- regmatches(mt_str, regexpr("[ACGT]>[ACGT]", mt_str))
            return(match)
        })
    }

    # ID83 Indel Signature Color Assignment
    if (sig_type == "ID") {
        # Extract category from mutation type (e.g., '1:Del:C:0' -> '1:Del:C')
        categories <- sapply(mutation_types, function(mt) {
            parts <- strsplit(as.character(mt), ":")[[1]]
            paste(parts[1:3], collapse = ":")
        })
    }

    colors <- sapply(categories, function(cat) {
        return(color_map[[cat]])
    })

    return(unname(colors))
}

# Helper function to generate full SBS96 color vector (for bar plots)
get_sbs96_color_vector <- function() {
    # Returns a vector of 96 colors in standard trinucleotide order 16 contexts
    # per substitution type
    color_map <- get_signature_colors("SBS")

    col_96 <- c(rep(color_map[["C>A"]], 16), rep(color_map[["C>G"]], 16), rep(color_map[["C>T"]],
        16), rep(color_map[["T>A"]], 16), rep(color_map[["T>C"]], 16), rep(color_map[["T>G"]],
        16))

    return(col_96)
}

# ============================================================================
# 4. Plot ID83 De Novo Signature
# ============================================================================

plot_id83 <- function(sig_data, title = "De Novo Signature", text_annotate = TRUE) {

    # Assign colors based on mutation type
    bar_colors <- assign_signature_colors(mutation_types = sig_data$MutationType,
        sig_type = "ID")

    # Create category labels for grouping
    sig_data$category <- sapply(as.character(sig_data$MutationType), function(mt) {
        parts <- strsplit(mt, ":")[[1]]
        paste(parts[1:3], collapse = ":")
    })

    # Create x-axis label based on homopolymer length / number of repeat units
    # / microhomology length
    sig_data$x_axis_labels <- sapply(as.character(sig_data$MutationType), function(mt) {
        parts <- strsplit(mt, ":")[[1]]
        label <- ifelse(test = parts[4] == "5", yes = "5+", no = parts[4])
        return(label)
    })

    # Create the plot
    p <- ggplot2::ggplot(sig_data, aes(x = MutationType, y = proportion)) + ggplot2::geom_col(fill = bar_colors,
        width = 0.8) + ggplot2::scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0, 0), limits = c(0,
        max(sig_data$proportion) * 1.25), labels = scales::label_percent(accuracy = 0.1)) +
        ggplot2::scale_x_discrete(expand = c(0, 0), labels = sig_data$x_axis_labels) +
        ggplot2::labs(title = title, x = "Homopolymer Length | Number of Repeat Units | Microhomology Length",
            y = "Percentage of InDels") + ggplot2::theme_minimal() + ggplot2::theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5,
        face = "bold", size = 12), axis.text.x = element_text(size = 5), axis.title = element_text(size = 10),
        panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(), panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
        panel.border = element_rect(fill = NA, color = "black", linewidth = 0.5))

    # Add category header bars
    color_map <- get_signature_colors(sig_type = "ID")
    categories <- unique(sig_data$category)

    # Calculate positions for category headers
    cat_positions <- sig_data %>%
        dplyr::mutate(idx = row_number()) %>%
        dplyr::group_by(category) %>%
        dplyr::reframe(xmin = min(idx) - 0.5, xmax = max(idx) + 0.5, .groups = "drop")

    ymax <- max(sig_data$proportion) * 1.25
    ymin_rect <- max(sig_data$proportion) * 1.15

    # Labels for categories
    cat_labels <- c("C", "T", "C", "T", "2", "2", "2", "3", "3", "3", "4", "4", "4",
        "5+", "5+", "5+")
    cat_label_cols <- c("black", "white", "black", "white", rep("black", 6), rep("white",
        6))
    cat_label_x_pos <- ((cat_positions$xmax - cat_positions$xmin)/2) + cat_positions$xmin
    cat_label_y_pos <- ((ymax - ymin_rect)/2) + ymin_rect

    for (i in seq_len(nrow(cat_positions))) {
        cat <- cat_positions$category[i]
        fill_col <- ifelse(cat %in% names(color_map), color_map[[cat]], "#808080")

        p <- p + ggplot2::annotate("rect", xmin = cat_positions$xmin[i], xmax = cat_positions$xmax[i],
            ymin = ymin_rect, ymax = ymax, fill = fill_col)

        # Add the informative annotation labels if space available
        if (text_annotate) {
            p <- p + ggplot2::annotate(geom = "text", x = cat_label_x_pos[i], y = cat_label_y_pos,
                label = cat_labels[i], size = 2.25, color = cat_label_cols[i])
        }
    }

    # Add the informative annotation labels if space available
    if (text_annotate) {
        # Add broad category labels
        p <- p + ggplot2::annotate(geom = "text", x = c(6.5, 18.5, 36.5, 60, 78),
            y = ymin_rect * 0.97, label = c("1bp Deletion", "1bp Insertion", ">1bp Deletion at Repeats",
                ">1bp Insertion at Repeats", "Microhomology"), size = 2.5, color = "black")
    }

    return(p)
}

# updated version of plot_sbs96
plot_sbs96_new <- function(sig_data, title = "De Novo Signature") {

    # =========================================================================
    # Sort mutation types into COSMIC standard order Format: N[X>Y]N where X is
    # ref base (C or T) and Y is alt base Order: C>A, C>G, C>T, T>A, T>C, T>G
    # (16 trinucleotide contexts each)
    # =========================================================================

    # Define the standard COSMIC SBS96 order
    bases <- c("A", "C", "G", "T")
    substitution_types <- c("C>A", "C>G", "C>T", "T>A", "T>C", "T>G")

    # Generate standard order
    standard_order <- c()
    for (sub in substitution_types) {
        ref_base <- substr(sub, 1, 1)
        for (five_prime in bases) {
            for (three_prime in bases) {
                context <- paste0(five_prime, "[", sub, "]", three_prime)
                standard_order <- c(standard_order, context)
            }
        }
    }

    # Reorder data to match standard order
    sig_data <- sig_data %>%
        dplyr::mutate(MutationType = as.character(MutationType)) %>%
        dplyr::arrange(match(MutationType, standard_order))

    # Set factor levels to maintain COSMIC standard order
    sig_data$MutationType <- factor(sig_data$MutationType, levels = standard_order)

    # Extract substitution type for color assignment
    sig_data$substitution <- sapply(as.character(sig_data$MutationType), function(mt) {
        # Extract 'X>Y' from 'N[X>Y]N' format
        match <- regmatches(mt, regexpr("[ACGT]>[ACGT]", mt))
        return(match)
    })

    # Get color map
    color_map <- get_signature_colors(sig_type = "SBS")

    # Assign colors based on substitution type
    bar_colors <- sapply(sig_data$substitution, function(sub) {
        return(color_map[[sub]])
    })

    # Calculate plot dimensions
    ymax <- max(sig_data$proportion, na.rm = TRUE) * 1.15
    ymin_rect <- max(sig_data$proportion, na.rm = TRUE) * 1.05
    p <- ggplot2::ggplot(sig_data, aes(x = MutationType, y = proportion)) + ggplot2::geom_col(fill = bar_colors,
        width = 0.8) + ggplot2::scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0, 0), limits = c(0,
        ymax), labels = scales::label_percent(accuracy = 0.1)) + ggplot2::scale_x_discrete(expand = c(0,
        0)) + ggplot2::labs(title = title, x = "Trinucleotide Context", y = "Single-Base Substitutions (%)") +
        ggplot2::theme_minimal() + ggplot2::theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5,
        face = "bold", size = 12), axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 1,
        vjust = 0.5, size = 3, ), axis.title = element_text(size = 10), panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank(), panel.border = element_rect(fill = NA,
            color = "black", linewidth = 0.5))

    # Add category header bars for the 6 substitution types
    text_colors <- c("black", "white", "black", "black", "black", "black")

    p <- p + ggplot2::annotate(geom = "rect", xmin = c(0.5, 16.5, 32.5, 48.5, 64.5,
        80.5), xmax = c(16.5, 32.5, 48.5, 64.5, 80.5, 96.5), ymin = ymin_rect, ymax = ymax,
        fill = c(color_map[["C>A"]], color_map[["C>G"]], color_map[["C>T"]], color_map[["T>A"]],
            color_map[["T>C"]], color_map[["T>G"]])) + ggplot2::annotate(geom = "text",
        x = c(8, 24, 40, 56, 72, 88), y = (ymin_rect + ymax)/2, label = substitution_types,
        size = 2.75, color = text_colors)

    return(p)
}


# ============================================================================
# 5. Create Metrics Annotation Panel
# ============================================================================

create_metrics_text <- function(metrics, cosmic_sigs) {
    # Format COSMIC signature contributions
    sig_text <- paste(sprintf("%s: %.2f%%", cosmic_sigs$signature, cosmic_sigs$percentage),
        collapse = "\n")

    metrics_text <- paste0("Decomposition to COSMIC:\n", sig_text, "\n\n", "Quality Metrics:\n",
        sprintf("Cosine Similarity: %.3f\n", metrics$cosine_similarity), sprintf("Correlation: %.3f\n",
            metrics$correlation), sprintf("L1 Error: %.1f%%\n", metrics$l1_error),
        sprintf("L2 Error: %.1f%%\n", metrics$l2_error), sprintf("KL Divergence: %.4f",
            metrics$kl_divergence))

    return(metrics_text)
}

# ============================================================================
# 6. Fetch COSMIC Reference Signatures (ID83)
# ============================================================================

# You'll need COSMIC reference signatures - here's a helper to load them
# Download from: https://cancer.sanger.ac.uk/signatures/downloads/

load_cosmic_ref_signatures <- function(cosmic_file = NULL) {
    if (!is.null(cosmic_file) && file.exists(cosmic_file)) {
        cosmic_sigs <- read.delim(cosmic_file, header = TRUE, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
        return(cosmic_sigs)
    } else {
        message("COSMIC signature reference file not found. Please download from COSMIC.")
        return(NULL)
    }
}

# ============================================================================
# 7. Main Visualization Function
# ============================================================================

# TODO: need to update this to run for both current signature types, ID
# (current) and SBS (not yet added)
create_decomposition_figure <- function(sig_type = NULL, denovo_txt, decomposition_csv,
    cosmic_reference = NULL) {

    if (is.null(sig_type) || !sig_type %in% c("SBS", "ID")) {
        stop(cli::cli_alert_danger("Signature type must be either: `SBS` or `ID`"))
    }

    # Parse input files
    decomp_info <- parse_decomposition_csv(decomposition_csv, sig_type)
    denovo_sig <- parse_denovo_signature(denovo_txt)

    cli::cli_alert_success(paste0("Parsed de novo signature: ", decomp_info$sig_name))
    cli::cli_alert_info("Contributing COSMIC signatures:\n")
    decomp_info$cosmic_signatures <- decomp_info$cosmic_signatures %>%
        dplyr::arrange(desc(percentage))
    print(decomp_info$cosmic_signatures)

    # Create de novo signature plot
    if (sig_type == "SBS") {
        denovo_plot <- plot_sbs96_new(denovo_sig, title = paste("De Novo:", decomp_info$sig_name))
    }

    if (sig_type == "ID") {
        denovo_plot <- plot_id83(denovo_sig, title = paste("De Novo:", decomp_info$sig_name))
    }

    # Create metrics annotation
    metrics_text <- create_metrics_text(decomp_info$metrics, decomp_info$cosmic_signatures)

    # Create text grob for metrics
    metrics_grob <- ggplot2::ggplot() + ggplot2::annotate("text", x = 0.5, y = 0.5,
        label = metrics_text, hjust = 0.5, vjust = 0.5, size = 3.5, family = "mono") +
        ggplot2::theme_void() + ggplot2::labs(title = "Decomposition Metrics") +
        ggplot2::theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", size = 11))

    # Create COSMIC signature plots if reference available
    cosmic_plots <- list()

    if (!is.null(cosmic_reference)) {
        cosmic_ref_sigs <- load_cosmic_ref_signatures(cosmic_file = cosmic_reference)

        for (i in seq_len(nrow(decomp_info$cosmic_signatures))) {
            sig_id <- decomp_info$cosmic_signatures$signature[i]
            sig_pct <- decomp_info$cosmic_signatures$percentage[i]

            if (sig_id %in% colnames(cosmic_ref_sigs)) {
                sig_data <- data.frame(MutationType = factor(cosmic_ref_sigs$MutationType,
                  levels = cosmic_ref_sigs$MutationType), proportion = cosmic_ref_sigs[[sig_id]])

                if (sig_type == "SBS") {
                  cosmic_plots[[i]] <- plot_sbs96_new(sig_data, title = sprintf("%s (%.1f%%)",
                    sig_id, sig_pct))
                }

                if (sig_type == "ID") {
                  cosmic_plots[[i]] <- plot_id83(sig_data, title = sprintf("%s (%.1f%%)",
                    sig_id, sig_pct), text_annotate = F)
                }
            }
        }
    } else {
        # Create placeholder plots showing signature names and contributions
        for (i in seq_len(nrow(decomp_info$cosmic_signatures))) {
            sig_id <- decomp_info$cosmic_signatures$signature[i]
            sig_pct <- decomp_info$cosmic_signatures$percentage[i]

            cosmic_plots[[i]] <- ggplot2::ggplot() + ggplot2::annotate("text", x = 0.5,
                y = 0.5, label = sprintf("COSMIC %s\n%.1f%%", sig_id, sig_pct), size = 6,
                fontface = "bold") + ggplot2::theme_void() + ggplot2::theme(panel.border = element_rect(fill = NA,
                color = "black"), plot.margin = margin(10, 10, 10, 10))
        }
    }

    # Combine plots using patchwork Left side: de novo signature + metrics
    left_panel <- denovo_plot/metrics_grob + patchwork::plot_layout(heights = c(0.45,
        0.55))

    # Right side: COSMIC signatures stacked
    if (length(cosmic_plots) > 0) {
        right_panel <- patchwork::wrap_plots(cosmic_plots, ncol = 1, axis_titles = "collect")
    } else {
        right_panel <- ggplot2::ggplot() + ggplot2::theme_void()
    }

    # Final combined figure
    final_plot <- left_panel | right_panel
    final_plot <- final_plot + patchwork::plot_layout(widths = c(1, 1)) + patchwork::plot_annotation(title = "De Novo Signature Decomposition to COSMIC",
        theme = theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 14, face = "bold")))

    return(final_plot)
}
```

Clinical metadata table of high quality patient set

::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code .code-overflow-wrap  code-fold="true"}
hg_cov_tn_df <- hq_dataset_metadata %>%
    distinct(patient, coverage_normal, coverage_tumor) %>%
    reshape2::melt(id.vars = "patient")

hg_cov_tn_plot <- ggplot(hg_cov_tn_df) + geom_jitter(aes(x = variable, y = value,
    fill = variable), shape = 21, na.rm = T) + geom_boxplot(aes(x = variable, y = value,
    fill = variable), position = "dodge", outlier.shape = NA, alpha = 0.6, na.rm = T) +
    scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0.03, 0.03), breaks = c(0, 30, 60, 90, round(max(meta_and_cov_df$value,
        na.rm = T), digits = 0)), limits = c(0, round(max(meta_and_cov_df$value,
        na.rm = T), digits = 0) + 2)) + scale_x_discrete(labels = c("N", "T")) +
    scale_fill_manual(name = "Sample", labels = c("Normal", "Tumor"), values = c("black",
        "#8000FF")) + labs(y = "Coverage") + theme(axis.title.x = element_blank(),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "gray90"), panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_line(colour = "gray75"))

hq_clinical_meta_table <- data.table(Metadata = c("", "Age", "Median (range)", "Sex",
    "Male", "Female", "Admixture Cluster", "EUR Dominant", "AFR Dominant", "AMR Dominant",
    "EAS Dominant", "SAS Dominant", "Highly Admixed", "Self-Identification", "White",
    "Black or\n\tAfrican American", "Hispanic", "Asian", "IGH Translocation", "t(11;14)",
    "t(4;14)", "t(14;16)", "t(14;20)", "t(6;14)", "t(8;14)", "Not Detected", "Hyperdiploidy",
    "yes", "no"), MGUS = c("", "", NA, "", NA, NA, "", NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, "",
    NA, NA, NA, NA, "", NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, "", NA, NA), SMM = c("", "",
    NA, "", NA, NA, "", NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, "", NA, NA, NA, NA, "", NA, NA, NA,
    NA, NA, NA, NA, "", NA, NA), MM = c("", "", NA, "", NA, NA, "", NA, NA, NA, NA,
    NA, NA, "", NA, NA, NA, NA, "", NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, "", NA, NA), RRMM = c("",
    "", NA, "", NA, NA, "", NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, "", NA, NA, NA, NA, "", NA, NA,
    NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, "", NA, NA))
for (i in 1:length(levels(hq_dataset_metadata$disease_stage)[1:4])) {

    # Grab all unique patient records in the metadata per disease stage
    patients_per_stage <- hq_dataset_metadata %>%
        droplevels() %>%
        dplyr::filter(disease_stage == levels(hq_dataset_metadata$disease_stage)[i]) %>%
        dplyr::select(patient, age, sex, super5_cluster_id, self_identified_race,
            igh_translocation, genomic_hrd_status) %>%
        unique()

    # If patient has multiple samples per disease stage then take only the
    # first record (earliest)
    patients_per_stage <- patients_per_stage[!duplicated(patients_per_stage$patient),
        ]

    # Now calculate some metrics to display n per group
    n_record <- paste0("n = ", length(patients_per_stage$patient))

    # Age
    median_age <- round(median(as.numeric(patients_per_stage$age), na.rm = T), digits = 0)
    range_age <- round(range(as.numeric(patients_per_stage$age), na.rm = T), digits = 0)
    age_record <- paste0(median_age, " (", range_age[1], "-", range_age[2], ")")

    # Sex
    male_sex <- sum(patients_per_stage$sex == "Male", na.rm = T)
    male_pct_sex <- round(mean(patients_per_stage$sex == "Male", na.rm = T) * 100,
        digits = 1)
    male_sex_record <- paste0(male_pct_sex, " (", male_sex, ")")

    female_sex <- sum(patients_per_stage$sex == "Female", na.rm = T)
    female_pct_sex <- round(mean(patients_per_stage$sex == "Female", na.rm = T) *
        100, digits = 1)
    female_sex_record <- paste0(female_pct_sex, " (", female_sex, ")")

    # Admixture cluster assignment
    eur_admix_cluster <- sum(patients_per_stage$super5_cluster_id == "EUR Dominant",
        na.rm = T)
    eur_pct_admix_cluster <- round(mean(patients_per_stage$super5_cluster_id == "EUR Dominant",
        na.rm = T) * 100, digits = 1)
    eur_admix_cluster_record <- paste0(eur_pct_admix_cluster, " (", eur_admix_cluster,
        ")")

    afr_admix_cluster <- sum(patients_per_stage$super5_cluster_id == "AFR Dominant",
        na.rm = T)
    afr_pct_admix_cluster <- round(mean(patients_per_stage$super5_cluster_id == "AFR Dominant",
        na.rm = T) * 100, digits = 1)
    afr_admix_cluster_record <- paste0(afr_pct_admix_cluster, " (", afr_admix_cluster,
        ")")

    amr_admix_cluster <- sum(patients_per_stage$super5_cluster_id == "AMR Dominant",
        na.rm = T)
    amr_pct_admix_cluster <- round(mean(patients_per_stage$super5_cluster_id == "AMR Dominant",
        na.rm = T) * 100, digits = 1)
    amr_admix_cluster_record <- paste0(amr_pct_admix_cluster, " (", amr_admix_cluster,
        ")")

    eas_admix_cluster <- sum(patients_per_stage$super5_cluster_id == "EAS Dominant",
        na.rm = T)
    eas_pct_admix_cluster <- round(mean(patients_per_stage$super5_cluster_id == "EAS Dominant",
        na.rm = T) * 100, digits = 1)
    eas_admix_cluster_record <- paste0(eas_pct_admix_cluster, " (", eas_admix_cluster,
        ")")

    sas_admix_cluster <- sum(patients_per_stage$super5_cluster_id == "SAS Dominant",
        na.rm = T)
    sas_pct_admix_cluster <- round(mean(patients_per_stage$super5_cluster_id == "SAS Dominant",
        na.rm = T) * 100, digits = 1)
    sas_admix_cluster_record <- paste0(sas_pct_admix_cluster, " (", sas_admix_cluster,
        ")")

    ha_admix_cluster <- sum(patients_per_stage$super5_cluster_id == "Highly Admixed",
        na.rm = T)
    ha_pct_admix_cluster <- round(mean(patients_per_stage$super5_cluster_id == "Highly Admixed",
        na.rm = T) * 100, digits = 1)
    ha_admix_cluster_record <- paste0(ha_pct_admix_cluster, " (", ha_admix_cluster,
        ")")

    # Self-Identification
    w_self_id <- sum(patients_per_stage$self_identified_race == "White", na.rm = T)
    w_pct_self_id <- round(mean(patients_per_stage$self_identified_race == "White",
        na.rm = T) * 100, digits = 1)
    w_self_id_record <- paste0(w_pct_self_id, " (", w_self_id, ")")

    aa_self_id <- sum(patients_per_stage$self_identified_race == "Black or African American",
        na.rm = T)
    aa_pct_self_id <- round(mean(patients_per_stage$self_identified_race == "Black or African American",
        na.rm = T) * 100, digits = 1)
    aa_self_id_record <- paste0(aa_pct_self_id, " (", aa_self_id, ")")

    h_self_id <- sum(patients_per_stage$self_identified_race == "Hispanic", na.rm = T)
    h_pct_self_id <- round(mean(patients_per_stage$self_identified_race == "Hispanic",
        na.rm = T) * 100, digits = 1)
    h_self_id_record <- paste0(h_pct_self_id, " (", h_self_id, ")")

    a_self_id <- sum(patients_per_stage$self_identified_race == "Asian", na.rm = T)
    a_pct_self_id <- round(mean(patients_per_stage$self_identified_race == "Asian",
        na.rm = T) * 100, digits = 1)
    a_self_id_record <- paste0(a_pct_self_id, " (", a_self_id, ")")

    # IgH Translocation
    t11_14 <- sum(patients_per_stage$igh_translocation == "t(11;14)", na.rm = T)
    t11_14_pct <- round(mean(patients_per_stage$igh_translocation == "t(11;14)",
        na.rm = T) * 100, digits = 1)
    t11_14_record <- paste0(t11_14_pct, " (", t11_14, ")")

    t4_14 <- sum(patients_per_stage$igh_translocation == "t(4;14)", na.rm = T)
    t4_14_pct <- round(mean(patients_per_stage$igh_translocation == "t(4;14)", na.rm = T) *
        100, digits = 1)
    t4_14_record <- paste0(t4_14_pct, " (", t4_14, ")")

    t14_16 <- sum(patients_per_stage$igh_translocation == "t(14;16)", na.rm = T)
    t14_16_pct <- round(mean(patients_per_stage$igh_translocation == "t(14;16)",
        na.rm = T) * 100, digits = 1)
    t14_16_record <- paste0(t14_16_pct, " (", t14_16, ")")

    t14_20 <- sum(patients_per_stage$igh_translocation == "t(14;20)", na.rm = T)
    t14_20_pct <- round(mean(patients_per_stage$igh_translocation == "t(14;20)",
        na.rm = T) * 100, digits = 1)
    t14_20_record <- paste0(t14_20_pct, " (", t14_20, ")")

    t6_14 <- sum(patients_per_stage$igh_translocation == "t(6;14)", na.rm = T)
    t6_14_pct <- round(mean(patients_per_stage$igh_translocation == "t(6;14)", na.rm = T) *
        100, digits = 1)
    t6_14_record <- paste0(t6_14_pct, " (", t6_14, ")")

    t8_14 <- sum(patients_per_stage$igh_translocation == "t(8;14)", na.rm = T)
    t8_14_pct <- round(mean(patients_per_stage$igh_translocation == "t(8;14)", na.rm = T) *
        100, digits = 1)
    t8_14_record <- paste0(t8_14_pct, " (", t8_14, ")")

    ndet <- sum(patients_per_stage$igh_translocation == "Not Detected", na.rm = T)
    ndet_pct <- round(mean(patients_per_stage$igh_translocation == "Not Detected",
        na.rm = T) * 100, digits = 1)
    ndet_record <- paste0(ndet_pct, " (", ndet, ")")

    # Hyperdiploidy
    yhrd <- sum(patients_per_stage$genomic_hrd_status == "yes", na.rm = T)
    yhrd_pct <- round(mean(patients_per_stage$genomic_hrd_status == "yes", na.rm = T) *
        100, digits = 1)
    yhrd_record <- paste0(yhrd_pct, " (", yhrd, ")")

    nhrd <- sum(patients_per_stage$genomic_hrd_status == "no", na.rm = T)
    nhrd_pct <- round(mean(patients_per_stage$genomic_hrd_status == "no", na.rm = T) *
        100, digits = 1)
    nhrd_record <- paste0(nhrd_pct, " (", nhrd, ")")

    # Compile clinical metadata table n per group
    hq_clinical_meta_table[1, i + 1] <- n_record
    # Age
    hq_clinical_meta_table[3, i + 1] <- age_record
    # Sex
    hq_clinical_meta_table[5, i + 1] <- male_sex_record
    hq_clinical_meta_table[6, i + 1] <- female_sex_record
    # Admixture Cluster
    hq_clinical_meta_table[8, i + 1] <- eur_admix_cluster_record
    hq_clinical_meta_table[9, i + 1] <- afr_admix_cluster_record
    hq_clinical_meta_table[10, i + 1] <- amr_admix_cluster_record
    hq_clinical_meta_table[11, i + 1] <- eas_admix_cluster_record
    hq_clinical_meta_table[12, i + 1] <- sas_admix_cluster_record
    hq_clinical_meta_table[13, i + 1] <- ha_admix_cluster_record
    # Self-Identification
    hq_clinical_meta_table[15, i + 1] <- w_self_id_record
    hq_clinical_meta_table[16, i + 1] <- aa_self_id_record
    hq_clinical_meta_table[17, i + 1] <- h_self_id_record
    hq_clinical_meta_table[18, i + 1] <- a_self_id_record
    # IgH Translocation
    hq_clinical_meta_table[20, i + 1] <- t11_14_record
    hq_clinical_meta_table[21, i + 1] <- t4_14_record
    hq_clinical_meta_table[22, i + 1] <- t14_16_record
    hq_clinical_meta_table[23, i + 1] <- t14_20_record
    hq_clinical_meta_table[24, i + 1] <- t6_14_record
    hq_clinical_meta_table[25, i + 1] <- t8_14_record
    hq_clinical_meta_table[26, i + 1] <- ndet_record
    # Hyperdiploidy
    hq_clinical_meta_table[28, i + 1] <- yhrd_record
    hq_clinical_meta_table[29, i + 1] <- nhrd_record
}

# Now create and customize the table aesthetics
hq_clinical_metadata_table <- flextable::flextable(data = hq_clinical_meta_table) %>%
    flextable::prepend_chunks(i = ~!Metadata %in% c("Age", "Sex", "Admixture Cluster",
        "Self-Identification", "IgH Translocation", "Hyperdiploidy"), j = 1, as_chunk("\t")) %>%
    flextable::width(j = 1, width = 1.47) %>%
    flextable::width(j = 2:5, width = 1.1) %>%
    flextable::bold(part = "header") %>%
    flextable::fontsize(size = 13, part = "header") %>%
    flextable::fontsize(size = 11, part = "body") %>%
    flextable::hline_top(part = "header", border = fp_border_default(width = 2, color = "#8000FF")) %>%
    flextable::hline_bottom(part = "header", border = fp_border_default(width = 0,
        color = "#8000FF")) %>%
    flextable::hline(i = 1, border = fp_border_default(width = 2, color = "#8000FF")) %>%
    flextable::hline_bottom(part = "body", border = fp_border_default(width = 2,
```

Summary table that details key genomic and demographic metadata variables for all patients stratified by disease stage. Admixture cluster refers to the assignment of each patient to a continental ancestry grouping inferred from genomic ancestry. Age is describe using the median with the range in parenthesis. The variables sex, admixture cluster, self-identification, IgH translocation, and hyperdiploidy are described using the percentage of patients per disease stage with raw count in parenthesis. MGUS, monoclonal gammopathy of undetermined significance; SMM, smoldering multiple myeloma; MM, multiple myeloma; RRMM, relapsed/refractory multiple myeloma; EUR, European ancestry; AFR, African ancestry; AMR, American ancestry; EAS, East Asian ancestry; SAS, South Asian ancestry.
:::
:::


## Identification of SNV and InDel enrichment, Part 1

Let's analyze the mutations

::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code .code-overflow-wrap  code-fold="true"}
# Read in genes
genes <- get_genes_shortcut(gtf_file_path = paste0(base_data_path, "Homo_sapiens.GRCh38.108.gtf.gz"))

# Read in dataset-wide MAF file including all variants (no filtering based on
# consequence, which happens with maftools::read.maf)
muts <- read_maf_file(maf_file_path = paste0(base_data_path, "mgp1000.hq.union.consensus.somatic.snv.indel.maf.gz"))
## Warning in gUtils::dt2gr(input_dt, seqlengths = gUtils::hg_seqlengths()[1:24]):
## Warning: Coercing to GRanges via non-standard columns
muts$VAF <- muts$t_alt_count/muts$t_depth

# Gather high-coverage, high-quality samples
muts_in_hq_patients <- muts %Q% (Tumor_Sample_Barcode %in% unique(hq_dataset_metadata$sample))

# Collapse all mutations per patient, remove any duplicated mutations (i.e.
# shared across timepoints) Column 13 is Matched_Norm_Sample_Barcode
muts_hq <- muts_in_hq_patients[!duplicated(gUtils::gr2dt(muts_in_hq_patients[, 13]))]

# Separate SNVs and InDels
snv_muts_hq <- muts_hq %Q% (VARIANT_CLASS == "SNV")
indel_muts_hq <- muts_hq %Q% (VARIANT_CLASS %in% c("deletion", "insertion"))

# How many SNVs and InDels before removing Silent mutations
print("Total SNVs and InDels before filtering out silent mutations ...")
## [1] "Total SNVs and InDels before filtering out silent mutations ..."
length(snv_muts_hq)
## [1] 1957997
length(indel_muts_hq)
## [1] 480318

# Convert mutations to DT
snv_muts_hq_dt <- gUtils::gr2dt(snv_muts_hq)
indel_muts_hq_dt <- gUtils::gr2dt(indel_muts_hq)

# print('SNVs per sample ... ') snv_muts_hq_dt %>%
# dplyr::group_by(Tumor_Sample_Barcode) %>% dplyr::count(name = 'total_snvs')
# %>% dplyr::ungroup() %>% dplyr::reframe('median_snvs_per_sample' =
# median(total_snvs), 'range_snvs' = range(total_snvs)) print('Median InDels
# per sample ... ') indel_muts_hq_dt %>% dplyr::group_by(Tumor_Sample_Barcode)
# %>% dplyr::count(name = 'total_indels') %>% dplyr::ungroup() %>%
# dplyr::reframe('median_indels_per_sample' = median(total_indels),
# 'range_indels' = range(total_indels))

# simplify mut DT for plotting downstream
snv_muts_hq_dt_for_plot <- snv_muts_hq_dt[, .(seqnames, start, end, Hugo_Symbol,
    Variant_Classification, Variant_Type, Consequence, Reference_Allele, Tumor_Seq_Allele2,
    VAF, Tumor_Sample_Barcode, Matched_Norm_Sample_Barcode)]
indel_muts_hq_dt_for_plot <- indel_muts_hq_dt[, .(seqnames, start, end, Hugo_Symbol,
    Variant_Classification, Variant_Type, Consequence, Reference_Allele, Tumor_Seq_Allele2,
    VAF, Tumor_Sample_Barcode, Matched_Norm_Sample_Barcode)]

# Add metadata to mut DT
snv_muts_hq_dt_for_plot <- snv_muts_hq_dt_for_plot %>%
    dplyr::rename(sample = Tumor_Sample_Barcode, patient = Matched_Norm_Sample_Barcode) %>%
    dplyr::left_join(y = hq_dataset_metadata, by = c("sample", "patient"))

indel_muts_hq_dt_for_plot <- indel_muts_hq_dt_for_plot %>%
    dplyr::rename(sample = Tumor_Sample_Barcode, patient = Matched_Norm_Sample_Barcode) %>%
    dplyr::left_join(y = hq_dataset_metadata, by = c("sample", "patient"))

muts_hq_dt_for_plot <- gUtils::rrbind(snv_muts_hq_dt_for_plot, indel_muts_hq_dt_for_plot)
```
:::


## The landscape of the non-coding genome in myeloma is characterized by a discrete set of mutationally enriched regions, Part 1

We are searching the entire genome (ocean) for enrichment (trophy fish) beyond coding regions.
Here, fishy fishy ...

::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code .code-overflow-wrap  code-fold="true"}
# Read in WGS calling BED
eligible_bed <- read_bed_file(bed_file_path = paste0(base_data_path, "wgs_calling_regions.hg38.bed"))
## Warning in gUtils::dt2gr(input_dt, seqlengths = gUtils::hg_seqlengths()[1:24]):
## Warning: Coercing to GRanges via non-standard columns

# total eligible size to start
message("Eligible genome size start: ", sum(GenomicRanges::width(eligible_bed)))
## Eligible genome size start: 2923745819

# First, to focus the analysis and remove potential confounders, specifically
# those due to low-complexity repeat regions, imbalance of calls on sex
# chromosomes, and immunoglobulin regions (functional and non-functional), the
# eligible region will be subset to remove these regions. This will properly
# reduce both the background regions to test against (eligible) and the
# hypothesis regions (tiles) before the run to instantiate the Fish obj

# WindowMasker/SDust low-complexity, repeat region filter BED
wm_sdust_mask_bed <- read_bed_file(bed_file_path = paste0(base_data_path, "windowMaskerSDust.hg38.bed.gz"),
    additional_col_names = "wm_sdust_id")
## Warning in gUtils::dt2gr(input_dt, seqlengths = gUtils::hg_seqlengths()[1:24]):
## Warning: Coercing to GRanges via non-standard columns
# WM/SDust mask size
message("WM/SDust mask size start: ", sum(GenomicRanges::width(wm_sdust_mask_bed)))
## WM/SDust mask size start: 1074813116


# IG regions and genes filter BED
# (https://github.com/ferrannadeu/IgCaller/blob/master/IgCaller_reference_files/hg38/dicts/AllRegionsAndGenes_hg38.bed).
# This also includes non-functional IG genes
# (https://www.genenames.org/data/genegroup/#!/group/350)
ig_region_mask_bed <- read_bed_file(bed_file_path = paste0(base_data_path, "igFunctionalAndNonFunctionalGenesAndRegions.hg38.bed"),
    additional_col_names = "ig_region_id")
## Warning in gUtils::dt2gr(input_dt, seqlengths = gUtils::hg_seqlengths()[1:24]):
## Warning: Coercing to GRanges via non-standard columns
# Add 250 padding to both ends of IG regions
ig_region_mask_bed <- ig_region_mask_bed + 250
# IG regions mask size
message("IG regions mask size start: ", sum(GenomicRanges::width(ig_region_mask_bed)))
## IG regions mask size start: 3994620

# Reduce the background eligible BED by the WM/SDust mask, sex chromosomes, IG
# regions, and exons
eligible_bed <- eligible_bed %_% wm_sdust_mask_bed
eligible_bed <- eligible_bed %_% ig_region_mask_bed
eligible_bed <- eligible_bed %Q% (!seqnames %in% c("chrX", "chrY"))
# final eligible size
message("Final eligible genome size end :", sum(GenomicRanges::width(eligible_bed)))
## Final eligible genome size end :1760671127


# # For WGS, the eligible region is a set of 10kbp tiled windows where each
# successive window is shifted # 500bp of the previous (i.e. each tile 10kbp
# window overlaps the next by 9.5kbp) tiles_set_init <- gUtils::gr.tile(gr =
# seqinfo(eligible_bed), width = 10000) # Since we want to shift the 10kbp
# tiles by 500 each set, we need [(10000 / 500) - 1] number of steps (i.e.
# window / shift - 1) # As the initiating set of tiles will be the same as the
# 20th set tile_set <- GenomicRanges::GRangesList(tiles_set_init) for(i in
# 1:19) { # First, add 500bp to each side of the tile (shifting it) and trim
# the ends to ensure they don't # exceed the seqlength tiles_step <-
# suppressWarnings(tile_set[[i]] %+% 500) tiles_step <-
# #GenomicRanges::trim(tiles_step) tile_set[[i+1]] <- tiles_step } #
# Concatenate the tile steps set into single set of tiles, and fix seqinfo
# final_tiles <-
# GenomicRanges::sort.GenomicRanges(gUtils::grl.unlist(tile_set)) final_tiles
# <- gr_refactor_seqs(input_gr = final_tiles) # Remove any tiles less than
# 10kbp final_tiles <- final_tiles[!final_tiles@ranges@width < 10000,] #
# Annotate each tile with the nearest gene # TODO: gather annotations for
# super-enhancers, B-cell TF binding sites, etc. as these can be added to #
# tile metadata in same fashion of genes final_tiles$nearest_gene <-
# genes$gene_name[nearest(x = final_tiles, subject = gr.stripstrand(genes))]

final_tiles <- readRDS(file = paste0(base_data_path, "genome_tiles.rds"))
# Collect bait (i.e. covariates) for the best model to catch the trophy fish
# (drivers)

# From Gajic et al Nat. Comm. 2022 Covariate set (“covariome”) to
# comprehensively capture the contribution of background genomic features to
# the neutral mutation density.  Replitome - replication timing across 96 cell
# lines, from Roadmap Epigenomics Project Encodome - 15 ChromHMM states across
# 127 cell lines and tissues, from ENCODE Seqome - sequence context (mono, di
# and tri), These are binned numeric values that were calculated using hg19
# data, they will be lifted over for this analysis

##################### Replication timing

# replication_timing_hg19 <- readRDS(paste0(base_data_path,
# 'reptome_fish.rds')) # Original data is hg19 so let's liftOver liftchain <-
# rtracklayer::import.chain(paste0(base_data_path, 'hg19ToHg38.over.chain'))
# replication_timing_hg38 <- rtracklayer::liftOver(x =
# gUtils::gr.chr(replication_timing_hg19$data), chain = liftchain) # Unlist
# output to get GR replication_timing <-
# gUtils::grl.unlist(replication_timing_hg38) # Extract the covariate data GR
# and convert to good seqinfo replication_timing <- gr_refactor_seqs(input_gr =
# replication_timing) # Save copy of hg38 version of full reptome
# #saveRDS(object = replication_timing, file = 'reptome_fish.hg38.rds') Subset
# to top 10 PCs
replication_timing <- readRDS(file = paste0(base_data_path, "reptome_fish.hg38.rds"))
replication_timing <- replication_timing[, 4:13]
replication_timing_covar <- Cov(data = replication_timing, field = colnames(GenomicRanges::mcols(x = replication_timing)))

################### Sequence context

# seq_context_hg19 <- readRDS(paste0(base_data_path, 'seqome_fish.rds')) #
# Original data is hg19 so let's liftOver seq_context_hg38 <-
# rtracklayer::liftOver(x = gUtils::gr.chr(seq_context_hg19$data), chain =
# liftchain) # Unlist output to get GR seq_context <-
# gUtils::grl.unlist(seq_context_hg38) # Extract the covariate data GR and
# convert to good seqinfo seq_context <- gr_refactor_seqs(input_gr =
# seq_context) # Save copy of hg38 version of full seqome #saveRDS(object =
# seq_context, file = 'seqome_fish.hg38.rds') Subset to top 10 PCs
seq_context <- readRDS(file = paste0(base_data_path, "seqome_fish.hg38.rds"))
seq_context <- seq_context[, 4:13]
seq_context_covar <- Cov(data = seq_context, field = colnames(GenomicRanges::mcols(x = seq_context)))

################### ChromHMM states

# chrom_states_hg19 <- readRDS(paste0(base_data_path,
# 'epigenetic_covariate_fish_top200.rds')) # Original data is hg19 so let's
# liftOver chrom_states_hg38 <- rtracklayer::liftOver(x =
# gUtils::gr.chr(chrom_states_hg19$data), chain = liftchain) # Unlist output to
# get GR chrom_states <- gUtils::grl.unlist(chrom_states_hg38) # Extract the
# covariate data GR and convert to good seqinfo chrom_states <-
# gr_refactor_seqs(input_gr = chrom_states) # Save copy of hg38 version of full
# encodome #saveRDS(object = chrom_states, file = 'encodome_fish.hg38.rds')
# Subset to top 10 PCs
chrom_states <- readRDS(file = paste0(base_data_path, "encodome_fish.hg38.rds"))
chrom_states <- chrom_states[, 4:13]
chrom_states_covar <- Cov(data = chrom_states, field = colnames(GenomicRanges::mcols(x = chrom_states)))

# With the bait (covariates) loaded, now we can catch the trophy fish!
covars <- c(replication_timing_covar, chrom_states_covar, seq_context_covar)

# Creating an empty FishHook object covars_bait <- Fish(hypotheses =
# final_tiles, mc.cores = 1) covars_bait$merge(covars)

# export to HPC for run saveRDS(object = covars_bait, file = 'covars_bait.rds')
# saveRDS(object = final_tiles, file = 'genome_tiles.rds') saveRDS(object =
# eligible_bed, file = 'eligible_region.rds')

# Split events for various models Pan-Myeloma saveRDS(object = snv_fish, file =
# 'fishHookModels/panMyeloma/mgp1000_snv_mutations.rds') saveRDS(object =
# indel_fish, file = 'fishHookModels/panMyeloma/mgp1000_indel_mutations.rds')

# Separate SNVs and InDels to be used in fishHook non-coding analysis
snv_fish <- snv_muts_hq %Q% (Variant_Classification != "Silent")
indel_fish <- indel_muts_hq %Q% (Variant_Classification != "Silent")

# Disease Stage specific
mgus_snv_fish <- snv_fish %Q% (Tumor_Sample_Barcode %in% unique(hq_dataset_metadata %>%
    dplyr::filter(disease_stage == "MGUS") %>%
    dplyr::select(sample) %>%

mgus_indel_fish <- indel_fish %Q% (Tumor_Sample_Barcode %in% unique(hq_dataset_metadata %>%

smm_snv_fish <- snv_fish %Q% (Tumor_Sample_Barcode %in% unique(hq_dataset_metadata %>%

smm_indel_fish <- indel_fish %Q% (Tumor_Sample_Barcode %in% unique(hq_dataset_metadata %>%
```
