Let's look at SBS signatures in MERs

::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code .code-overflow-wrap  code-fold="true"}
######## WITH SBSBLOOD ##########

# For proper SBS signatures to be called, we must have sufficient number of
# SNVs Typical minimum cutoff is 1000 SNVs (2000 is default for
# SigProfilerTopography) First, collect all SNVs across all MERs
mer_sig_muts <- muts_per_mer_for_plot %>%
    dplyr::filter(VARIANT_CLASS == "SNV") %>%
    dplyr::select(mer_id, seqnames, start, Reference_Allele, Tumor_Seq_Allele2)

# Unfortunately, there aren't enough mutations per MER to assess relative
# signatures individually TODO: try spliting mers by SE overlap group
# muts_per_mer_for_plot %>% dplyr::group_by(mer_id) %>% dplyr::count() %>%
# dplyr::arrange(desc(n))

# We'll overcome this by looking at all SNVs within the MERs First, given that
# there are now duplicated mutations per MER (from different samples/patients
# etc.), remove them here
mer_sig_muts <- mer_sig_muts[!duplicated(mer_sig_muts), ]
mer_sig_muts$mer_id <- "MERs"

# Now prepare the data for mmsig Correct the column names for input to mmsig
colnames(mer_sig_muts) <- c("sample", "chr", "pos", "ref", "alt")

# Prep the input for mmsig using deconstructSig
mer_sig_96 <- deconstructSigs::mut.to.sigs.input(mut.ref = mer_sig_muts, sample.id = "sample",
    chr = "chr", pos = "pos", ref = "ref", alt = "alt", bsg = BSgenome.Hsapiens.UCSC.hg38)

# Generate a 96 class SBS profile
mer_sig_96_p <- plot_sbs96(mer_sig_96) + labs(title = "MERs")

# Translate data for use in mmsig
mer_sig_96$Total <- rowSums(mer_sig_96)
mer_sig_96_transpose <- as.data.frame.matrix(t(mer_sig_96))

# Prep the signature reference for the fitted approach
data(signature_ref_cosmic_v3_2_hg38)

# SBS1 & SBS5 for clock-like (priority to use SBS5 for all rather than split
# into SBS5 / SBS40) SBS2 & SBS13 for APOBEC SBS8 still unknown SBS9 for Pol
# Eta somatic hyper mutation SBS18 for Damage by reactive oxygen species (ROS)
# SBS84 & SBS85 for canonical AID (excluded in analysis) SBS-MM1 for melphalan
# exposure (included from previous analysis as known signature) SBS45, SBS46,
# and SBS49 for sequencing artifacts (used to identify possilbe exclusion of
# samples, if not excluded via de novo extraction) Add SBSblood as more
# appropriate description of SBS5 Fit the signatures Complete set of all MER
# mutations
mer_sig_96_out <- mm_fit_signatures(muts.input = mer_sig_96_transpose, sig.input = sig_ref_cosmic_v3_2_hg38,
    input.format = "classes", sample.sigt.profs = NULL, strandbias = FALSE, bootstrap = FALSE,
    genome = "hg38", dbg = FALSE, force_include = NULL)

mer_sigs_t <- reshape2::melt(data = mer_sig_96_out$estimate %>%
    tibble::rownames_to_column(var = "sample") %>%
    dplyr::select(-mutations) %>%
    as.data.table(), id.vars = "sample", variable.name = "signature", value.name = "rel_contrib")

mer_sigs_t$signature <- factor(mer_sigs_t$signature, levels = c("SBS1", "SBSblood",
    "SBS8", "SBS9", "SBS84", "SBS18", "SBS99", "SBS2", "SBS13"))
```

Let's look at the signatures in eligible region of non-coding analysis excluding the MERs

::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code .code-overflow-wrap  code-fold="true"}
# Collect all mutations that fall within the eligible regions of the genome
# (same as input for fishHook model)
fish_muts <- grbind(snv_fish, indel_fish)
muts_eligible <- gUtils::gr.findoverlaps(query = fish_muts, subject = eligible_bed,
    qcol = c("Matched_Norm_Sample_Barcode", "Tumor_Sample_Barcode", "VARIANT_CLASS",
        "Reference_Allele", "Tumor_Seq_Allele2", "VAF", "Variant_Classification",
        "SYMBOL"))
muts_eligible_dt <- gr2dt(muts_eligible)

# Let's look at SBS signatures in non-MER First, collect all SNVs across
# eligible region
eligible_sig_muts <- muts_eligible_dt[VARIANT_CLASS == "SNV", .(Tumor_Sample_Barcode,
    seqnames, start, Reference_Allele, Tumor_Seq_Allele2)]

# Now prepare the data for mmsig and compatability with MER SBS mutations
# Correct the column names for input to mmsig
colnames(eligible_sig_muts) <- c("sample", "chr", "pos", "ref", "alt")

# Subtract out the mutations within the MERs from the eligible region mutations
non_mer_sig_muts <- dplyr::setdiff(x = eligible_sig_muts[, -1], y = mer_sig_muts[,
    -1])

# TODO: try to match samples to mutations in nonMERs, could gain some better
# insight depending on signal
non_mer_sig_muts$sample <- "non-MERs"

# Prep the input for mmsig using deconstructSig
non_mer_sig_96 <- deconstructSigs::mut.to.sigs.input(mut.ref = non_mer_sig_muts,
    sample.id = "sample", chr = "chr", pos = "pos", ref = "ref", alt = "alt", bsg = BSgenome.Hsapiens.UCSC.hg38)

# Generate a 96 class SBS profile
non_mer_sig_96_p <- plot_sbs96(non_mer_sig_96) + labs(title = "Total Non-MERs")

# Translate data for use in mmsig
non_mer_sig_96$Total <- rowSums(non_mer_sig_96)
non_mer_sig_96_transpose <- as.data.frame.matrix(t(non_mer_sig_96))

# Fit the signatures
non_mer_sig_96_out <- mm_fit_signatures(muts.input = non_mer_sig_96_transpose, sig.input = sig_ref_cosmic_v3_2_hg38,
    input.format = "classes", sample.sigt.profs = NULL, strandbias = FALSE, bootstrap = FALSE,
    genome = "hg38", dbg = FALSE, force_include = NULL)

non_mer_sigs_t <- reshape2::melt(data = non_mer_sig_96_out$estimate %>%
    tibble::rownames_to_column(var = "sample") %>%
    dplyr::select(-mutations) %>%
    as.data.table(), id.vars = "sample", variable.name = "signature", value.name = "rel_contrib")

non_mer_sigs_t$signature <- factor(non_mer_sigs_t$signature, levels = c("SBS1", "SBSblood",
    "SBS8", "SBS9", "SBS84", "SBS18", "SBS99", "SBS2", "SBS13"))

mer_mmsig_bar_p <- ggplot(non_mer_sigs_t, aes(x = sample, y = rel_contrib, fill = signature)) +
    geom_col(width = 1) + scale_fill_manual(name = "Signature", values = mmsig_colors) +
    scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0, 0)) + labs(x = "Samples", y = "Relative Contribution") +
    theme(legend.position = "right", axis.text.x = element_blank(), axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
        panel.border = element_rect(fill = "transparent"))

# 'Simulated\nNon-MERs') + theme(legend.position = 'right', panel.border =

# hjust = 0.5))
```

## Simulating the genomic background

Create a background genome for random sampling that is the eligible regions excluding the MERs

::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code .code-overflow-wrap  code-fold="true"}
non_mer_eligible <- eligible_bed %_% gr_refactor_seqs(gUtils::dt2gr(mers))

# To control for best representation, random regions will be restricted to
# range of MERs (i.e. control the variance to the same as the observed) Look at
# the distribution of MER widths for proper simulation of similar distribution
# derived from the non-MER background mer_min_width <-
# min(GenomicRanges::width(gr_refactor_seqs(gUtils::dt2gr(mers))))

# mer_q1_width <- mer_median_width - (IQR(x =
# GenomicRanges::width(gr_refactor_seqs(gUtils::dt2gr(mers)))) / 2)
# mer_q3_width <- mer_median_width + (IQR(x =
# GenomicRanges::width(gr_refactor_seqs(gUtils::dt2gr(mers)))) / 2)
# mer_max_width <-
# max(GenomicRanges::width(gr_refactor_seqs(gUtils::dt2gr(mers))))

# Visualize the observed distribution
mer_median_width <- median(GenomicRanges::width(gr_refactor_seqs(gUtils::dt2gr(mers))))
mer_width_distro <- data.table::data.table(gr2dt(mers)[, 1], gr2dt(mers)[, 2], gr2dt(mers)[,
    3], width = GenomicRanges::width(gr_refactor_seqs(gUtils::dt2gr(mers)))/1000,
    region = "MERs", simulation = 0)

mer_width_distro_plot <- ggplot(data = mer_width_distro) + ggdist::stat_slab(aes(x = width,
    y = region, fill = region), show.legend = F) + ggdist::stat_dotsinterval(aes(x = width,
    y = region, fill = region), quantiles = 200, side = "bottom", slab_linewidth = NA,
    show.legend = F) + labs(y = "MERs", x = "Width (kbp)") + scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(10,
    100, 10), expand = c(0.03, 0.03)) + scale_fill_manual(values = "#9467BD") + theme(axis.text.y = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks.y = element_blank(), axis.title.y = element_text(angle = 0, vjust = 0.5)) +
    ggplot2::annotate(geom = "text", x = mer_median_width/1000, y = 1.07, label = paste(mer_median_width/1000,
        "kbp"))

# https://stats.stackexchange.com/questions/132652/how-to-determine-which-distribution-fits-my-data-best
# https://aursiber.github.io/fitdistrplus/articles/fitdistrplus_vignette.html#Choice
# # Let's fit the MER width distribution instead of making up from quantiles #
# First, test the observed evaluation of kurtosis and squared skewness and a
# number of bootstrap runs # to help identify the best fit of the observed data
# fitdistrplus::plotdist(data = mer_width_distro$width, histo = T, demp = T,
# discrete = F) fitdistrplus::descdist(data = mer_width_distro$width, discrete
# = F, boot = 500) maybe gamma or log-normal
width_lognorm_fit <- fitdistrplus::fitdist(data = mer_width_distro$width, distr = "lnorm",
    discrete = F, method = "mle")
width_gamma_fit <- fitdistrplus::fitdist(data = mer_width_distro$width, distr = "gamma",
    discrete = F, method = "mle")
width_beta_fit <- fitdistrplus::fitdist(data = mer_width_distro$width/100, distr = "beta",
    discrete = F, optim.method = "BFGS", method = "mle")

# Let's look at the fit summaries summary(width_lognorm_fit)
# summary(width_gamma_fit) summary(width_beta_fit)

# now some fit metrics fitdistrplus::denscomp(ft = list(width_lognorm_fit,
```

Now run simulations of the background to see what they look like compared to MERs 

::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code .code-overflow-wrap  code-fold="true"}
# The regular log-normal fit is best but could be improved by limiting the
# width to same limitations as MERs (i.e. must be 10kbp or greater), This
# requires a truncated log-normal fit with a minimum of 10.0 (kbp)
# https://stackoverflow.com/questions/16947799/fitting-a-lognormal-distribution-to-truncated-data-in-r
dtruncated_log_normal <- function(x, meanlog, sdlog) {
    a_min = min(mer_width_distro$width)
    b_max = max(mer_width_distro$width)
    dtrunc(x, "lnorm", a = a_min, b = b_max, meanlog = meanlog, sdlog = sdlog)
}

ptruncated_log_normal <- function(q, meanlog, sdlog) {
    a_min = min(mer_width_distro$width)
    b_max = max(mer_width_distro$width)
    ptrunc(q, "lnorm", a = a_min, b = b_max, meanlog = meanlog, sdlog = sdlog)
}

qtruncated_log_normal <- function(x, meanlog, sdlog) {
    a_min = min(mer_width_distro$width)
    b_max = max(mer_width_distro$width)
    qtrunc(x, "lnorm", a = a_min, b = b_max, meanlog = meanlog, sdlog = sdlog)
}

width_trunc_lognorm_fit <- fitdistrplus::fitdist(data = mer_width_distro$width, distr = "truncated_log_normal",
    discrete = F, method = "mle", start = list(meanlog = 0, sdlog = 1))
```

Look at the contribution across disease stages
Starting with signatures in MGUS MERs

::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code .code-overflow-wrap  code-fold="true"}
##################################### SPLIT BY DISEASE STAGE
raw_mgus_mer_sig_muts <- muts_per_mer_for_plot %>%
    dplyr::filter(VARIANT_CLASS == "SNV" & disease_stage == "MGUS") %>%
    dplyr::select(sample, mer_id, seqnames, start, Reference_Allele, Tumor_Seq_Allele2)

# We'll overcome this by looking at all SNVs within the MERs First, given that
# there are now duplicated mutations per MER (from different samples/patients
# etc.), remove them here
mgus_mer_sig_muts <- raw_mgus_mer_sig_muts %>%
    dplyr::select(-sample) %>%
    dplyr::distinct()
mgus_mer_sig_muts$mer_id <- "MGUS"

# Now prepare the data for mmsig Correct the column names for input to mmsig
colnames(mgus_mer_sig_muts) <- c("sample", "chr", "pos", "ref", "alt")

# Prep the input for mmsig using deconstructSig
mgus_mer_sig_96 <- deconstructSigs::mut.to.sigs.input(mut.ref = mgus_mer_sig_muts,
    sample.id = "sample", chr = "chr", pos = "pos", ref = "ref", alt = "alt", bsg = BSgenome.Hsapiens.UCSC.hg38)

# Generate a 96 class SBS profile
mgus_mer_sig_96_p <- plot_sbs96(mgus_mer_sig_96) + labs(title = "MGUS MERs") + theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 14,
    hjust = 0.5))

# Translate data for use in mmsig
mgus_mer_sig_96$Total <- rowSums(mgus_mer_sig_96)
mgus_mer_sig_96_transpose <- as.data.frame.matrix(t(mgus_mer_sig_96))

# Fit the signatures Complete set of all MER mutations
mgus_mer_sig_96_out <- mm_fit_signatures(muts.input = mgus_mer_sig_96_transpose,
    sig.input = sig_ref_cosmic_v3_2_hg38, input.format = "classes", sample.sigt.profs = NULL,
    strandbias = FALSE, bootstrap = FALSE, genome = "hg38", dbg = FALSE, force_include = NULL)

mgus_mer_sigs_t <- reshape2::melt(data = mgus_mer_sig_96_out$estimate %>%
    tibble::rownames_to_column(var = "sample") %>%
    dplyr::select(-mutations) %>%
    as.data.table(), id.vars = "sample", variable.name = "signature", value.name = "rel_contrib")

mgus_mer_sigs_t$signature <- factor(mgus_mer_sigs_t$signature, levels = mmsig_signames_updated)

mgus_mer_mmsig_bar_p <- ggplot(mgus_mer_sigs_t, aes(x = sample, y = rel_contrib,
    fill = signature)) + geom_col(width = 1) + scale_fill_manual(name = "Signature",
    values = mmsig_colors) + scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0, 0)) + labs(x = "MGUS",
    y = "Relative Contribution") + theme(legend.position = "right", axis.text.x = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks.x = element_blank(), panel.border = element_rect(fill = "transparent"))

# mer_sig_96_p + mer_mmsig_bar_p + plot_layout(widths = c(1.1,0.075)) export 4
# x 11 landscape

message("SBS Signatures found in MERs:")
## SBS Signatures found in MERs:
round(t(mgus_mer_sig_96_out$estimate[-10]) * 100, digits = 2)
##           MGUS
## SBS1      2.48
## SBS2      0.00
## SBS8      0.00
## SBS9     21.80
## SBS13     0.00
## SBS18     0.00
## SBS84     0.00
## SBS99     0.00
## SBSblood 75.72

# Convert from relative to absolute for the signatures
mgus_mer_abs_sig_contrib <- as.data.frame(t(mgus_mer_sig_96_out$estimate[-10])) %>%
    tibble::rownames_to_column(var = "signatures") %>%
    dplyr::mutate(abs_burden = round(MGUS * (mgus_mer_sig_96$Total/n_distinct(raw_mgus_mer_sig_muts$sample)),
        digits = 0), group = "MGUS")
```

```{.r .cell-code .code-overflow-wrap  code-fold="true"}
    dplyr::select(sample, mer_id, seqnames, start, Reference_Allele, Tumor_Seq_Allele2)

# We'll overcome this by looking at all SNVs within the MERs First, given that
# there are now duplicated mutations per MER (from different samples/patients
# etc.), remove them here
smm_mer_sig_muts <- raw_smm_mer_sig_muts %>%
    dplyr::select(-sample) %>%
    dplyr::distinct()
smm_mer_sig_muts$mer_id <- "SMM"

# Now prepare the data for mmsig Correct the column names for input to mmsig
colnames(smm_mer_sig_muts) <- c("sample", "chr", "pos", "ref", "alt")

# Prep the input for mmsig using deconstructSig
smm_mer_sig_96 <- deconstructSigs::mut.to.sigs.input(mut.ref = smm_mer_sig_muts,
    sample.id = "sample", chr = "chr", pos = "pos", ref = "ref", alt = "alt", bsg = BSgenome.Hsapiens.UCSC.hg38)

# Generate a 96 class SBS profile
smm_mer_sig_96_p <- plot_sbs96(smm_mer_sig_96) + labs(title = "SMM MERs") + theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 14,
    hjust = 0.5))

# Translate data for use in mmsig
smm_mer_sig_96$Total <- rowSums(smm_mer_sig_96)
smm_mer_sig_96_transpose <- as.data.frame.matrix(t(smm_mer_sig_96))

# Fit the signatures Complete set of all MER mutations
smm_mer_sig_96_out <- mm_fit_signatures(muts.input = smm_mer_sig_96_transpose, sig.input = sig_ref_cosmic_v3_2_hg38,
    input.format = "classes", sample.sigt.profs = NULL, strandbias = FALSE, bootstrap = FALSE,
    genome = "hg38", dbg = FALSE, force_include = NULL)

smm_mer_sigs_t <- reshape2::melt(data = smm_mer_sig_96_out$estimate %>%
    tibble::rownames_to_column(var = "sample") %>%
    dplyr::select(-mutations) %>%
    as.data.table(), id.vars = "sample", variable.name = "signature", value.name = "rel_contrib")

smm_mer_sigs_t$signature <- factor(smm_mer_sigs_t$signature, levels = mmsig_signames_updated)

smm_mer_mmsig_bar_p <- ggplot(smm_mer_sigs_t, aes(x = sample, y = rel_contrib, fill = signature)) +
    geom_col(width = 1) + scale_fill_manual(name = "Signature", values = mmsig_colors) +
    scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0, 0)) + labs(x = "SMM", y = "Relative Contribution") +
    theme(legend.position = "right", axis.text.x = element_blank(), axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
        panel.border = element_rect(fill = "transparent"))

# mer_sig_96_p + mer_mmsig_bar_p + plot_layout(widths = c(1.1,0.075)) export 4
# x 11 landscape

message("SBS Signatures found in MERs:")
## SBS Signatures found in MERs:
round(t(smm_mer_sig_96_out$estimate[-10]) * 100, digits = 2)
##            SMM
## SBS1      4.28
## SBS2      0.00
## SBS8      0.00
## SBS9     28.25
## SBS13     0.00
## SBS18     0.00
## SBS84     0.00
## SBS99     0.00
## SBSblood 67.47

# Convert from relative to absolute for the signatures
smm_mer_abs_sig_contrib <- as.data.frame(t(smm_mer_sig_96_out$estimate[-10])) %>%
    tibble::rownames_to_column(var = "signatures") %>%
    dplyr::mutate(abs_burden = round(SMM * (smm_mer_sig_96$Total/n_distinct(raw_smm_mer_sig_muts$sample)),
        digits = 0), group = "SMM")
# ggplot(smm_mer_abs_sig_contrib) + ggplot2::geom_col(aes(x = signatures, y =
# abs_burden))
```

Convert relative burden of MER signatures to absolute

::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code .code-overflow-wrap  code-fold="true"}
# Need to include difference in relative change to sims
non_mer_sim_sigs_t <- as.data.frame(t(mean_non_mer_sim_mmsig_sbs[, -10])) %>%
    tibble::rownames_to_column(var = "signature") %>%
    dplyr::rename(rel_contrib = V1) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(sample = "Simulated\nNon-MERs")

mer_vs_non_mer_sig_pct_delta <- rbind(mgus_mer_sigs_t, smm_mer_sigs_t, mm_mer_sigs_t,
    rrmm_mer_sigs_t) %>%
    dplyr::left_join(y = non_mer_sim_sigs_t %>%
        dplyr::mutate(init_val = rel_contrib + .Machine$double.eps) %>%
        dplyr::select(signature, init_val), by = "signature") %>%
    dplyr::group_by(sample) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(abs_change_from_sim = round(((rel_contrib + .Machine$double.eps) -
        init_val) * 100, digits = 2)) %>%
    dplyr::ungroup()

mer_vs_non_mer_sig_pct_delta$sample <- factor(mer_vs_non_mer_sig_pct_delta$sample,
    levels = disease_stage_order)

mer_vs_non_mer_sig_pct_delta$signature <- factor(mer_vs_non_mer_sig_pct_delta$signature,
    levels = mmsig_signames_updated)

mer_vs_non_mer_sig_pct_delta_plot <- ggplot(mer_vs_non_mer_sig_pct_delta, aes(x = sample,
    y = abs_change_from_sim, fill = signature)) + ggplot2::annotate(geom = "rect",
    xmin = c(0.5, 1.5, 2.5, 3.5), xmax = c(1.5, 2.5, 3.5, 4.5), ymin = -20, ymax = 40,
    alpha = 0.15, fill = disease_stage_colors) + geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "dodge") +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "black", linewidth = 0.75) +
    scale_fill_manual(name = "Signature", values = mmsig_colors) + scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0.03,
    0.03), labels = scales::label_percent(accuracy = 1, scale = 1)) + labs(x = NULL,
    y = "Difference in contribution relative to non-MERs") + theme(legend.position = "right",
    axis.ticks.x = element_blank(), panel.border = element_rect(fill = "transparent"))

# Look at differences between MERs and non-MERs
message("SBSblood in MERs compared to non-MERs:")
## SBSblood in MERs compared to non-MERs:
mer_vs_non_mer_sig_pct_delta %>%
    dplyr::filter(signature == "SBSblood")
## # A tibble: 4 × 5
##   sample signature rel_contrib init_val abs_change_from_sim
##   <fct>  <fct>           <dbl>    <dbl>               <dbl>
## 1 MGUS   SBSblood        0.757    0.379                37.8
## 2 SMM    SBSblood        0.675    0.379                29.6
## 3 MM     SBSblood        0.698    0.379                31.8
## 4 RRMM   SBSblood        0.675    0.379                29.6
mer_vs_non_mer_sig_pct_delta %>%
    dplyr::filter(signature == "SBSblood") %>%
    dplyr::reframe(mean_diff_pct = mean(abs_change_from_sim))
## # A tibble: 1 × 1
##   mean_diff_pct
##           <dbl>
## 1          32.2

message("SBS9 (AID) in MERs compared to non-MERs:")

mer_vs_non_mer_sig_pct_delta %>%
    dplyr::filter(signature == "SBS9")

## 3 MM     SBS9            0.240    0.339               -9.92
## 4 RRMM   SBS9            0.191    0.339              -14.8
mer_vs_non_mer_sig_pct_delta %>%
    dplyr::filter(signature == "SBS9") %>%
    dplyr::reframe(mean_diff_pct = mean(abs_change_from_sim))
## # A tibble: 1 × 1

    tibble::rownames_to_column(var = "signatures") %>%

sig_abs_burden_barplot <- ggplot(sig_abs_burden_comparison, aes(x = group, y = abs_burden,

    values = mmsig_colors) + scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0.03, 0.03), limits = c(0,
```

```{.r .cell-code .code-overflow-wrap  code-fold="true"}
    sep = "\t", header = T)

    dplyr::filter(signature == "aggregatedsubstitutions" & biosample != "K562") %>%

    dplyr::recode_factor(Myeloma = "Myeloma\nPatients")
```

Epigenomics occupancy profiles for MM patients

::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code .code-overflow-wrap  code-fold="true"}
# Cycle through epigenomic marks, collect signal from observed mutations and
# each simulation run
epigenomic_mark <- c("H3K27ac", "H3K4me1", "H3K4me3", "H3K27me3", "H3K36me3", "ATAC")
occupancy_enrichment_profile_list <- list()
for (i in 1:length(epigenomic_mark)) {
    # Read in observed signal
    obs_occupancy_profile <- data.table(mean_signal = data.table::fread(file = paste0(base_data_path,
        "epigenomicOccupancy/observed/Myeloma/PanMyeloma-MER_RefEpigenome_Myeloma_",
        epigenomic_mark[i], "_AverageSignalArray.txt")) %>%
        purrr::as_vector() %>%
        as.numeric(), region = "MERs", pos = seq(-1000, 1000, 1))

    # Read in each simulated signal
    total_sim_occupancy_profile <- data.table()
    for (j in 1:5) {
        per_sim_occupancy_profile <- data.table(signal = data.table::fread(file = paste0(base_data_path,
            "epigenomicOccupancy/simulated/Myeloma/PanMyeloma-MER_", "sim", j, "_RefEpigenome_Myeloma_",
            epigenomic_mark[i], "_AverageSignalArray.txt")) %>%
            purrr::as_vector() %>%
            as.numeric(), region = "Simulated Non-MERs", pos = seq(-1000, 1000, 1))
        total_sim_occupancy_profile <- rrbind(total_sim_occupancy_profile, per_sim_occupancy_profile)
    }

    sim_avg_sd_occupancy_profile <- total_sim_occupancy_profile %>%
        dplyr::group_by(region, pos) %>%
        dplyr::reframe(mean_signal = mean(signal), sd_signal = sd(signal))

    occupancy_enrichment_profile_plot <- ggplot() + geom_vline(xintercept = 0, color = "black",
        linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.75, alpha = 0.8) + geom_ribbon(data = sim_avg_sd_occupancy_profile,
        aes(x = pos, ymin = mean_signal - sd_signal, ymax = mean_signal + sd_signal),
        fill = "gray70", alpha = 0.5) + geom_line(data = rrbind(sim_avg_sd_occupancy_profile[,
        -4], obs_occupancy_profile), aes(x = pos, y = mean_signal, color = region),
        linewidth = 1.25) + labs(title = epigenomic_mark[i], x = "Interval around SNV (bp)",
        y = "Mean Signal") + scale_color_manual(name = "Region", values = c("#9467BD",
        "#91D1C2")) + scale_x_continuous(expand = c(0, 0)) + theme(legend.position = "none",
        plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5), panel.border = element_rect(fill = "transparent"))

    occupancy_enrichment_profile_list[[i]] <- occupancy_enrichment_profile_plot
}

epigenomic_occupancy_enrichment_plots <- patchwork::wrap_plots(occupancy_enrichment_profile_list,
    ncol = 2)
```

## Distribution of MERs across functional genomic elements of the genome

Let's look at overlap of MERs with different typese of functional genomic features
including CDS, UTRs, E/SEs, Promoters, lncRNA, miRNA, Fragile Sites, and Normal Lymphocyte MERs

::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code .code-overflow-wrap  code-fold="true"}
# There are some elements that were splintered after conversion to hg38 with
# ensembl MM1S hg18 --> hg38
liftchain_hg18 <- rtracklayer::import.chain(paste0(base_data_path, "hg18ToHg38.over.chain"))
liftchain_hg19 <- rtracklayer::import.chain(paste0(base_data_path, "hg19ToHg38.over.chain"))

mm_enhancer_elements_hg18 <- dt2gr(fread(file = paste0(base_data_path, "MM1SEnhancerAndSuperEnhancers.hg18.bed"),
    sep = "\t", header = F, col.names = c("seqnames", "start", "end", "enhancer_id",
        "enhancer_type")))
mm_enhancer_elements_hg18$tissue <- "PC"

mm_enhancer_elements_hg38 <- rtracklayer::liftOver(x = mm_enhancer_elements_hg18,
    chain = liftchain_hg18)
# Unlist output to get GR
mm_enhancer_elements_hg38 <- gUtils::grl.unlist(mm_enhancer_elements_hg38)
mm_enhancer_elements_hg38 <- gr_refactor_seqs(mm_enhancer_elements_hg38[, -c(4, 5)])

# GC/DLBCL hg19 --> hg38
gc_dlbcl_enhancer_elements_hg19 <- dt2gr(fread(file = paste0(base_data_path, "GCandPostGCEnhancersAndSuperEnhancers.hg19.bed"),
    sep = "\t", header = F, col.names = c("seqnames", "start", "end", "tissue_original",
        "enhancer_type")))
gc_dlbcl_enhancer_elements_hg19$tissue <- "GCB"
gc_dlbcl_enhancer_elements_hg19$enhancer_id <- paste0(gc_dlbcl_enhancer_elements_hg19$tissue_original,
    rep("_", length(gc_dlbcl_enhancer_elements_hg19)), gc_dlbcl_enhancer_elements_hg19$enhancer_type,
    rep("_", length(gc_dlbcl_enhancer_elements_hg19)), seq(1, length(gc_dlbcl_enhancer_elements_hg19)))


gc_dlbcl_enhancer_elements_hg38 <- rtracklayer::liftOver(x = gc_dlbcl_enhancer_elements_hg19,
    chain = liftchain_hg19)
# Unlist output to get GR
gc_dlbcl_enhancer_elements_hg38 <- gUtils::grl.unlist(gc_dlbcl_enhancer_elements_hg38)
gc_dlbcl_enhancer_elements_hg38 <- gr_refactor_seqs(gc_dlbcl_enhancer_elements_hg38[,
    -c(1, 5, 6)])

# Combine the sets of elements
enhancer_elements <- gUtils::grbind(mm_enhancer_elements_hg38, gc_dlbcl_enhancer_elements_hg38)

# # Still some splintered elements (by small bp), let's fix here

# splintered_ee@ranges@start[1], end =

# collapsed_ee$enhancer_type <- unique(splintered_ee$enhancer_type)

# gUtils::grbind(stitched_ee, collapsed_ee) } } # Now combine the stitched with
# the others that were not splintered after conversion to hg38
# non_splintered_ee <- enhancer_elements %Q% (!enhancer_id %in%
# splintered_ee_id) complete_ee <- gUtils::grbind(stitched_ee,
# non_splintered_ee)

# Write out the complete EE list as a BED fwrite(x =
# gr2dt(complete_ee)[,c(1:3,6:8)], file = 'enhancerElements.hg38.bed', sep =
# '\t', row.names = F, col.names = T)

complete_ee <- read_bed_file(bed_file_path = paste0(base_data_path, "enhancerElements.hg38.bed"),
    has_header = T)

    "Reference_Allele", "Tumor_Seq_Allele2", "VAF", "Tumor_Sample_Barcode", "Matched_Norm_Sample_Barcode",
```

:::


Gather all other elements for the overlap analysis

::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code .code-overflow-wrap  code-fold="true"}
# The functional genomic elements that will be assessed for overlap with MERs,
# as defined by PCAWG non-coding paper Coding Sequences - Exons of all
# protein-coding genes (from Ensembl v108) 5' UTRs - 5' UTRs of all
# protein-coding genes (from Ensembl v108) 3' UTRs - 3' UTRs of all
# protein-coding genes (from Ensembl v108) Promoters - Promoters for
# protein-coding genes (EPIraction - ENCODE ENCFF801AIE / Ensembl v106, will
# harmonize based on gene name) Enhancers/Super-Enhancers - GCB and PC sets
# from Bal et al and Loven et al Normal Lymphocyte MERs - MERs identified in
# HSCs, Naive B, and Memory B cells (Machado et al) Fragile Sites - HumCFS
# adapted from JaBbA analysis (Kumar et al) Long non-coding RNA - lncRNAs
# adapted from PCAWG (Rheinbay et al) Micro RNA - miRNAs adapted from (Rheinbay
# et al)

########## lncRNA ###########
lncrna_elements_raw <- gene_body_hg38 %Q% (gene_biotype == "lncRNA")

# Now filter the raw set based on PCAWG recommendations no sense overlap (i.e.
# no overlap on same strand)
lncrna_cds_overlap_to_exclude <- lncrna_elements_raw[gr.in(query = lncrna_elements_raw,
    subject = gene_body_hg38 %Q% (gene_biotype == "protein_coding"), ignore.strand = F)]

# lncRNA genes below 200 bp in length
lncrna_small_genes_to_exclude <- gr2dt(lncrna_elements_raw) %>%
    dplyr::group_by(gene_name) %>%
    dplyr::reframe(tot_width = sum(width)) %>%
    dplyr::filter(tot_width < 200) %>%
    dplyr::select(gene_name) %>%
    purrr::as_vector() %>%
    as.character()

# filter to get final set
lncrna_elements_gr <- lncrna_elements_raw %Q% (!gene_name %in% c(lncrna_cds_overlap_to_exclude$gene_name,
    lncrna_small_genes_to_exclude))

########## miRNA ###########
mirna_elements_gr <- gene_body_hg38 %Q% (gene_biotype == "miRNA")

########## Promoters ########### Read in per chrom set
promoter_sites_gr <- GenomicRanges::trim(read_bed_file(bed_file_path = paste0(base_data_path,
    "functionalGenomicElements/promoters.hg38.bed"), additional_col_names = "gene_name")) %Q%
    (gene_name %in% unique(genes$gene_name))
## Warning in gUtils::dt2gr(input_dt, seqlengths = gUtils::hg_seqlengths()[1:24]):
## Warning: Coercing to GRanges via non-standard columns
# sum(GenomicRanges::width(promoter_sites_gr)) / 1e6
promoter_sites_gr$prom_id <- paste0("prom_", promoter_sites_gr$gene_name)

########## CDS ###########
cds_elements_gr <- cds_regions

########## 5' UTR ###########
five_prime_utr_elements_gr <- five_prime_utr_regions

########## 3' UTR ###########
three_prime_utr_elements_gr <- three_prime_utr_regions

########## Introns ###########
intron_elements_gr <- intron_regions

########## Intergenic ###########
intergenic_elements_gr <- intergenic_regions
```
:::


Normal lymphocyte mutationally enriched regions
Extracting the mutations for the fishHook models

::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code .code-overflow-wrap  code-fold="true"}
liftchain_hg19 <- rtracklayer::import.chain(paste0(base_data_path, "hg19ToHg38.over.chain"))

# Read in SNVs from normal lymphs
normal_sites <- fread(file = paste0(base_data_path, "som2_indel_SNV_allDF_v2.machado_normalLymphocytes.txt.gz"),
    sep = "\t", header = T)[, -1]
# Convert to GR and then to hg38
normal_sites_gr <- dt2gr(normal_sites)
## Warning in dt2gr(normal_sites): Warning: Coercing to GRanges via non-standard
## columns
normal_sites_hg38 <- rtracklayer::liftOver(x = gr.chr(normal_sites_gr), chain = liftchain_hg19)
# Unlist output to get GR
normal_sites_hg38 <- gUtils::grl.unlist(normal_sites_hg38)
normal_sites_hg38 <- gr_refactor_seqs(normal_sites_hg38)
```

:::


Building the consensus regions

::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code .code-overflow-wrap  code-fold="true"}
snv_normal_lymphocyte_sushi_results <- readRDS(paste0(base_data_path, "fishHookModels/lymphocyteLineage/normal_lymphocyte_snv_results.sushi.rds"))
indel_normal_lymphocyte_sushi_results <- readRDS(paste0(base_data_path, "fishHookModels/lymphocyteLineage/normal_lymphocyte_indel_results.sushi.rds"))

# Visualize in a genome-wide fashion like GWAS for tiles
snv_normal_lymphocyte_sushi_results$mut_type <- "SNV"
indel_normal_lymphocyte_sushi_results$mut_type <- "InDel"

# first, subset to only tiles with FDR < 0.05 then sort by genomic coordinate
sig_snv_lymph_sushi <- snv_normal_lymphocyte_sushi_results[fdr < 0.05][order(seqnames,
    start)]
sig_indel_lymph_sushi <- indel_normal_lymphocyte_sushi_results[fdr < 0.05][order(seqnames,
    start)]

# How many final tiles
message("FDR Significant SNV enriched tiles in Normal Lymphocytes: ", nrow(sig_snv_lymph_sushi))
## FDR Significant SNV enriched tiles in Normal Lymphocytes: 608
message("FDR Significant InDel enriched tiles in Normal Lymphocytes: ", nrow(sig_indel_lymph_sushi))
## FDR Significant InDel enriched tiles in Normal Lymphocytes: 153

# Create a set of stitched overlapping tiles to reduce to consensus segments
stitched_lymph_ncsnv_er <- gr_refactor_seqs(gUtils::gr.reduce(gr_refactor_seqs(gUtils::dt2gr(sig_snv_lymph_sushi)))[,
    c()])
stitched_lymph_ncsnv_er$nearest_gene <- genes$gene_name[nearest(x = stitched_lymph_ncsnv_er,
    subject = gUtils::gr.stripstrand(genes))]

stitched_lymph_ncindel_er <- gr_refactor_seqs(gUtils::gr.reduce(gr_refactor_seqs(gUtils::dt2gr(sig_indel_lymph_sushi)))[,
    c()])
stitched_lymph_ncindel_er$nearest_gene <- genes$gene_name[nearest(x = stitched_lymph_ncindel_er,
    subject = gUtils::gr.stripstrand(genes))]


# Lets loop through each stitched mutation enriched region and grab number of
# tiles and combined p-value SNVs
lymph_ncsnv_fish_data_per_site <- data.table::data.table(k_tiles = rep(NA, length(stitched_lymph_ncsnv_er)),
    q_value = rep(NA, length(stitched_lymph_ncsnv_er)), effect_size_mean = rep(NA,
        length(stitched_lymph_ncsnv_er)), count_density_mean = rep(NA, length(stitched_lymph_ncsnv_er)),
    pred_count_density_mean = rep(NA, length(stitched_lymph_ncsnv_er)), eligible_size_mean = rep(NA,
        length(stitched_lymph_ncsnv_er)))

for (i in 1:length(stitched_lymph_ncsnv_er)) {
    tile_set_to_stitch <- gr.findoverlaps(subject = stitched_lymph_ncsnv_er[i, ],
        query = gr_refactor_seqs(gUtils::dt2gr(sig_snv_lymph_sushi)), type = "within",
        qcol = c("fdr", "effectsize", "count.density", "count.pred.density", "eligible"))

    # Calculate the mean FDR p-value, effect size, count density, predicted
    # count density and the eligible region
    new_qval <- poolr::bonferroni(p = tile_set_to_stitch$fdr)

    lymph_ncsnv_fish_data_per_site$k_tiles[i] <- length(tile_set_to_stitch)
    lymph_ncsnv_fish_data_per_site$q_value[i] <- as.numeric(new_qval$p)
    lymph_ncsnv_fish_data_per_site$effect_size_mean[i] <- mean(tile_set_to_stitch$effectsize)
    lymph_ncsnv_fish_data_per_site$count_density_mean[i] <- mean(tile_set_to_stitch$count.density)
    lymph_ncsnv_fish_data_per_site$pred_count_density_mean[i] <- mean(tile_set_to_stitch$count.pred.density)
    lymph_ncsnv_fish_data_per_site$eligible_size_mean[i] <- mean(tile_set_to_stitch$eligible)
}

# Add the final metadata to the consensus sites
stitched_lymph_ncsnv_er$k_tiles <- lymph_ncsnv_fish_data_per_site$k_tiles
stitched_lymph_ncsnv_er$q_value <- lymph_ncsnv_fish_data_per_site$q_value
stitched_lymph_ncsnv_er$effect_size_mean <- lymph_ncsnv_fish_data_per_site$effect_size_mean
stitched_lymph_ncsnv_er$count_density_mean <- lymph_ncsnv_fish_data_per_site$count_density_mean
stitched_lymph_ncsnv_er$pred_count_density_mean <- lymph_ncsnv_fish_data_per_site$pred_count_density_mean
stitched_lymph_ncsnv_er$eligible_size_mean <- lymph_ncsnv_fish_data_per_site$eligible_size_mean
stitched_lymph_ncsnv_er$mut_uniq_id <- paste0("lymph_snv_er", seq(1, length(stitched_lymph_ncsnv_er)))

# InDels
lymph_ncindel_fish_data_per_site <- data.table::data.table(k_tiles = rep(NA, length(stitched_lymph_ncindel_er)),
    q_value = rep(NA, length(stitched_lymph_ncindel_er)), effect_size_mean = rep(NA,
        length(stitched_lymph_ncindel_er)), count_density_mean = rep(NA, length(stitched_lymph_ncindel_er)),
    pred_count_density_mean = rep(NA, length(stitched_lymph_ncindel_er)), eligible_size_mean = rep(NA,
        length(stitched_lymph_ncindel_er)))

for (i in 1:length(stitched_lymph_ncindel_er)) {
    tile_set_to_stitch <- gr.findoverlaps(subject = stitched_lymph_ncindel_er[i,
        ], query = gr_refactor_seqs(gUtils::dt2gr(sig_indel_lymph_sushi)), type = "within",
        qcol = c("fdr", "effectsize", "count.density", "count.pred.density", "eligible"))

    # Calculate the mean FDR p-value, effect size, count density, predicted
    # count density and the eligible region
    new_qval <- poolr::bonferroni(p = tile_set_to_stitch$fdr)

    lymph_ncindel_fish_data_per_site$k_tiles[i] <- length(tile_set_to_stitch)
    lymph_ncindel_fish_data_per_site$q_value[i] <- as.numeric(new_qval$p)
    lymph_ncindel_fish_data_per_site$effect_size_mean[i] <- mean(tile_set_to_stitch$effectsize)
    lymph_ncindel_fish_data_per_site$count_density_mean[i] <- mean(tile_set_to_stitch$count.density)
    lymph_ncindel_fish_data_per_site$pred_count_density_mean[i] <- mean(tile_set_to_stitch$count.pred.density)
    lymph_ncindel_fish_data_per_site$eligible_size_mean[i] <- mean(tile_set_to_stitch$eligible)
}

# Add the final metadata to the consensus sites
stitched_lymph_ncindel_er$k_tiles <- lymph_ncindel_fish_data_per_site$k_tiles
stitched_lymph_ncindel_er$q_value <- lymph_ncindel_fish_data_per_site$q_value
stitched_lymph_ncindel_er$effect_size_mean <- lymph_ncindel_fish_data_per_site$effect_size_mean
stitched_lymph_ncindel_er$count_density_mean <- lymph_ncindel_fish_data_per_site$count_density_mean
stitched_lymph_ncindel_er$pred_count_density_mean <- lymph_ncindel_fish_data_per_site$pred_count_density_mean
stitched_lymph_ncindel_er$eligible_size_mean <- lymph_ncindel_fish_data_per_site$eligible_size_mean
stitched_lymph_ncindel_er$mut_uniq_id <- paste0("indel_er", seq(1, length(stitched_lymph_ncindel_er)))

# Combine the lymphoctye enriched regions to get the normal lymphocyte MERs
lymphocyte_mers <- gr2dt(gr_refactor_seqs(grbind(stitched_lymph_ncsnv_er, stitched_lymph_ncindel_er))) %>%
    dplyr::arrange(q_value)
```
:::


Look at the breakdown of MERs based on overlap with protein-coding gene bodies
(CDS / 3' UTRs / 5' UTRs / Introns)

::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code .code-overflow-wrap  code-fold="true"}
# All MER muts GR
muts_per_mer_gr <- muts_per_mer %>%
    dplyr::left_join(y = mers %>%
        dplyr::select(mut_uniq_id, mer_id, mer_gene_label), by = "mut_uniq_id") %>%
    dt_to_gr()

# Loop through all MERs and find the overlap of each mutation in relation to
# protein coding gene bodies
gene_body_overlap_pct <- data.table::data.table(mer_id = mers$mer_id, mer_gene_label = mers$mer_gene_label,
    cds_overlap_pct = 0, three_prime_utr_overlap_pct = 0, five_prime_utr_overlap_pct = 0,
    intron_overlap_pct = 0)

for (i in 1:n_distinct(gene_body_overlap_pct$mer_id)) {
    # Get the total number of mutations at the MER
    per_mer_muts <- muts_per_mer_gr %Q% (mer_id == unique(gene_body_overlap_pct$mer_id)[i])
    mer_mut_denom <- length(per_mer_muts)

    # Find where each mutation in the MER overlaps and calculate the percentage
    # CDS
    mer_mut_cds_overlap <- gr_refactor_seqs(gUtils::gr.findoverlaps(query = GenomicRanges::reduce(cds_elements_gr),
        subject = per_mer_muts, scol = c("mer_gene_label", "mer_id")))
    gene_body_overlap_pct$cds_overlap_pct[i] <- round(length(mer_mut_cds_overlap)/mer_mut_denom *
        100, digits = 2)
    # remove the eligible muts, muts cannot be in two regions (InDels)
    if (length(mer_mut_cds_overlap) > 0) {
        per_mer_muts <- per_mer_muts[-mer_mut_cds_overlap$subject.id]
    }

    # 3' UTR
    mer_mut_three_prime_utr_overlap <- gr_refactor_seqs(gUtils::gr.findoverlaps(query = GenomicRanges::reduce(three_prime_utr_elements_gr) %_%
        cds_elements_gr, subject = per_mer_muts, scol = c("mer_gene_label", "mer_id")))
    gene_body_overlap_pct$three_prime_utr_overlap_pct[i] <- round(length(mer_mut_three_prime_utr_overlap)/mer_mut_denom *
        100, digits = 2)
    # remove the eligible muts, muts cannot be in two regions (InDels)
    if (length(mer_mut_three_prime_utr_overlap) > 0) {
        per_mer_muts <- per_mer_muts[-mer_mut_three_prime_utr_overlap$subject.id]
    }

    # 5' UTR
    mer_mut_five_prime_utr_overlap <- gr_refactor_seqs(gUtils::gr.findoverlaps(query = GenomicRanges::reduce(five_prime_utr_elements_gr) %_%
        cds_regions %_% three_prime_utr_regions, subject = per_mer_muts, scol = c("mer_gene_label",
        "mer_id")))
    gene_body_overlap_pct$five_prime_utr_overlap_pct[i] <- round(length(mer_mut_five_prime_utr_overlap)/mer_mut_denom *
        100, digits = 2)
    # remove the eligible muts, muts cannot be in two regions (InDels)
    if (length(mer_mut_five_prime_utr_overlap) > 0) {
        per_mer_muts <- per_mer_muts[-mer_mut_five_prime_utr_overlap$subject.id]
    }

    # Intronic
    mer_mut_intron_overlap <- gr_refactor_seqs(gUtils::gr.findoverlaps(query = GenomicRanges::reduce(intron_elements_gr) %_%
        cds_elements_gr %_% five_prime_utr_elements_gr %_% three_prime_utr_elements_gr,
        subject = per_mer_muts, scol = c("mer_gene_label", "mer_id")))
    gene_body_overlap_pct$intron_overlap_pct[i] <- round(length(mer_mut_intron_overlap)/mer_mut_denom *
        100, digits = 2)
}

# gene body
gene_body_mers <- gene_body_overlap_pct %>%
    dplyr::group_by(mer_id, mer_gene_label) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(gene_body_pct = round(sum(cds_overlap_pct, intron_overlap_pct,
        five_prime_utr_overlap_pct, three_prime_utr_overlap_pct), digits = 1)) %>%
    dplyr::ungroup() %>%
    dplyr::arrange(desc(gene_body_pct)) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(rank = row_number(), intergenic_pct = round(100 - gene_body_pct,
        digits = 2))

# For initial plotting to see the breakdown high-level ordering based on gene
# body or intergenic
gene_body_mers$rank <- factor(gene_body_mers$rank, levels = gene_body_mers$rank)

gene_body_mers_ordered <- gene_body_mers %>%
    dplyr::select(-gene_body_pct) %>%
    reshape2::melt(id.vars = c("mer_id", "mer_gene_label", "rank"), variable.name = "enhancer_type",
        value.name = "pct")

gene_body_mers_ordered$enhancer_type <- factor(gene_body_mers_ordered$enhancer_type,
    levels = c("cds_overlap_pct", "five_prime_utr_overlap_pct", "three_prime_utr_overlap_pct",
        "intron_overlap_pct", "intergenic_pct"))

gene_body_mer_barplot <- ggplot(gene_body_mers_ordered) + geom_col(aes(x = rank,
    y = pct, fill = enhancer_type), width = 1) + scale_fill_manual(name = "Features",
    values = alpha(paletteer_d("ggsci::hallmarks_light_cosmic")[c(1, 4, 2, 3, 5)],
        alpha = 0.7), labels = c("CDS", "5' UTR", "3' UTR", "Intron", "Intergenic")) +
    labs(y = "Overlap with feature", x = "MERs") + scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::label_percent(accuracy = 1,
    scale = 1), expand = c(0.01, 0.01)) + theme(axis.text.x = element_blank(), axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
    panel.border = element_rect(fill = NA))

# Now cluster the MERs based on overlap with gene body features
# clustered_gene_body_mers_dendro <- factoextra::fviz_nbclust(x =
# gene_body_mers %>% dplyr::select(mer_id,cds_overlap_pct,
# three_prime_utr_overlap_pct, five_prime_utr_overlap_pct, intron_overlap_pct,
# intergenic_pct) %>% tibble::column_to_rownames('mer_id'), FUN =
# factoextra::hcut, method = 'wss')

mer_gene_body_feature_clusters <- factoextra::hcut(x = gene_body_mers %>%
    dplyr::select(mer_id, cds_overlap_pct, three_prime_utr_overlap_pct, five_prime_utr_overlap_pct,
        intron_overlap_pct, intergenic_pct) %>%
    tibble::column_to_rownames("mer_id"), k = 4, stand = F)
# factoextra::fviz_dend(mer_gene_body_feature_clusters, show_labels = T)

# Attach the MER ID to the cluster assignment
gene_body_mer_cluster_assignemnt <- data.table::data.table(mer_id = names(mer_gene_body_feature_clusters$cluster),
    cluster_id = mer_gene_body_feature_clusters$cluster)

# Visualize the composite of each gene body MER cluster
clustered_gene_body_mers_comp_boxplot <- gene_body_mers_ordered %>%
    dplyr::left_join(y = gene_body_mer_cluster_assignemnt, by = "mer_id") %>%
    ggplot() + geom_boxplot(aes(x = enhancer_type, y = pct, fill = enhancer_type)) +
    facet_wrap(facets = ~cluster_id, nrow = 2) + scale_fill_manual(name = "Features",
    values = alpha(paletteer_d("ggsci::hallmarks_light_cosmic")[c(1, 4, 2, 3, 5)],
        alpha = 0.7), labels = c("CDS", "5' UTR", "3' UTR", "Intron", "Intergenic")) +
    labs(y = "Overlap with feature", x = NULL) + scale_x_discrete(labels = c("CDS",
    "5' UTR", "3' UTR", "Intron", "Intergenic")) + scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::label_percent(accuracy = 1,
    scale = 1)) + theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 50, vjust = 0.5), axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
    panel.border = element_rect(fill = NA))

table(gene_body_mer_cluster_assignemnt$cluster_id)
## 
##   1   2   3   4 
##  96   9  31 171

# cluster 1 - Intronic MERs (n=96) cluster 2 - CDS MERs (n=9) cluster 3 - UTR
# MERs (n=31) cluster 4 - Intergenic MERs (n=171)

gene_body_mers %>%
    dplyr::left_join(y = gene_body_mer_cluster_assignemnt, by = "mer_id") %>%
    dplyr::group_by(cluster_id) %>%
    dplyr::reframe(mean_intron_overlap = mean(intron_overlap_pct)) %>%
    dplyr::arrange(desc(mean_intron_overlap))
## # A tibble: 4 × 2

##        <int>               <dbl>
## 1          1              97.7

## 3          2              12.2  
## 4          4               0.903

gene_body_mers %>%
    dplyr::left_join(y = gene_body_mer_cluster_assignemnt, by = "mer_id") %>%
    dplyr::group_by(cluster_id) %>%
    dplyr::reframe(mean_cds_overlap = mean(cds_overlap_pct)) %>%
    dplyr::arrange(desc(mean_cds_overlap))
## # A tibble: 4 × 2

##        <int>            <dbl>
## 1          2           81.0  
## 2          3            8.70 
## 3          1            0.627
## 4          4            0.218

gene_body_mers %>%
    dplyr::left_join(y = gene_body_mer_cluster_assignemnt, by = "mer_id") %>%
    dplyr::group_by(cluster_id) %>%
    dplyr::reframe(mean_three_prime_utr_overlap = mean(three_prime_utr_overlap_pct)) %>%
    dplyr::arrange(desc(mean_three_prime_utr_overlap))
## # A tibble: 4 × 2
##   cluster_id mean_three_prime_utr_overlap
##        <int>                        <dbl>
## 1          3                       12.9  
## 2          2                        3.06 
## 3          1                        0.196
## 4          4                        0.144

gene_body_mers %>%
    dplyr::left_join(y = gene_body_mer_cluster_assignemnt, by = "mer_id") %>%
    dplyr::group_by(cluster_id) %>%
    dplyr::reframe(mean_five_prime_utr_overlap = mean(five_prime_utr_overlap_pct)) %>%
    dplyr::arrange(desc(mean_five_prime_utr_overlap))
## # A tibble: 4 × 2
##   cluster_id mean_five_prime_utr_overlap
##        <int>                       <dbl>
## 1          3                       3.90 
## 2          1                       0.259
## 3          2                       0.231
## 4          4                       0.217

gene_body_mers %>%
    dplyr::left_join(y = gene_body_mer_cluster_assignemnt, by = "mer_id") %>%
    dplyr::group_by(cluster_id) %>%
    dplyr::reframe(median_intergenic_overlap = median(intergenic_pct)) %>%
    dplyr::arrange(desc(median_intergenic_overlap))
## # A tibble: 4 × 2
##   cluster_id median_intergenic_overlap
##        <int>                     <dbl>
## 1          4                     100  
## 2          3                      21.7
## 3          1                       0  
## 4          2                       0

# Create labels for each cluster
gene_body_mers <- gene_body_mers %>%
    dplyr::left_join(y = gene_body_mer_cluster_assignemnt, by = "mer_id") %>%
    dplyr::mutate(cluster_label = dplyr::case_when(cluster_id == 1 ~ "Intronic",
        cluster_id == 2 ~ "CDS", cluster_id == 3 ~ "UTR", cluster_id == 4 ~ "Intergenic"))
# Create the MER overlap base
mers_overlap <- mers %>%
    dplyr::left_join(y = gene_body_mers %>%
        dplyr::select(mer_id, cds_overlap_pct, three_prime_utr_overlap_pct, five_prime_utr_overlap_pct,
            intron_overlap_pct, intergenic_pct, gene_body_pct, cluster_label), by = "mer_id")

message("Protein-coding gene body MERs: ", mers_overlap %>%
    dplyr::filter(cluster_label != "Intergenic") %>%
    dplyr::count() %>%
    as.numeric(), " (", round(mers_overlap %>%
    dplyr::filter(cluster_label != "Intergenic") %>%
    dplyr::count() %>%
    as.numeric()/307 * 100, digits = 1), "%)")
## Protein-coding gene body MERs: 136 (44.3%)

message("Intergenic MERs: ", mers_overlap %>%
    dplyr::filter(cluster_label == "Intergenic") %>%
    dplyr::count() %>%
    as.numeric(), " (", round(mers_overlap %>%
    dplyr::filter(cluster_label == "Intergenic") %>%
    dplyr::count() %>%
    as.numeric()/307 * 100, digits = 1), "%)")
## Intergenic MERs: 171 (55.7%)
```

```{.r .cell-code .code-overflow-wrap  code-fold="true"}
        "Intergenic")) + labs(y = "Overlap with feature", x = NULL) + scale_x_discrete(labels = c("CDS",
    "3' UTR", "5' UTR", "Intron", "Intergenic")) + scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::label_percent(accuracy = 1,
    scale = 1)) + theme(legend.position = "none", axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 50,
    vjust = 0.5), axis.ticks.x = element_blank(), panel.border = element_rect(fill = NA))
```

```{.r .cell-code .code-overflow-wrap  code-fold="true"}
    input_flank = 0)$name, labels = get_locus_axis(input_window = zfp36l1_window,
    input_flank = 0)$labels, limits = get_locus_axis(input_window = zfp36l1_window,
    input_flank = 0)$limits, expand = c(0.01, 0.01)) + ggplot2::theme(panel.background = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(), panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(), axis.title.y = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks.y = element_line(linewidth = 2.5, color = "black"), axis.ticks.length.y = unit(2,
        units = "mm"), plot.margin = unit(c(0.1, 0.1, 0.1, 0.1), "mm"), axis.text.y = element_text(size = 10),
    axis.title.x = element_blank(), axis.text.x = element_blank(), axis.line.x = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks.x = element_blank(), legend.text = element_text(size = 11), legend.position = "none")

zfp36l1_locus_view <- patchwork::wrap_plots(list(scale_track_zfp36l1, mutation_track_zfp36l1,
    gene_track_zfp36l1), ncol = 1, guides = "collect", heights = c(0.07, 0.09, 0.07))
```

Now look at the MER mutation overlap with lncRNA and miRNA gene bodies

::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code .code-overflow-wrap  code-fold="true"}
# Loop through all MERs and find the overlap of each mutation in relation to
# protein coding gene bodies
ncgene_body_overlap_pct <- data.table::data.table(mer_id = mers$mer_id, mer_gene_label = mers$mer_gene_label,
    lncrna_overlap_pct = 0, mirna_overlap_pct = 0)

for (i in 1:n_distinct(ncgene_body_overlap_pct$mer_id)) {
    # Get the total number of mutations at the MER
    per_mer_muts <- muts_per_mer_gr %Q% (mer_id == unique(ncgene_body_overlap_pct$mer_id)[i])
    mer_mut_denom <- length(per_mer_muts)

    # Find where each mutation in the MER overlaps and calculate the percentage
    # miRNA
    mer_mut_mirna_overlap <- gr_refactor_seqs(gUtils::gr.findoverlaps(query = mirna_elements_gr,
        subject = per_mer_muts, qcol = "gene_name", scol = c("mer_gene_label", "mer_id")))
    ncgene_body_overlap_pct$mirna_overlap_pct[i] <- round(length(mer_mut_mirna_overlap)/mer_mut_denom *
        100, digits = 2)

    # lncRNA
    mer_mut_lncrna_overlap <- gr_refactor_seqs(gUtils::gr.findoverlaps(query = lncrna_elements_gr,
        subject = per_mer_muts, qcol = "gene_name", scol = c("mer_gene_label", "mer_id")))
    ncgene_body_overlap_pct$lncrna_overlap_pct[i] <- round(length(mer_mut_lncrna_overlap)/mer_mut_denom *
        100, digits = 2)
}

# Add the lncRNA and miRNA gene bodies to MER overlap
mers_overlap <- mers_overlap %>%
    dplyr::left_join(y = ncgene_body_overlap_pct, by = c("mer_id", "mer_gene_label"))

ncgene_mers <- mers_overlap %>%
    dplyr::filter(lncrna_overlap_pct > 0) %>%
    dplyr::select(mer_id, mer_gene_label, total_mut_patients, pct_total_mut_patients,
        cluster_label, gene_body_pct, lncrna_overlap_pct, mirna_overlap_pct, overlapping_genes) %>%
    dplyr::arrange(desc(pct_total_mut_patients))

# All lncRNA

    dplyr::select(mer_id, mer_gene_label, cluster_label, gene_body_pct, lncrna_overlap_pct,
```

Enhancers/Super-Enhancers and Promoters

::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code .code-overflow-wrap  code-fold="true"}
# Loop through all MERs and find the overlap of each mutation in relation to
# protein coding gene bodies
cre_overlap_pct <- data.table::data.table(mer_id = mers$mer_id, mer_gene_label = mers$mer_gene_label,
    enhancer_overlap_pct = 0, enhancer_id = NA, tissue = NA, enhancer_type = NA,
    promoter_overlap_pct = 0, prom_id = NA)

for (i in 1:n_distinct(cre_overlap_pct$mer_id)) {
    # Get the total number of mutations at the MER
    per_mer_muts <- muts_per_mer_gr %Q% (mer_id == unique(cre_overlap_pct$mer_id)[i])
    mer_mut_denom <- length(per_mer_muts)

    # E/SEs
    mer_mut_ee_overlap <- gr_refactor_seqs(gUtils::gr.findoverlaps(query = complete_ee,
        subject = per_mer_muts, qcol = c("enhancer_id", "tissue", "enhancer_type"),
        scol = c("mer_gene_label", "mer_id")))

    cre_overlap_pct$enhancer_overlap_pct[i] <- round(length(unique(mer_mut_ee_overlap$subject.id))/mer_mut_denom *
        100, digits = 2)

    # Get cell line specific EE ID
    if (length(unique(mer_mut_ee_overlap$enhancer_id)) > 1) {
        cre_overlap_pct$enhancer_id[i] <- stringr::str_flatten(string = sort(unique(mer_mut_ee_overlap$enhancer_id)),
            collapse = ",")
    } else if (length(unique(mer_mut_ee_overlap$enhancer_id)) == 1) {
        cre_overlap_pct$enhancer_id[i] <- unique(mer_mut_ee_overlap$enhancer_id)
    }

    # Get cell line
    if (length(unique(mer_mut_ee_overlap$tissue)) > 1) {
        cre_overlap_pct$tissue[i] <- stringr::str_flatten(string = sort(unique(mer_mut_ee_overlap$tissue)),
            collapse = ",")
    } else if (length(unique(mer_mut_ee_overlap$tissue)) == 1) {
        cre_overlap_pct$tissue[i] <- unique(mer_mut_ee_overlap$tissue)
    }

    # Get EE enhancer_type
    if (length(unique(mer_mut_ee_overlap$enhancer_type)) > 1) {
        cre_overlap_pct$enhancer_type[i] <- stringr::str_flatten(string = sort(unique(mer_mut_ee_overlap$enhancer_type)),
            collapse = ",")
    } else if (length(unique(mer_mut_ee_overlap$enhancer_type)) == 1) {
        cre_overlap_pct$enhancer_type[i] <- unique(mer_mut_ee_overlap$enhancer_type)
    }

    # Promoters

    mer_mut_promoter_overlap <- gr_refactor_seqs(gUtils::gr.findoverlaps(query = promoter_sites_gr,
        subject = per_mer_muts, qcol = "prom_id", scol = c("mer_gene_label", "mer_id")))
    cre_overlap_pct$promoter_overlap_pct[i] <- round(length(mer_mut_promoter_overlap)/mer_mut_denom *
        100, digits = 2)

    # Get all overlapping promoters
    if (length(unique(mer_mut_promoter_overlap$prom_id)) > 1) {
        cre_overlap_pct$prom_id[i] <- stringr::str_flatten(string = sort(unique(mer_mut_promoter_overlap$prom_id)),
            collapse = ",")
    } else if (length(unique(mer_mut_promoter_overlap$prom_id)) == 1) {
        cre_overlap_pct$prom_id[i] <- unique(mer_mut_promoter_overlap$prom_id)
    }
}

# Add the Enhancer / Promoter to MER overlap
mers_overlap <- mers_overlap %>%
    dplyr::left_join(y = cre_overlap_pct, by = c("mer_id", "mer_gene_label"))

# E/SE MERs
e_se_mers <- mers_overlap %>%
    dplyr::filter(!is.na(tissue))
e_se_mers_gr <- gr_refactor_seqs(dt2gr(e_se_mers))

# How many MERs / genes
message("Number of significant MERs that overlap E/SE elements: ", length(unique(e_se_mers$mer_id)))
## Number of significant MERs that overlap E/SE elements: 50
message("Number of unique coding genes associated: ", length(unique(e_se_mers$nearest_gene)))
## Number of unique coding genes associated: 39

e_se_mers %>%
    dplyr::reframe(mean_enhancer_overlap = mean(enhancer_overlap_pct)) %>%
    dplyr::arrange(desc(mean_enhancer_overlap))
##   mean_enhancer_overlap
## 1               85.0808

# How many mutations in E/SE-MERs
message("Total SNVs in E/SE-MERs: ", muts_per_mer %>%
    dplyr::filter(mut_type == "SNVs" & mut_uniq_id %in% e_se_mers$mut_uniq_id) %>%
    dplyr::count())
## Total SNVs in E/SE-MERs: 4257
message("% of all MER SNVs in E/SEs: ", (muts_per_mer %>%
    dplyr::filter(mut_type == "SNVs" & mut_uniq_id %in% e_se_mers$mut_uniq_id) %>%
    dplyr::count())/(muts_per_mer %>%
    dplyr::filter(mut_type == "SNVs") %>%
    dplyr::count()) * 100)
## % of all MER SNVs in E/SEs: 23.3913951316006

message("Total InDels in E/SE-MERs: ", muts_per_mer %>%
    dplyr::filter(mut_type == "InDels" & mut_uniq_id %in% e_se_mers$mut_uniq_id) %>%
    dplyr::count())
## Total InDels in E/SE-MERs: 162
message("% of all MER InDels in E/SEs: ", (muts_per_mer %>%
    dplyr::filter(mut_type == "InDels" & mut_uniq_id %in% e_se_mers$mut_uniq_id) %>%
    dplyr::count())/(muts_per_mer %>%
    dplyr::filter(mut_type == "InDels") %>%
    dplyr::count()) * 100)
## % of all MER InDels in E/SEs: 36.8181818181818

# Range of % mutated patients
message("Range of Percentage of patients mutated at E/SE-MERs: ", paste0(range(e_se_mers$pct_total_mut_patients),
    collapse = " - "))
## Range of Percentage of patients mutated at E/SE-MERs: 5.4 - 66
```
:::

```{.r .cell-code .code-overflow-wrap  code-fold="true"}
# MERs

se_query <- e_se_universe[str_detect(string = names(e_se_universe), pattern = "SE_")]

# mer_and_non_mer_se_lola <- runLOLA(userSets = se_query, userUniverse =
# e_se_universe, regionDB = mer_and_non_mer_db) saveRDS(object =
# mer_and_non_mer_se_lola, file = paste0(base_data_path,
# 'lolaAnalysis/mers_and_simulations/mer_vs_non_mer_se_lola.rds'))
mer_and_non_mer_se_lola <- readRDS(file = paste0(base_data_path, "lolaAnalysis/mers_and_simulations/mer_vs_non_mer_se_lola.rds"))

# convert log10 p-value to empirical and then add correction
message("SE-MER overlap FDR adj. P-value: ", scales::scientific(x = mer_and_non_mer_se_lola$qValue[1]))
## SE-MER overlap FDR adj. P-value: 1.12e-30
message("Odds ratio of SE-MER overlap: ", round(mer_and_non_mer_se_lola$oddsRatio[1],
    digits = 1))
## Odds ratio of SE-MER overlap: 33
message("SE-Simulated Non-MER overlap median P-value: ", round(median(mer_and_non_mer_se_lola$qValue[-1]),
    digits = 3))
## SE-Simulated Non-MER overlap median P-value: 0.156
message("Odds ratio of SE-Simulated Non-MER overlap: ", round(median(mer_and_non_mer_se_lola$oddsRatio[-1]),
    digits = 1))
## Odds ratio of SE-Simulated Non-MER overlap: 2.9

# Transform LOLA output for plotting
mer_and_non_mer_se_lola_for_plot <- mer_and_non_mer_se_lola %>%
    dplyr::group_by(antibody) %>%
    dplyr::reframe(median_or = median(oddsRatio), sd_or = sd(oddsRatio))
message("95% CI of SE-Simulated Non-MER overlap odds ratio: ", mer_and_non_mer_se_lola_for_plot$median_or[2] -
    mer_and_non_mer_se_lola_for_plot$sd_or[2], " - ", mer_and_non_mer_se_lola_for_plot$median_or[2] +
    mer_and_non_mer_se_lola_for_plot$sd_or[2])
## 95% CI of SE-Simulated Non-MER overlap odds ratio: 0.762415005377327 - 5.02329829113012
```

Look at the BCL6 locus for comparison to Dalla Favera's DLBCL analysis

::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code .code-overflow-wrap  code-fold="true"}
bcl6_intron_window <- "chr3:187744850-187745350"

# Create window labels for additional layers bcl6_intron_woi <-
# data.table('win_start' = c(187744872, 187745199, 187744678), 'win_end' =
# c(187744917, 187745246, 187744698), 'win_name' = c(window_labeler(window =
# 'chr3:187744872-187744917', padding = 0, name_of_window = 'Normal MBC Hotspot
# 1\n(Iqbal et al. 2007)'), window_labeler(window = 'chr3:187745199-187745246',
# padding = 0, name_of_window = 'Normal MBC Hotspot 2\n(Iqbal et al. 2007)'),
# window_labeler(window = 'chr3:187744678-187744698', padding = 0,
# name_of_window = 'DLBCL Hotspot\n(Bal et al. 2022)')), 'win_color' =
# c('#9BD08A', '#C2EFB4', '#DC766F'))

bcl6_intron_woi <- data.table(win_start = c(187744872, 187745199, 187744678), win_end = c(187744917,
    187745246, 187744698), win_name = c("Normal MBC Hotspot 1*", "Normal MBC Hotspot 2**",
    "DLBCL Hotspot***"), win_color = c("#9BD08A", "#C2EFB4", "#DC766F"))

bcl6_intron_woi$win_name <- factor(bcl6_intron_woi$win_name, levels = bcl6_intron_woi$win_name)

ideogram_track_bcl6_intron <- geom_single_chrom_ideogrom(window_of_interest = bcl6_intron_window,
    flank = 250)
## Scale for x is already present.
## Adding another scale for x, which will replace the existing scale.

scale_track_bcl6_intron <- geom_locus_scale(window_of_interest = bcl6_intron_window,
    flank = 250)

gene_track_bcl6_intron <- geom_gene_track(window_of_interest = bcl6_intron_window,
    flank = 250, genes_of_interest = "BCL6", gene_name_side = "five_prime", gene_label_nudge = 30,
    gene_label_size = 4) + labs(y = "BCL6 Intron 1")

gene_track_bcl6_intron_lines <- insert_gg_layer(ggplot_base = gene_track_bcl6_intron,
    insert_layer_after = 0, ggplot2::geom_rect(data = bcl6_intron_woi, aes(xmin = win_start,
        xmax = win_end, ymin = 0, ymax = 2, fill = win_name), alpha = 0.3)) + scale_fill_manual(name = "Windows",
    values = bcl6_intron_woi$win_color)

enhancer_track_bcl6_intron <- geom_bed_track(data = complete_ee, window_of_interest = bcl6_intron_window,
    flank = 250, aes_y_column = "tissue", aes_fill_column = "enhancer_type", x_axis_theme = "blank",
    legend_title = "Element", aes_fill_colors = "#DC9445") + labs(y = "Enhancers") +
    ggplot2::geom_rect(aes(xmin = 187744872, xmax = 187744917, ymin = 0, ymax = 2),
        alpha = 0.3, fill = "#9BD08A", color = alpha("#9BD08A", alpha = 0.2)) + ggplot2::geom_rect(aes(xmin = 187745199,
    xmax = 187745246, ymin = 0, ymax = 2), alpha = 0.3, fill = "#C2EFB4", color = alpha("#C2EFB4",
    alpha = 0.2)) + ggplot2::geom_rect(aes(xmin = 187744678, xmax = 187744698, ymin = 0,
    ymax = 2), alpha = 0.3, fill = "#DC766F", color = alpha("#DC766F", alpha = 0.2))

mer_track_bcl6_intron <- geom_bed_track(data = mers_gr, window_of_interest = bcl6_intron_window,
    flank = 250, aes_y_column = "mer_type", aes_fill_column = "mer_type", x_axis_theme = "blank",
    aes_fill_colors = c("black", "#FB832D")) + labs(y = "MERs") + ggplot2::geom_rect(aes(xmin = 187744872,
    xmax = 187744917, ymin = 0, ymax = 3), alpha = 0.2, fill = "#9BD08A", color = alpha("#9BD08A",
    alpha = 0.06)) + ggplot2::geom_rect(aes(xmin = 187745199, xmax = 187745246, ymin = 0,
    ymax = 3), alpha = 0.2, fill = "#C2EFB4", color = alpha("#C2EFB4", alpha = 0.06)) +
    ggplot2::geom_rect(aes(xmin = 187744678, xmax = 187744698, ymin = 0, ymax = 3),
        alpha = 0.2, fill = "#DC766F", color = alpha("#DC766F", alpha = 0.06))

# create SNV track layer to add on top of
snv_track_bcl6_intron <- geom_count_mutations(data = snv_muts_hq_dt, window_of_interest = bcl6_intron_window,
    flank = 250, aes_y_column = "Reference_Allele", aes_fill_color = "#191970", x_axis_theme = "blank") +
    ylab("Myeloma\nSNVs") + scale_y_continuous(, breaks = scales::pretty_breaks(n = 3),
    expand = c(0.01, 0.01))
## Scale for y is already present.
## Adding another scale for y, which will replace the existing scale.

snv_track_bcl6_intron_lines <- insert_gg_layer(ggplot_base = snv_track_bcl6_intron,
    insert_layer_after = 0, ggplot2::geom_rect(data = bcl6_intron_woi, aes(xmin = win_start,
        xmax = win_end, ymin = 0, ymax = max(snv_track_bcl6_intron$data$n) + 1, fill = win_name),
        alpha = 0.3)) + scale_fill_manual(name = "Windows", values = bcl6_intron_woi$win_color) +
    scale_y_continuous(, breaks = scales::pretty_breaks(n = 3), expand = c(0.01,
        0.01))
## Scale for y is already present.
## Adding another scale for y, which will replace the existing scale.

# snv_vaf_track_bcl6_intron <- geom_vaf_landscape(data = snv_muts_hq_dt,

# fill = win_name), alpha = 0.2)) + scale_fill_manual(name = 'Windows',values =
# bcl6_intron_woi$win_color) + theme(legend.position = 'none')

indel_track_bcl6_intron <- geom_count_mutations(data = indel_muts_hq_dt, window_of_interest = bcl6_intron_window,
    flank = 250, aes_y_column = "Reference_Allele", aes_fill_color = "#EEA9B8", x_axis_theme = "blank") +
    ylab("Myeloma\nInDels") + scale_y_continuous(, breaks = scales::pretty_breaks(n = 3),
    expand = c(0.01, 0.01))
## Scale for y is already present.
## Adding another scale for y, which will replace the existing scale.

indel_track_bcl6_intron_lines <- insert_gg_layer(ggplot_base = indel_track_bcl6_intron,
    insert_layer_after = 0, ggplot2::geom_rect(data = bcl6_intron_woi, aes(xmin = win_start,
        xmax = win_end, ymin = 0, ymax = max(indel_track_bcl6_intron$data$n) + 1,
        fill = win_name), alpha = 0.3)) + scale_fill_manual(name = "Windows", values = bcl6_intron_woi$win_color) +
    scale_y_continuous(, breaks = scales::pretty_breaks(n = 3), expand = c(0.01,
        0.01))
## Scale for y is already present.
## Adding another scale for y, which will replace the existing scale.

normal_snv_track_bcl6_intron <- geom_count_mutations(data = gr2dt(normal_lymphocyte_muts_for_model) %>%
    dplyr::filter(VARIANT_CLASS == "SNV"), window_of_interest = bcl6_intron_window,
    flank = 250, aes_y_column = "Reference_Allele", aes_fill_color = "gray", x_axis_theme = "blank") +
    ylab("Lymphocyte\nSNVs") + scale_y_continuous(, breaks = scales::pretty_breaks(n = 3),
    expand = c(0.01, 0.01))
## Scale for y is already present.
## Adding another scale for y, which will replace the existing scale.

normal_snv_track_bcl6_intron_lines <- insert_gg_layer(ggplot_base = normal_snv_track_bcl6_intron,
    insert_layer_after = 0, ggplot2::geom_rect(data = bcl6_intron_woi, aes(xmin = win_start,
        xmax = win_end, ymin = 0, ymax = max(normal_snv_track_bcl6_intron$data$n) +
            1, fill = win_name), alpha = 0.3)) + scale_fill_manual(name = "Windows",
    values = bcl6_intron_woi$win_color) + scale_y_continuous(, breaks = scales::pretty_breaks(n = 3),
    expand = c(0.01, 0.01))

bcl6_intron_locus_view <- patchwork::wrap_plots(list(ideogram_track_bcl6_intron,
    scale_track_bcl6_intron, normal_snv_track_bcl6_intron_lines, indel_track_bcl6_intron_lines,
```

Look at MER-promoter overlap

::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code .code-overflow-wrap  code-fold="true"}
# Main gene set list will include any B cell related pathway from the
# immunesigdb bcell_c7_immunesigdb_pathways <-
# fgsea::gmtPathways(paste0(base_data_path,
# 'functionalGenomicElements/c7.bcell.immunesigdb.v2025.1.Hs.symbols.gmt'))

prom_mers <- mers_overlap %>%
    dplyr::filter(promoter_overlap_pct > 0) %>%
    dplyr::arrange(desc(pct_total_mut_patients))

# mean overlap of all
prom_mers %>%
    dplyr::reframe(mean_promotor_overlap = mean(promoter_overlap_pct))
##   mean_promotor_overlap
## 1              20.67854

message("Number of Promoter MERs: ", length(unique(prom_mers$mer_id)))
## Number of Promoter MERs: 41

prom_mers_genes <- mers_overlap %>%
    dplyr::filter(promoter_overlap_pct > 0) %>%
    dplyr::arrange(desc(pct_total_mut_patients)) %>%
    dplyr::select(prom_id) %>%
    dplyr::distinct() %>%
    purrr::as_vector() %>%
    stringr::str_split(pattern = ",") %>%
    unlist() %>%
    stringr::str_remove(pattern = "prom_")

message("Number of Genes linked to Promoter MERs: ", length(unique(prom_mers_genes)))
## Number of Genes linked to Promoter MERs: 41

# All promoter - E/SE-MER overlap
prom_mers %>%
    dplyr::filter(!is.na(enhancer_id)) %>%
    dplyr::select(mer_id, mer_type, mer_gene_label, total_mut_patients, pct_total_mut_patients,
        seqnames, start, end, cluster_label, cds_overlap_pct, three_prime_utr_overlap_pct,
        five_prime_utr_overlap_pct, intron_overlap_pct, intergenic_pct, lncrna_overlap_pct,
        mirna_overlap_pct, enhancer_overlap_pct, tissue, enhancer_type, promoter_overlap_pct,
        prom_id, overlapping_genes) %>%
    dplyr::arrange(desc(pct_total_mut_patients))
```

:::


Assess the overlap of MERs and normal lymphocyte MERs

::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code .code-overflow-wrap  code-fold="true"}
lymph_overlap_pct <- data.table::data.table(mer_id = mers$mer_id, mer_gene_label = mers$mer_gene_label,
    lymphocyte_overlap_pct = 0)

for (i in 1:n_distinct(lymph_overlap_pct$mer_id)) {
    # Get the total number of mutations at the MER
    per_mer_muts <- muts_per_mer_gr %Q% (mer_id == unique(lymph_overlap_pct$mer_id)[i])
    mer_mut_denom <- length(per_mer_muts)

    # Normal Lymphocyte MER overlap
    mer_mut_lymph_overlap <- gr_refactor_seqs(gUtils::gr.findoverlaps(query = gr_refactor_seqs(dt2gr(lymphocyte_mers)),
        subject = per_mer_muts, qcol = "nearest_gene", scol = c("mer_gene_label",
            "mer_id")))
    lymph_overlap_pct$lymphocyte_overlap_pct[i] <- round(length(unique(mer_mut_lymph_overlap$subject.id))/mer_mut_denom *
        100, digits = 2)
}

# Add the Enhancer / Promoter to MER overlap
mers_overlap <- mers_overlap %>%
    dplyr::left_join(y = lymph_overlap_pct, by = c("mer_id", "mer_gene_label"))

lymph_mers_overlap <- mers_overlap %>%
    dplyr::filter(lymphocyte_overlap_pct > 0)

# mean overlap of all
lymph_mers_overlap %>%
    dplyr::reframe(mean_lymphocyte_overlap = mean(lymphocyte_overlap_pct))
##   mean_lymphocyte_overlap
## 1                96.32842

message("Number of Lymphocyte overlap MERs: ", length(unique(lymph_mers_overlap$mer_id)))
## Number of Lymphocyte overlap MERs: 19

# E/SE-MERs that overlap with normal lymphocyte MERs

    dplyr::filter(tissue == "GCB") %>%
    dplyr::select(mer_id, mer_type, mer_gene_label, total_mut_patients, pct_total_mut_patients,
```

```{.r .cell-code .code-overflow-wrap  code-fold="true"}
mer_distro_by_protein_coding <- mers_overlap %>%
    dplyr::select(mer_id, midpoint_abs_linear, q_value, cluster_label) %>%
    dplyr::rename(feature = cluster_label) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(group = "Protein-coding")

mer_distro_by_enhancer <- e_se_mers %>%
    dplyr::mutate(feature = case_when(!is.na(enhancer_id) ~ "Enhancers")) %>%
    dplyr::select(mer_id, midpoint_abs_linear, q_value, feature) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(group = "Enhancers")

mer_distro_by_promoter <- prom_mers %>%
    dplyr::mutate(feature = case_when(!is.na(prom_id) ~ "Promoters")) %>%
    dplyr::select(mer_id, midpoint_abs_linear, q_value, feature) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(group = "Promoters")

mer_distro_by_lncrna <- lncrna_mers %>%
    dplyr::mutate(feature = case_when(!is.na(lncrna) ~ "lncRNA")) %>%
    dplyr::filter(!is.na(lncrna)) %>%
    dplyr::select(mer_id, midpoint_abs_linear, q_value, feature) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(group = "lncRNA")

mer_distro_by_mirna <- mirna_mers %>%
    dplyr::mutate(feature = case_when(!is.na(mirna) ~ "miRNA")) %>%
    dplyr::filter(!is.na(mirna)) %>%
    dplyr::select(mer_id, midpoint_abs_linear, q_value, feature) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(group = "miRNA")

mer_distro_by_lymphocyte <- lymph_mers_overlap %>%
    dplyr::mutate(feature = "Normal Lymphocytes") %>%
    dplyr::select(mer_id, midpoint_abs_linear, q_value, feature) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(group = "Normal Lymphocytes")

mer_distro_by_feature <- rbind(mer_distro_by_protein_coding, mer_distro_by_enhancer,
    mer_distro_by_promoter, mer_distro_by_lncrna, mer_distro_by_mirna, mer_distro_by_lymphocyte)

mer_distro_by_feature$group <- factor(mer_distro_by_feature$group, levels = c("Protein-coding",
    "Enhancers", "Promoters", "lncRNA", "miRNA", "Normal Lymphocytes"))

    "Normal Lymphocytes"))

    facet_wrap(facets = ~group, ncol = 1) + ggplot2::labs(y = expression("-log"[10] *
    "(" * italic("q-value") * ")")) + ggplot2::scale_x_continuous(limits = c(1, last(linearized_hg38_end_breaks[1:22])),
    labels = names(hg_seqlengths()[1:22]), breaks = linearized_hg38_midpoint_breaks[1:22],
    expand = c(0.01, 0.01)) + ggplot2::scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0.01, 0.01)) +
    ggplot2::scale_color_manual(name = "Genomic\nFeature", values = paletteer_d("ggthemes::stata_s2color")[1:9]) +
    ggplot2::theme(axis.title.y = element_text(family = "sans", size = 15), axis.title.x = element_blank(),
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 0.95, size = 11), legend.position = "top",
```

Analyze MM genomes for clustered mutation scars
Collect all clustered mutations per sample and calculate 

::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code .code-overflow-wrap  code-fold="true"}
# Read in the total set of clustered vs non-clustered mutations
sigpro_clusters_all_clustered <- fread(file = paste0(base_data_path, "clusteredMutationsAnalysis/clusters_clustered_vaf.txt"),
    header = T, sep = "\t")

sigpro_clusters_non_clustered <- fread(file = paste0(base_data_path, "clusteredMutationsAnalysis/clusters_nonClustered.txt"),
    header = F, sep = "\t", skip = 1, col.names = colnames(sigpro_clusters_all_clustered)[-14])

# Extract the high-quality samples
hq_sigpro_clusters_all_clustered <- sigpro_clusters_all_clustered %>%
    dplyr::mutate(patient = str_split(string = samples, pattern = "_vs_", simplify = T)[,
        2], sample = str_split(string = samples, pattern = "_vs_", simplify = T)[,
        1]) %>%
    dplyr::filter(sample %in% unique(hq_dataset_metadata$sample))

hq_sigpro_clusters_non_clustered <- sigpro_clusters_non_clustered %>%
    dplyr::mutate(patient = str_split(string = samples, pattern = "_vs_", simplify = T)[,
        2], sample = str_split(string = samples, pattern = "_vs_", simplify = T)[,
        1]) %>%
    dplyr::filter(sample %in% unique(hq_dataset_metadata$sample))

# Calculate the clustered mutational burden and percentage clustered vs non
# clustered
hq_clustered_metrics <- hq_sigpro_clusters_all_clustered %>%
    dplyr::group_by(sample) %>%
    dplyr::count() %>%
    dplyr::ungroup() %>%
    dplyr::rename(tot_clustered = n) %>%
    dplyr::left_join(y = hq_sigpro_clusters_non_clustered %>%
        dplyr::group_by(sample) %>%
        dplyr::count() %>%
        dplyr::ungroup() %>%
        dplyr::rename(tot_non_clustered = n), by = "sample") %>%
    dplyr::mutate(clustered_tmb = tot_clustered/2800, clustered_pct = round((tot_clustered/(tot_clustered +
        tot_non_clustered)) * 100, digits = 1)) %>%
    dplyr::left_join(y = hq_dataset_metadata %>%
        dplyr::select(patient, sample, disease_stage, age, SBS5, SBS9), by = "sample") %>%
    dplyr::mutate(age_group = case_when(age < 65 ~ "Younger than 65", age >= 65 ~
        "65 and older"))

# TODO: work on this snake plot
hq_clustered_metrics$sample <- factor(hq_clustered_metrics$sample, levels = hq_clustered_metrics %>%
    dplyr::arrange(clustered_pct) %>%
    dplyr::group_by(disease_stage) %>%
    dplyr::arrange(disease_stage) %>%
    dplyr::ungroup() %>%
    dplyr::select(sample) %>%
    purrr::as_vector() %>%
    as.character())

# Calculate some median All hq_clustered_metrics %>% dplyr::group_by(patient)
# %>% dplyr::reframe('median_pct_clustered' = median(clustered_pct))

# by Dx
clustered_muts_by_dx_medians <- hq_clustered_metrics %>%
    dplyr::group_by(disease_stage) %>%
    dplyr::reframe(median_pct_clustered = median(clustered_pct))

# find point for median bar
clustered_muts_by_dx_medians_x <- hq_clustered_metrics %>%
    dplyr::group_by(disease_stage) %>%
    dplyr::count() %>%
    dplyr::mutate(group_midpoint = n/2)

clustered_muts_by_dx_medians_x$final_midpoint <- clustered_muts_by_dx_medians_x$group_midpoint +
    c(0, clustered_muts_by_dx_medians_x$n[1], sum(clustered_muts_by_dx_medians_x$n[c(1:2)]),
        sum(clustered_muts_by_dx_medians_x$n[c(1:3)]))

clustered_muts_by_dx_medians_x$group_range <- c(clustered_muts_by_dx_medians_x$n[1],
    sum(clustered_muts_by_dx_medians_x$n[c(1:2)]), sum(clustered_muts_by_dx_medians_x$n[c(1:3)]),
    sum(clustered_muts_by_dx_medians_x$n[c(1:4)]))

clustered_pct_by_dx_snake_plot <- ggplot(hq_clustered_metrics) + geom_point(aes(x = sample,
    y = clustered_pct, color = disease_stage), size = 3.5) + labs(x = "Samples",
    y = "Mutations in\nclustered events (%)") + scale_color_manual(name = "Disease\nStage",
    values = disease_stage_colors) + scale_x_discrete(expand = c(0.01, 0.01)) + scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0.01,
    0.01), limits = c(0, 38)) + theme(axis.ticks.x = element_blank(), axis.text.x = element_blank(),
    panel.border = element_rect(fill = "transparent")) + ggplot2::annotate(geom = "segment",
    x = clustered_muts_by_dx_medians_x$final_midpoint - 7, xend = clustered_muts_by_dx_medians_x$final_midpoint +
        7, y = clustered_muts_by_dx_medians$median_pct_clustered, yend = clustered_muts_by_dx_medians$median_pct_clustered,
    color = "red", linewidth = 2.25) + ggplot2::annotate(geom = "rect", xmin = c(0,
    clustered_muts_by_dx_medians_x$group_range[-4]), xmax = clustered_muts_by_dx_medians_x$group_range,
    ymin = 0, ymax = 38, alpha = 0.3, fill = c("gray75", "gray35", "gray75", "gray35"))

# Read in each set of clustered event type
cluster_subclass <- c("DBS", "MBS", "Omikli", "KataegisProcessiveKetones", "KataegisNonProcessive",
    "KataegisProcessiveStrong", "KataegisProcessivePyramidine", "Other")
cluster_mainclass <- c("DBS", "MBS", "Omikli", "Kataegis", "Kataegis", "Kataegis",
    "Kataegis", "Other")
sigpro_clusters_output <- data.table()
for (i in 1:length(cluster_subclass)) {
    cluster_subclass_data <- fread(file = paste0(base_data_path, "clusteredMutationsAnalysis/subclasses/",
        list.files(path = paste0(base_data_path, "clusteredMutationsAnalysis/subclasses"),
            pattern = paste0("*", cluster_subclass[i], ".txt"))), fill = T)[, c(1:15)] %>%
        dplyr::mutate(seqnames = paste0("chr", chr), sample = str_split(string = samples,
            pattern = "_vs_", simplify = T)[, 1], patient = str_split(string = samples,
            pattern = "_vs_", simplify = T)[, 2], subclass = cluster_subclass[i],
            mainclass = cluster_mainclass[i]) %>%
        dplyr::select(seqnames, start, end, ref, alt, patient, sample, group, IMD,
            `VAF/CCF`, subclass, mainclass)

    sigpro_clusters_output <- rrbind(sigpro_clusters_output, cluster_subclass_data)
}

# Tally the composition of each subclass of clustered event
per_patient_cluster_composition <- sigpro_clusters_output %>%
    dplyr::filter(sample %in% unique(hq_dataset_metadata$sample)) %>%
    dplyr::left_join(y = hq_clustered_metrics, by = c("patient", "sample")) %>%
    dplyr::group_by(patient, sample, disease_stage, mainclass) %>%
    dplyr::count(name = "n_per_class") %>%
    dplyr::ungroup() %>%
    dplyr::group_by(patient, sample, disease_stage) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(sum_clustered_muts = sum(n_per_class)) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(pct_per_class = round(n_per_class/sum_clustered_muts * 100, digits = 2)) %>%
    dplyr::ungroup()

# Make same organization as snake plot
per_patient_cluster_composition$sample <- factor(per_patient_cluster_composition$sample,
    levels = hq_clustered_metrics$sample)
per_patient_cluster_composition$mainclass <- factor(per_patient_cluster_composition$mainclass,
    levels = c("DBS", "MBS", "Omikli", "Kataegis", "Other"))

clustered_events_colors <- c("#000000", "#E69F00", "#56B4E9", "#009E73", "#D55E00",
    "#767F8B")

clustered_muts_composition_barplot <- ggplot(per_patient_cluster_composition) + geom_col(aes(x = sample,
    y = pct_per_class, fill = mainclass), width = 1) + labs(x = "Samples", y = "Relative\nContribution (%)") +
    scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0, 0)) + scale_x_discrete(expand = c(0.01, 0.01)) +
    scale_fill_manual(name = "Clustered\nEvent\nSubclass", values = clustered_events_colors[2:6]) +
    theme(panel.border = element_rect(fill = "transparent"), axis.text.x = element_blank(),
        axis.ticks.x = element_blank())

# Calculate some median
per_class_median_by_dx <- per_patient_cluster_composition %>%
    dplyr::group_by(disease_stage, mainclass) %>%
    dplyr::reframe(sum_n_per_class_dx = sum(n_per_class)) %>%
    dplyr::left_join(y = per_patient_cluster_composition %>%
        dplyr::select(disease_stage, patient, sample, sum_clustered_muts) %>%
        unique() %>%
        dplyr::group_by(disease_stage) %>%
        dplyr::reframe(sum_clustered_dx = sum(sum_clustered_muts)), by = "disease_stage") %>%
```
