Identify the most recurrent regions of clustered mutations

::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code .code-overflow-wrap  code-fold="true"}
# To find the consensus regions of clustered mutations, first need to define
# the consensus per main class of clustered event DBS, MBS, Omikli, Kataegis,
# Other Loop through the non-Ig clustered events by main class and find
# consensus regions
consensus_clustered_event_regions <- data.table()
for (i in 1:length(c("Omikli", "Kataegis", "Other"))) {
    class_specific_clustered_events <- uniq_hq_clustered_non_ig_regions_dt %>%
        dplyr::filter(mainclass == c("Omikli", "Kataegis", "Other")[i]) %>%
        dplyr::rename(chrom = seqnames, sample_name = patient) %>%
        dplyr::select(chrom, start, end, sample_name)

    # peakCombiner workflow 1. Input preparation, computes center genomic
    # coordinate per chromosome
    prep_class_specific_clustered_events <- peakCombiner::prepare_input_regions(data = class_specific_clustered_events,
        show_messages = F)

    # 2. Add small flank to each region using defined value or default of
    # median span of all input will use half of the median of all sizes
    prep_class_specific_clustered_events_expand <- peakCombiner::center_expand_regions(data = prep_class_specific_clustered_events,
        expand_by = median(GenomicRanges::width(dt2gr(prep_class_specific_clustered_events))) *
            0.25, show_messages = F)

    # 3. Consensus peak calling found_in_samples = threshold of minimumn sample
    # overlap for outpuut combined_center = nearest - the 'center' of the input
    # region that is closest to mean of the 'center's of all overlapping input
    # regions
    consensus_class_specific_clustered_events <- peakCombiner::combine_regions(data = prep_class_specific_clustered_events_expand,
        found_in_samples = 2, combined_center = "nearest", annotate_with_input_names = T,
        combined_sample_name = c("Omikli", "Kataegis", "Other")[i], show_messages = F)

    # Add to the final combined set of peaks
    consensus_clustered_event_regions <- rrbind(consensus_clustered_event_regions,
        consensus_class_specific_clustered_events)
}
## Warning in dt2gr(prep_class_specific_clustered_events): Warning: Coercing to
## GRanges via non-standard columns

## GRanges via non-standard columns
## Warning in dt2gr(prep_class_specific_clustered_events): Warning: Coercing to
## GRanges via non-standard columns

# Tabulate the consensus set of each class
consensus_clustered_event_regions_dt <- consensus_clustered_event_regions %>%
    dplyr::mutate(patient_count = stringr::str_count(string = input_names, pattern = ";") +
        1, pct_patients = round(patient_count/294 * 100, digits = 1)) %>%
    dplyr::rename(event_id = name, event_type = sample_name, patient_events_at_region = input_names) %>%
    dplyr::select(chrom, start, end, event_type, event_id, patient_events_at_region,
        patient_count, pct_patients)
```

Link the clustered mutation spectrum to mutational signatures in E/SE-MERs

::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code .code-overflow-wrap  code-fold="true"}
# First, given that there are now duplicated mutations per MER (from different
# samples/patients etc.), remove them here
e_se_mer_sig_muts <- muts_per_grouped_mer_for_plot[VARIANT_CLASS == "SNV", .(tissue,
    seqnames, start, Reference_Allele, Tumor_Seq_Allele2)]
e_se_mer_sig_muts <- e_se_mer_sig_muts[!duplicated(e_se_mer_sig_muts)]

# Now prepare the data for mmsig Correct the column names for input to mmsig
colnames(e_se_mer_sig_muts) <- c("sample", "chr", "pos", "ref", "alt")

# ALL GCB
gcb_e_se_mer_sig_96 <- deconstructSigs::mut.to.sigs.input(mut.ref = e_se_mer_sig_muts %>%
    dplyr::filter(sample == "GCB"), sample.id = "sample", chr = "chr", pos = "pos",
    ref = "ref", alt = "alt", bsg = BSgenome.Hsapiens.UCSC.hg38)

# Dual GCB/PC
dual_e_se_mer_sig_96 <- deconstructSigs::mut.to.sigs.input(mut.ref = e_se_mer_sig_muts %>%
    dplyr::filter(sample == "GCB,PC"), sample.id = "sample", chr = "chr", pos = "pos",
    ref = "ref", alt = "alt", bsg = BSgenome.Hsapiens.UCSC.hg38)

# ALL PC
pc_e_se_mer_sig_96 <- deconstructSigs::mut.to.sigs.input(mut.ref = e_se_mer_sig_muts %>%
    dplyr::filter(sample == "PC"), sample.id = "sample", chr = "chr", pos = "pos",
    ref = "ref", alt = "alt", bsg = BSgenome.Hsapiens.UCSC.hg38)


# Generate a 96 class SBS profile plot_sbs96(grouped_mer_sig_96)
gcb_e_se_mer_96class_plot <- plot_sbs96(gcb_e_se_mer_sig_96)
dual_e_se_mer_96class_plot <- plot_sbs96(dual_e_se_mer_sig_96)
pc_e_se_mer_96class_plot <- plot_sbs96(pc_e_se_mer_sig_96)

# Translate data for use in mmsig GCB
gcb_e_se_mer_sig_96$Total <- rowSums(gcb_e_se_mer_sig_96)
gcb_e_se_mer_sig_96_transpose <- as.data.frame.matrix(t(gcb_e_se_mer_sig_96))

# Dual
dual_e_se_mer_sig_96$Total <- rowSums(dual_e_se_mer_sig_96)
dual_e_se_mer_sig_96_transpose <- as.data.frame.matrix(t(dual_e_se_mer_sig_96))

# PC
pc_e_se_mer_sig_96$Total <- rowSums(pc_e_se_mer_sig_96)
pc_e_se_mer_sig_96_transpose <- as.data.frame.matrix(t(pc_e_se_mer_sig_96))


# Fit the signatures GCB
gcb_e_se_mer_sig_96_out <- mm_fit_signatures(muts.input = gcb_e_se_mer_sig_96_transpose,
    sig.input = sig_ref_cosmic_v3_2_hg38, input.format = "classes", sample.sigt.profs = NULL,
    strandbias = FALSE, bootstrap = TRUE, iterations = 10, genome = "hg38", dbg = FALSE,
    force_include = NULL)
## 
  |                                                                            
  |                                                                      |   0%
  |                                                                            
  |======================================================================| 100%

gcb_e_se_mer_sig_t <- reshape2::melt(data = gcb_e_se_mer_sig_96_out$estimate %>%
    tibble::rownames_to_column(var = "sample") %>%
    dplyr::select(-mutations) %>%
    as.data.table(), id.vars = "sample", variable.name = "signature", value.name = "rel_contrib")

gcb_e_se_mer_sig_t$signature <- factor(gcb_e_se_mer_sig_t$signature, levels = c("SBS1",
    "SBSblood", "SBS8", "SBS9", "SBS84", "SBS18", "SBS99", "SBS2", "SBS13"))

gcb_e_se_mmsig_barplot <- ggplot(gcb_e_se_mer_sig_t, aes(x = sample, y = rel_contrib,
    fill = signature)) + geom_col(width = 1) + scale_fill_manual(name = "Signature",
    values = mmsig_colors) + scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0, 0)) + labs(x = "E/SE-MER Group",
    y = "Relative\nContribution") + theme(legend.position = "right", axis.text.x = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks.x = element_blank(), panel.border = element_rect(fill = "transparent"))

message("AID-associated SBS in GCB E/SE-MERs: ")
## AID-associated SBS in GCB E/SE-MERs:
gcb_e_se_mer_sig_t %>%
    dplyr::filter(signature %in% c("SBS9", "SBS84")) %>%
    dplyr::reframe(sum = sum(rel_contrib))
##         sum
## 1 0.6429508

# Dual
```

Evaluate the overlap and composition of SV BPs to hotspots

::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code .code-overflow-wrap  code-fold="true"}
############# SV HOTSPOTS

# Calculate the percentage of patients with SVs within the hotspot
commpass_sv_hotspots_hg38_gr$pct_patients_with_SV.hg19 <- round(commpass_sv_hotspots_hg38_gr$patients_with_SV/752 *
    100, digits = 2)

# Calculate the overlap of SV BPs with hotspots
sv_hotspot_overlap <- gr.findoverlaps(query = hq_total_sv_bp_gr, subject = commpass_sv_hotspots_hg38_gr,
    qcol = c("class", "patient", "sample", "sv_id", "bp_order"), scol = "hotspot.id.hg19")
sv_hotspot_overlap_dt <- gr2dt(sv_hotspot_overlap)

message("Total occupancy of SV hotspots: ", length(unique(sv_hotspot_overlap$hotspot.id.hg19)))
## Total occupancy of SV hotspots: 69
message("Fraction of all SV hotspots are occupied: ", length(unique(sv_hotspot_overlap$hotspot.id.hg19))/length(commpass_sv_hotspots_hg38_gr %Q%
    (seqnames != "chrX")))
## Fraction of all SV hotspots are occupied: 0.985714285714286

# Hotspots with no SVs in our dataset
commpass_sv_hotspots_hg38_gr %Q% (hotspot.id.hg19 == "down_chr1_92.8Mb")
## GRanges object with 1 range and 17 metadata columns:
##       seqnames            ranges strand |  hotspot.id.hg19    length
##          <Rle>         <IRanges>  <Rle> |      <character> <integer>
##   [1]     chr1 92317444-92428443      * | down_chr1_92.8Mb    111000
##          cytoband patients_with_SV chromoplexy chromothripsis   complex
##       <character>        <integer>   <integer>      <integer> <integer>
##   [1]       p22.1                8           0              0         1
##       templated_insertion       DUP       TRA       DEL       INV hotspot_class
##                 <integer> <integer> <integer> <integer> <integer>   <character>
##   [1]                   0         0         0         5         2          LOSS
##       driver_status genes_involved references_pmid pct_patients_with_SV.hg19
##         <character>    <character>     <character>                 <numeric>
##   [1]         KNOWN           RPL5        27909306                      1.06
##   -------
##   seqinfo: 24 sequences from an unspecified genome

# Compare the pct of patients with SVs in hotspots
sv_hotspot_overlap_comp <- sv_hotspot_overlap_dt %>%
    dplyr::group_by(hotspot.id.hg19) %>%
    dplyr::select(hotspot.id.hg19, patient) %>%
    dplyr::ungroup() %>%
    dplyr::distinct() %>%
    dplyr::group_by(hotspot.id.hg19) %>%
    dplyr::count(name = "patients_with_SV") %>%
    dplyr::ungroup() %>%
    dplyr::mutate(pct_patients_with_SV = round(patients_with_SV/294 * 100, digits = 2)) %>%
    dplyr::left_join(y = gr2dt(commpass_sv_hotspots_hg38_gr) %>%
        dplyr::select(hotspot.id.hg19, pct_patients_with_SV.hg19), by = "hotspot.id.hg19") %>%
    dplyr::mutate(pct_delta = pct_patients_with_SV.hg19 - pct_patients_with_SV)

# Metrics on correlation of pct patients
message("Median difference in % of patients with SV at hotspots: ", median(abs(sv_hotspot_overlap_comp$pct_delta)))
## Median difference in % of patients with SV at hotspots: 0.83


cor_test(data = sv_hotspot_overlap_comp, vars = c("pct_patients_with_SV", "pct_patients_with_SV.hg19"))
## # A tibble: 1 × 8
##   var1                 var2     cor statistic        p conf.low conf.high method
##   <chr>                <chr>  <dbl>     <dbl>    <dbl>    <dbl>     <dbl> <chr> 
## 1 pct_patients_with_SV pct_p…  0.95      24.6 2.66e-35    0.919     0.968 Pears…

sv_hotspot_pct_corr <- ggplot(sv_hotspot_overlap_comp) + geom_point(aes(x = pct_patients_with_SV,
    y = pct_patients_with_SV.hg19), na.rm = T, size = 3, color = "#606372") + geom_smooth(aes(x = pct_patients_with_SV,
    y = pct_patients_with_SV.hg19), method = "lm", na.rm = T, formula = y ~ x, linewidth = 1.5,
    color = "thistle1", alpha = 0.75) + ggpubr::stat_regline_equation(aes(x = pct_patients_with_SV,
    y = pct_patients_with_SV.hg19), label.x = 0.5, label.y = 35, size = 4.5) + ggpubr::stat_cor(aes(x = pct_patients_with_SV,
    y = pct_patients_with_SV.hg19), label.x = 0.5, label.y = 32, size = 4.5, p.accuracy = 0.0001) +
    labs(x = "Query Dataset\nPatients with SVs at hotspots (%)", y = "CoMMpass Dataset\nPatients with SVs at hotspots (%)") +
    theme(legend.position = "top", legend.text = element_text(size = 13), panel.border = element_rect(fill = "transparent",
        linewidth = 1))

######################## COMPLEX EVENT HOTSPOTS

# Calculate the percentage of patients with SVs within the hotspot
commpass_complex_hotspots_hg38_gr$pct_patients_with_SV.hg19 <- round(commpass_complex_hotspots_hg38_gr$no.samples/752 *
    100, digits = 2)

# Calculate the overlap of SV BPs with hotspots
complex_hotspot_overlap <- gr.findoverlaps(query = hq_total_sv_bp_gr, subject = commpass_complex_hotspots_hg38_gr,
    qcol = c("class", "patient", "sample", "sv_id", "bp_order"), scol = "complex.hotspot.id.hg19")
complex_hotspot_overlap_dt <- gr2dt(complex_hotspot_overlap)

message("Total occupancy of Complex hotspots: ", length(unique(complex_hotspot_overlap$complex.hotspot.id.hg19)))
## Total occupancy of Complex hotspots: 33
message("Fraction of all Complex hotspots are occupied: ", length(unique(complex_hotspot_overlap$complex.hotspot.id.hg19))/length(commpass_complex_hotspots_hg38_gr))
## Fraction of all Complex hotspots are occupied: 1

# Compare the pct of patients with SVs in hotspots
complex_hotspot_overlap_comp <- complex_hotspot_overlap_dt %>%
    dplyr::group_by(complex.hotspot.id.hg19) %>%
    dplyr::select(complex.hotspot.id.hg19, patient) %>%
    dplyr::ungroup() %>%
    dplyr::distinct() %>%
    dplyr::group_by(complex.hotspot.id.hg19) %>%
    dplyr::count(name = "patients_with_SV") %>%
    dplyr::ungroup() %>%
    dplyr::mutate(pct_patients_with_SV = round(patients_with_SV/294 * 100, digits = 2)) %>%
    dplyr::left_join(y = gr2dt(commpass_complex_hotspots_hg38_gr) %>%
        dplyr::select(complex.hotspot.id.hg19, pct_patients_with_SV.hg19), by = "complex.hotspot.id.hg19") %>%
    dplyr::mutate(pct_delta = pct_patients_with_SV.hg19 - pct_patients_with_SV)

# Metrics on correlation of pct patients
message("Median difference in % of patients at Complex hotspots: ", median(abs(complex_hotspot_overlap_comp$pct_delta)))
## Median difference in % of patients at Complex hotspots: 5.65

cor_test(data = complex_hotspot_overlap_comp, vars = c("pct_patients_with_SV", "pct_patients_with_SV.hg19"))
## # A tibble: 1 × 8
##   var1                 var2      cor statistic       p conf.low conf.high method
##   <chr>                <chr>   <dbl>     <dbl>   <dbl>    <dbl>     <dbl> <chr> 
## 1 pct_patients_with_SV pct_pa…  0.59      4.02 3.44e-4    0.303     0.773 Pears…

complex_hotspot_pct_corr <- ggplot(complex_hotspot_overlap_comp) + geom_point(aes(x = pct_patients_with_SV,
    y = pct_patients_with_SV.hg19), na.rm = T, size = 3, color = "#B2AD8F") + geom_smooth(aes(x = pct_patients_with_SV,
    y = pct_patients_with_SV.hg19), method = "lm", na.rm = T, formula = y ~ x, linewidth = 1.5,
    color = "thistle1", alpha = 0.75) + ggpubr::stat_regline_equation(aes(x = pct_patients_with_SV,
    y = pct_patients_with_SV.hg19), label.x = 3, label.y = 17, size = 4.5) + ggpubr::stat_cor(aes(x = pct_patients_with_SV,
    y = pct_patients_with_SV.hg19), label.x = 3, label.y = 15, size = 4.5, p.accuracy = 0.0001) +
    labs(x = "Query Dataset\nPatients with Complex SVs at hotspots (%)", y = "CoMMpass Dataset\nPatients with Complex SVs at hotspots (%)") +
    theme(legend.position = "top", legend.text = element_text(size = 13), panel.border = element_rect(fill = "transparent",
        linewidth = 1))
```

Assess the proximity relationship between kataegis and SV breakpoints at complex event hotspots

::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code .code-overflow-wrap  code-fold="true"}
# Proximity profile of kataegis and omikli mutations to SV BPs will use
# non-clustered mutations as the background Correct for Ig region kataegis and
# SVs by removing them

# hq_sigpro_clusters_muts_gr %Q% (mainclass == 'Kataegis' & !seqnames %in%
# c('chrX','chrY')) %_% ig_region_mask_bed

# gr_refactor_seqs(dt2gr(hq_total_sv_bp_dt)) %_% ig_region_mask_bed

kataegis_sv_prox_dist <- GenomicDistributions::calcFeatureDist(query = hq_sigpro_clusters_muts_gr %Q%
    (mainclass == "Kataegis" & !seqnames %in% c("chrX", "chrY")) %_% ig_region_mask_bed,
    features = gr_refactor_seqs(dt2gr(hq_total_sv_bp_dt)) %_% ig_region_mask_bed)

omikli_sv_prox_dist <- GenomicDistributions::calcFeatureDist(query = hq_sigpro_clusters_muts_gr %Q%
    (mainclass == "Omikli" & !seqnames %in% c("chrX", "chrY")) %_% ig_region_mask_bed,
    features = gr_refactor_seqs(dt2gr(hq_total_sv_bp_dt)) %_% ig_region_mask_bed)

non_clustered_background_dist <- GenomicDistributions::calcFeatureDist(query = hq_sigpro_clusters_non_clustered_gr %Q%
    (!seqnames %in% c("chrX", "chrY")) %_% ig_region_mask_bed, features = gr_refactor_seqs(dt2gr(hq_total_sv_bp_dt)) %_%
    ig_region_mask_bed)

# Plot proximity distros
proxi_distro_to_svs <- list(Kataegis = kataegis_sv_prox_dist, Omikli = omikli_sv_prox_dist,
    `Non-Clustered` = non_clustered_background_dist)
proxi_distro_to_svs_plot <- GenomicDistributions::plotFeatureDist(dists = proxi_distro_to_svs,
    size = 100000, nbins = 40) + scale_color_manual(values = c("#D55E00", "#009E73",
    "#000000")) + scale_fill_manual(values = c("#D55E00", "#009E73", "#000000")) +
    labs(title = NULL, x = "Distance to proximal SV breakpoint") + theme(strip.text = element_text(size = 14),
    panel.border = element_rect(fill = "transparent"), axis.text.x = element_text(size = 12),
    axis.ticks.x = element_blank(), axis.text.y = element_text(size = 12), legend.position = "none",
    plot.margin = unit(c(6, 6, 6, 6), "mm"), panel.spacing.x = unit(3, "lines"))
## Scale for x is already present.
## Adding another scale for x, which will replace the existing scale.
# export 5 x 10 landscape

# quantify the distances and look at 5 Kb window around SV BPs
kataegis_binned_dists_around_bp <- GenomicDistributions:::cutDists(dists = kataegis_sv_prox_dist,
    size = 100000, nbins = 40)
omikli_binned_dists_around_bp <- GenomicDistributions:::cutDists(dists = omikli_sv_prox_dist,
    size = 100000, nbins = 40)
nonclustered_binned_dists_around_bp <- GenomicDistributions:::cutDists(dists = non_clustered_background_dist,
    size = 100000, nbins = 40)

kataegis_5k_window_around_bp <- kataegis_binned_dists_around_bp %>%
    dplyr::filter(cuts %in% c("-2,500 to 0", "0 to 2,500")) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(pct = Freq/sum(!is.na(kataegis_sv_prox_dist)))
message("Percentage of kataegis muts in 5 Kb of SV BP:")
## Percentage of kataegis muts in 5 Kb of SV BP:
round(sum(kataegis_5k_window_around_bp$pct) * 100, digits = 1)
## [1] 27.2

omikli_5k_window_around_bp <- omikli_binned_dists_around_bp %>%
    dplyr::filter(cuts %in% c("-2,500 to 0", "0 to 2,500")) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(pct = Freq/sum(!is.na(omikli_sv_prox_dist)))
message("Percentage of omikli muts in 5 Kb of SV BP:")
## Percentage of omikli muts in 5 Kb of SV BP:
round(sum(omikli_5k_window_around_bp$pct) * 100, digits = 1)
## [1] 5.4

nonclustered_5k_window_around_bp <- nonclustered_binned_dists_around_bp %>%
    dplyr::filter(cuts %in% c("-2,500 to 0", "0 to 2,500")) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(pct = Freq/sum(!is.na(non_clustered_background_dist)))
message("Percentage of non-clustered muts in 5 Kb of SV BP:")
## Percentage of non-clustered muts in 5 Kb of SV BP:
round(sum(nonclustered_5k_window_around_bp$pct) * 100, digits = 1)
## [1] 1.9

# Fisher test of 5kb vs other distances
kataegis_5k_window_fish_tbl <- data.table(Kataegis = c(sum(kataegis_5k_window_around_bp$Freq),
    kataegis_binned_dists_around_bp %>%
        dplyr::filter(!cuts %in% c("-2,500 to 0", "0 to 2,500")) %>%
        dplyr::select(Freq) %>%
        purrr::as_vector() %>%
        sum()), `Non-Clustered` = c(sum(nonclustered_5k_window_around_bp$Freq), nonclustered_binned_dists_around_bp %>%
    dplyr::filter(!cuts %in% c("-2,500 to 0", "0 to 2,500")) %>%
    dplyr::select(Freq) %>%
    purrr::as_vector() %>%
    sum()))
rownames(kataegis_5k_window_fish_tbl) <- c("+/- 2.5 Kb", "Beyond +/- 2.5 Kb")
glance(fisher.test(x = kataegis_5k_window_fish_tbl))
## # A tibble: 1 × 6
##   estimate p.value conf.low conf.high method                         alternative
##      <dbl>   <dbl>    <dbl>     <dbl> <chr>                          <chr>      
## 1     19.0       0     18.1      19.9 Fisher's Exact Test for Count… two.sided


omikli_5k_window_fish_tbl <- data.table(Omikli = c(sum(omikli_5k_window_around_bp$Freq),
    omikli_binned_dists_around_bp %>%
        dplyr::filter(!cuts %in% c("-2,500 to 0", "0 to 2,500")) %>%
        dplyr::select(Freq) %>%
        purrr::as_vector() %>%
        sum()), `Non-Clustered` = c(sum(nonclustered_5k_window_around_bp$Freq), nonclustered_binned_dists_around_bp %>%
    dplyr::filter(!cuts %in% c("-2,500 to 0", "0 to 2,500")) %>%
    dplyr::select(Freq) %>%
    purrr::as_vector() %>%
    sum()))
rownames(omikli_5k_window_fish_tbl) <- c("+/- 2.5 Kb", "Beyond +/- 2.5 Kb")
glance(fisher.test(x = omikli_5k_window_fish_tbl))
## # A tibble: 1 × 6
##   estimate p.value conf.low conf.high method                         alternative
##      <dbl>   <dbl>    <dbl>     <dbl> <chr>                          <chr>      
## 1     2.91       0     2.81      3.02 Fisher's Exact Test for Count… two.sided
```

Pair the query proximal transloation partner junction with the distal (other chromosomes) partner for analysis

::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code .code-overflow-wrap  code-fold="true"}
# Grab all distal and proximal interchromosomal breakpoints
interchrom_juncs <- hq_total_sv_bp_dt %>%
    dplyr::filter(class == "TRA")
interchrom_juncs$nearest_gene <- genes$gene_name[nearest(x = gr_refactor_seqs(dt2gr(interchrom_juncs)),
    subject = gr.stripstrand(genes))]

# Need to remove all the MM canonical TRX BPs to extract true signal first find
# all BP that fall within the expected IGH range + 50 kbp pad
igh_trx_expected_range <- parse.gr("chr14:105586858-106879851") + 25000

igh_trx_bp <- gr.findoverlaps(query = gr_refactor_seqs(dt2gr(interchrom_juncs)),
    subject = igh_trx_expected_range, qcol = c("patient", "sample", "sv_id", "nearest_gene"))

# now that we have the IDs of any BP that occurs in IGH range, find all
# partners
igh_trx_partners <- interchrom_juncs %>%
    dplyr::filter(sv_id %in% igh_trx_bp$sv_id) %>%
    dplyr::filter(seqnames != "chr14")

# Next, find only those that are canonical partners
mm_igh_canon_expected_range <- read_bed_file(bed_file_path = paste0(base_data_path,
    "canonicalSubtypePartnerTranslocation.hg38.bed"), has_header = F)
## Warning in gUtils::dt2gr(input_dt, seqlengths = gUtils::hg_seqlengths()[1:24]):
## Warning: Coercing to GRanges via non-standard columns

# grab the SV IDs as these will be excluded from counting
mm_igh_canon_partners <- gr.findoverlaps(query = gr_refactor_seqs(dt2gr(igh_trx_partners)),
    subject = mm_igh_canon_expected_range, qcol = c("patient", "sample", "sv_id",
        "nearest_gene"))

canon_igh_trx_mask <- interchrom_juncs[interchrom_juncs$sv_id %in% mm_igh_canon_partners$sv_id]

# Mask the canonical IGH TRX from input
interchrom_juncs_igh_masked <- dplyr::setdiff(x = interchrom_juncs, y = canon_igh_trx_mask)

# Finally remove any IGK or IGL TRX
other_ig_region_mask <- GenomicRanges::reduce(ig_region_mask_bed + 25000) %Q% (seqnames %in%
    c("chr2", "chr22"))

other_ig_trx_bp <- gr.findoverlaps(query = gr_refactor_seqs(dt2gr(interchrom_juncs)),
    subject = other_ig_region_mask, qcol = c("patient", "sample", "sv_id", "nearest_gene"))

# now that we have the IDs of any BP that occurs in IGK/IGL range, find all
# partners
other_ig_trx_partners <- interchrom_juncs %>%
    dplyr::filter(sv_id %in% other_ig_trx_bp$sv_id) %>%
    dplyr::filter(!seqnames %in% c("chr2", "chr22"))

other_ig_trx_mask <- interchrom_juncs[interchrom_juncs$sv_id %in% other_ig_trx_partners$sv_id]

interchrom_juncs_fully_masked <- dplyr::setdiff(x = interchrom_juncs_igh_masked,
    y = other_ig_trx_mask)
```

```{.r .cell-code .code-overflow-wrap  code-fold="true"}
######################################################### RUN DENSITY CALC

bcl6_mer_sv_bp_control <- e_se_mers %>%
    dplyr::filter(mer_gene_label == "BCL6") %>%
    dplyr::select(mer_id) %>%
    purrr::as_vector()
bcl6_control_sv_bp_dists <- proximal_bp_dist(sv_bp_gr = interchrom_juncs_igh_masked_gr,
    gr_of_interest = dt2gr(e_se_mers[mer_id == bcl6_mer_sv_bp_control[1]]), proximal_dist_threshold = 500000)
## Setting parallel cores to 1 ...
## Setting parallel cores to 1 ...
bcl6_control_sv_bp_dens <- window_density(data_to_count = bcl6_control_sv_bp_dists,
    window_size = 500000 * 2, bin_size = 1000)
bcl6_control_sv_bp_dens$mer <- "BCL6"

bcl6_control_sv_bp_dens$scaled_count <- bcl6_control_sv_bp_dens$count * (1 + (1/sqrt(1)))

# FULL MER set loop through all mm_mer_dist <- data.table() for(i in
# 1:length(mers$mer_id)) { distances_to_add <- proximal_bp_dist(sv_bp_gr =
# gr_refactor_seqs(dt2gr(interchrom_juncs)), gr_of_interest = dt2gr(mers[mer_id
# == mers$mer_id[i]]), proximal_dist_threshold = 1500000) mm_mer_dist <-
# gUtils::rrbind(mm_mer_dist, distances_to_add) } mm_mer_dens <-
# window_density(data_to_count = mm_mer_dist, window_size = 10e6, bin_size =
# 1e4) mm_mer_dens$mer <- 'MM'

# GC/DLBCL MER set need to exclude E/SE-MER with overlapping SNV/InDel MERs so
# same BP aren't counted twice Any E/SE-MERs within 500 kb of each other are
# combined gr.dist(gr1 = dt_to_gr(e_se_mers %>% dplyr::filter(tissue ==
# 'GCB'))) / 5e5

gcb_mer_sv_bp_set <- fread(file = paste0(base_data_path, "gcbMersForTrxDensity.txt"),
    sep = "\t", header = T)

# gcb_mer_sv_bp_set <- e_se_mers %>% dplyr::filter(tissue == 'GCB') %>%
# dplyr::select(mer_id) %>% dplyr::filter(!mer_id %in%
# c('mer2','mer66','mer159','mer11','mer39','mer215', 'mer18','mer22')) %>% #
# BCL6, RPIA, LPP, ZCCHC7/PAX5 overlap purrr::as_vector()

gcb_mer_sv_bp_dists <- data.table()
for (i in 1:nrow(gcb_mer_sv_bp_set)) {

# Put some stats on the enrichment of BPs in PC MERs vs. simulated Non-MERs To
# do this, will emulate the process done in SigProfilerTopography 1. Calculate
# the mean of the loess density of SV BP for real data (i.e. PC MERs) 2.
# Calculate the mean of the loess density of simulated non-MER sets
# (iterations=10) 3. Z-test one mean (real) with means of n simulations
loess_dens_pc_bp <- loess(data = bp_density_for_plot %>%
    dplyr::filter(mer == "PC"), formula = scaled_count ~ midpoint_for_plot, span = 0.17)
real_mean_loess_dens_pc_mer_bp <- mean(loess_dens_pc_bp$fitted)

# <- loess(data = sim_non_mer_trx_bp_dens, formula = scaled_count ~
# midpoint_for_plot, span = 0.17) sim_mean_loess_trx_dens_non_mer <-
# rrbind(sim_mean_loess_trx_dens_non_mer, data.table('simulation' = i,
# 'mean_loess_density' = mean(sim_non_mer_trx_bp_loess_density$fitted))) }

# saveRDS(object = sim_mean_loess_trx_dens_non_mer, file =
# paste0(base_data_path, 'nonMerSimulationTrxLoessDensity.rds'))
sim_mean_loess_trx_dens_non_mer <- readRDS(file = paste0(base_data_path, "nonMerSimulationTrxLoessDensity.rds"))

###### From SigProfilerTopography ########## Author: Burcak Otlu Always ztest
###### one sample or two_sample?  I decided to use one sample because for
###### simulations I will get vertical vector and average of that vertical
###### vector must be equal to avg_simulated_signal, there is a way to self
###### verification Comparing one mean with means of n simulations gives a more
###### realistic p-value.  In case of comparison of two samples, ztest and
###### ttest gives either 0 or very low p-values.  zstat, pvalue =
###### ztest(expectedValues, value=observedValue) results in very small
###### p-values therefore we are not calling in this way.  if there is only one
###### simulation mean in simulationsHorizontalMeans, then pvalue is nan

# also use BOTH, GCB group and BCL6 as comparative
loess_dens_dual_bp <- loess(data = bp_density_for_plot %>%
    dplyr::filter(mer == "GCB,PC"), formula = scaled_count ~ midpoint_for_plot, span = 0.17)
real_mean_loess_dens_dual_mer_bp <- mean(loess_dens_dual_bp$fitted)

    dplyr::filter(mer == "GCB"), formula = scaled_count ~ midpoint_for_plot, span = 0.17)

    dplyr::filter(mer == "BCL6"), formula = scaled_count ~ midpoint_for_plot, span = 0.17)
real_mean_loess_dens_bcl6_mer_bp <- mean(loess_dens_bcl6_bp$fitted)

# now run Z-test of real obs mean vs simulated means

    mu = real_mean_loess_dens_pc_mer_bp, alternative = "two.sided", sigma.x = sd(sim_mean_loess_trx_dens_non_mer$mean_loess_density))

    mu = real_mean_loess_dens_dual_mer_bp, alternative = "two.sided", sigma.x = sd(sim_mean_loess_trx_dens_non_mer$mean_loess_density))

gcb_sptbo_ztest_trx_bp_density <- BSDA::z.test(x = sim_mean_loess_trx_dens_non_mer$mean_loess_density,
    mu = real_mean_loess_dens_gcb_mer_bp, alternative = "two.sided", sigma.x = sd(sim_mean_loess_trx_dens_non_mer$mean_loess_density))
# Fold change of means
message("Fold-change BP density at PC E/SE-MERs compared to simulated Non-MER background: ",
    round(real_mean_loess_dens_pc_mer_bp/mean(sim_mean_loess_trx_dens_non_mer$mean_loess_density),
```

Look at all intrachromosomal breakpoints

::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code .code-overflow-wrap  code-fold="true"}
intrachrom_juncs <- hq_total_sv_bp_dt %>%
    dplyr::filter(class != "TRA")
intrachrom_juncs$nearest_gene <- genes$gene_name[nearest(x = gr_refactor_seqs(dt2gr(intrachrom_juncs)),
    subject = gr.stripstrand(genes))]

# Convert to GR obj for assessment of overlap with MERs
intrachrom_ranges <- dt_to_gr(intrachrom_juncs)

# find E/SE-MER simple SVs by class GCB
gcb_mer_simple_sv <- data.table()
for (i in 1:nrow(gcb_mer_sv_bp_set)) {

    simple_sv_to_add <- gr.findoverlaps(query = dt_to_gr(intrachrom_juncs), subject = dt_to_gr(gcb_mer_sv_bp_set[i]),
        qcol = c("class", "support", "patient", "sample", "sv_id", "nearest_gene"),
        scol = c("mer_gene_label_overlap", "mer_id_overlap"))

    gcb_mer_simple_sv_per_mer <- data.table(mer_gene_label_overlap = rep(gcb_mer_sv_bp_set$mer_gene_label_overlap[i],
        3), mer_id_overlap = rep(gcb_mer_sv_bp_set$mer_id_overlap[i], 3), class = c("DEL",
        "INV", "DUP"), count = c(0, 0, 0))

    if (length(simple_sv_to_add) > 0) {

        if (length(simple_sv_to_add$class == "DEL") > 0) {
            gcb_mer_simple_sv_per_mer$count[1] <- gr2dt(simple_sv_to_add) %>%
                dplyr::filter(class == "DEL") %>%
                dplyr::select(sv_id) %>%
                dplyr::n_distinct()
        }
        if (length(simple_sv_to_add$class == "INV") > 0) {
            gcb_mer_simple_sv_per_mer$count[2] <- gr2dt(simple_sv_to_add) %>%
                dplyr::filter(class == "INV") %>%
                dplyr::select(sv_id) %>%
                dplyr::n_distinct()
        }
        if (length(simple_sv_to_add$class == "DUP") > 0) {
            gcb_mer_simple_sv_per_mer$count[3] <- gr2dt(simple_sv_to_add) %>%
                dplyr::filter(class == "DUP") %>%
                dplyr::select(sv_id) %>%
                dplyr::n_distinct()
        }
    }

    gcb_mer_simple_sv <- gUtils::rrbind(gcb_mer_simple_sv, gcb_mer_simple_sv_per_mer)
}

# PC

for (i in 1:nrow(pc_mer_sv_bp_set)) {

    simple_sv_to_add <- gr.findoverlaps(query = dt_to_gr(intrachrom_juncs), subject = dt_to_gr(pc_mer_sv_bp_set[i]),
        qcol = c("class", "support", "patient", "sample", "sv_id", "nearest_gene"),
        scol = c("mer_gene_label_overlap", "mer_id_overlap"))

    pc_mer_simple_sv_per_mer <- data.table(mer_gene_label_overlap = rep(pc_mer_sv_bp_set$mer_gene_label_overlap[i],
        3), mer_id_overlap = rep(pc_mer_sv_bp_set$mer_id_overlap[i], 3), class = c("DEL",
        "INV", "DUP"), count = c(0, 0, 0))

    if (length(simple_sv_to_add) > 0) {

        if (length(simple_sv_to_add$class == "DEL") > 0) {
            pc_mer_simple_sv_per_mer$count[1] <- gr2dt(simple_sv_to_add) %>%
                dplyr::filter(class == "DEL") %>%
                dplyr::select(sv_id) %>%
                dplyr::n_distinct()
        }
        if (length(simple_sv_to_add$class == "INV") > 0) {
            pc_mer_simple_sv_per_mer$count[2] <- gr2dt(simple_sv_to_add) %>%
                dplyr::filter(class == "INV") %>%
                dplyr::select(sv_id) %>%
                dplyr::n_distinct()
        }
        if (length(simple_sv_to_add$class == "DUP") > 0) {
            pc_mer_simple_sv_per_mer$count[3] <- gr2dt(simple_sv_to_add) %>%
                dplyr::filter(class == "DUP") %>%
                dplyr::select(sv_id) %>%
                dplyr::n_distinct()
        }
    }

    pc_mer_simple_sv <- gUtils::rrbind(pc_mer_simple_sv, pc_mer_simple_sv_per_mer)
}

# Dual
dual_mer_simple_sv <- data.table()
for (i in 1:nrow(dual_mer_sv_bp_set)) {

    simple_sv_to_add <- gr.findoverlaps(query = dt_to_gr(intrachrom_juncs), subject = dt_to_gr(dual_mer_sv_bp_set[i]),
        qcol = c("class", "support", "patient", "sample", "sv_id", "nearest_gene"),
        scol = c("mer_gene_label_overlap", "mer_id_overlap"))

    dual_mer_simple_sv_per_mer <- data.table(mer_gene_label_overlap = rep(dual_mer_sv_bp_set$mer_gene_label_overlap[i],
        3), mer_id_overlap = rep(dual_mer_sv_bp_set$mer_id_overlap[i], 3), class = c("DEL",
        "INV", "DUP"), count = c(0, 0, 0))

    if (length(simple_sv_to_add) > 0) {

        if (length(simple_sv_to_add$class == "DEL") > 0) {
            dual_mer_simple_sv_per_mer$count[1] <- gr2dt(simple_sv_to_add) %>%
                dplyr::filter(class == "DEL") %>%
                dplyr::select(sv_id) %>%
                dplyr::n_distinct()
        }
        if (length(simple_sv_to_add$class == "INV") > 0) {
            dual_mer_simple_sv_per_mer$count[2] <- gr2dt(simple_sv_to_add) %>%
                dplyr::filter(class == "INV") %>%
                dplyr::select(sv_id) %>%
                dplyr::n_distinct()
        }
        if (length(simple_sv_to_add$class == "DUP") > 0) {
            dual_mer_simple_sv_per_mer$count[3] <- gr2dt(simple_sv_to_add) %>%
                dplyr::filter(class == "DUP") %>%
                dplyr::select(sv_id) %>%
                dplyr::n_distinct()
        }
    }

    dual_mer_simple_sv <- gUtils::rrbind(dual_mer_simple_sv, dual_mer_simple_sv_per_mer)
}
```

```{.r .cell-code .hidden}
# Read in normal immune cell RNA data from Monaco
rna_immune_cell_monaco <- data.table::fread(paste0(base_data_path, "rna_immune_cell_monaco.tsv.gz"),
                                            sep = "\t",
                                            header = T,
                                            stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
```

```{.r .cell-code .code-overflow-wrap  code-fold="true"}
# Read in CoMMpass MER muts

    has_header = F, additional_col_names = c("ref", "alt", "ID", "non_coding_score"))
## Warning in gUtils::dt2gr(input_dt, seqlengths = gUtils::hg_seqlengths()[1:24]):
## Warning: Coercing to GRanges via non-standard columns

commpass_mer_muts <- gr.findoverlaps(query = commpass_mer_bed, subject = mers_gr,
    qcol = c("ID", "ref", "alt"), scol = c("mer_id", "mer_gene_label"))

commpass_mer_muts_dt <- gr2dt(commpass_mer_muts) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(Matched_Norm_Sample_Barcode = str_remove(string = ID, pattern = ".*--"),
        Tumor_Sample_Barcode = str_remove(string = ID, pattern = "--.*")) %>%
    dplyr::rename(Reference_Allele = ref, Tumor_Seq_Allele2 = alt)

# CoMMpass MYC events from Even's paper
commpass_sv_hg19 <- data.table::fread(file = paste0(base_data_path, "mmrf_delly_mapq_60_filtered_baseline.txt")) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(sample_short_id = paste0(sample, "_CD138pos"))

# expected ranges from Aneta's paper
commpass_myc_txndc5_events <- commpass_sv_hg19 %>%
    dplyr::filter(chrom1 == 8 & SVTYPE == "TRA" & pos1 > 126000000 & pos1 < 131000000 &
        chrom2 == 6 & pos2 > 7600000 & pos2 < 8400000)

commpass_myc_tent5c_events <- commpass_sv_hg19 %>%
    dplyr::filter(chrom1 == 8 & SVTYPE == "TRA" & pos1 > 126000000 & pos1 < 131000000 &
        chrom2 == 1 & pos2 > 118100000 & pos2 < 118500000)

commpass_myc_igh_events <- commpass_sv_hg19 %>%
    dplyr::filter(chrom1 == 14 & SVTYPE == "TRA" & pos1 > 105000000 & pos1 < 107500000 &
        chrom2 == 8 & pos2 > 126000000 & pos2 < 131000000)

commpass_myc_igk_events <- commpass_sv_hg19 %>%
    dplyr::filter(chrom1 == 8 & SVTYPE == "TRA" & pos1 > 126000000 & pos1 < 131000000 &
        chrom2 == 2 & pos2 > 88800000 & pos2 < 90200000)

commpass_myc_igl_events <- commpass_sv_hg19 %>%
    dplyr::filter(chrom1 == 22 & SVTYPE == "TRA" & pos1 > 22500000 & pos1 < 24500000 &
        chrom2 == 8 & pos2 > 126000000 & pos2 < 131000000)

commpass_myc_other_events1 <- commpass_sv_hg19 %>%
    dplyr::filter(chrom1 == 8 & SVTYPE == "TRA" & pos1 > 126000000 & pos1 < 131000000)

commpass_myc_other_events2 <- commpass_sv_hg19 %>%
    dplyr::filter(chrom2 == 8 & SVTYPE == "TRA" & pos2 > 126000000 & pos2 < 131000000)
```

```{.r .cell-code .hidden}
commpass_wgs_rna_pairs_myc_anno <- wgs_rna_pairs %>%
  dplyr::mutate("myc_trx" = case_when(
    sample_short_id %in% unique(c(commpass_myc_txndc5_events$sample_short_id, commpass_myc_tent5c_events$sample_short_id)) ~ "TXNDC5/TENT5C",
    sample_short_id %in% unique(c(commpass_myc_igh_events$sample_short_id, commpass_myc_igk_events$sample_short_id, commpass_myc_igl_events$sample_short_id)) ~ "IGH/IGK/IGL",
    !sample_short_id %in% unique(c(commpass_myc_txndc5_events$sample_short_id,commpass_myc_tent5c_events$sample_short_id,
                                   commpass_myc_igh_events$sample_short_id,commpass_myc_igk_events$sample_short_id,commpass_myc_igl_events$sample_short_id,
                                   commpass_myc_other_events1$sample_short_id, commpass_myc_other_events2$sample_short_id)) ~ "No event"),
                "all_v_none" = case_when(myc_trx %in% c("TXNDC5/TENT5C","IGH/IGK/IGL") ~ "myc",
                                         myc_trx == "No event" ~ "none"))

# Remove unused placeholders in the genes
paired_wgs_rna_counts <- paired_wgs_rna_counts %>%
                           dplyr::filter(!Gene %in% c("N_ambiguous","N_multimapping","N_noFeature","N_unmapped"))

# Write out the data.table of counts needed for DESeq2 workflow
#fwrite(x = paired_wgs_rna_counts,
#       file = paste0(base_data_path, "commpass_paired_wgs_rna_raw_counts.txt"),
#       sep = "\t",
#       col.names = T)

# Build the conditions file for DESeq2 workflow
# Split by MYC event
commpass_rna_wgs_pairs_myc_conditions <- data.table("sample" = commpass_wgs_rna_pairs_myc_anno$sample_short_id,
                                                    "condition" = commpass_wgs_rna_pairs_myc_anno$all_v_none)

#fwrite(x = commpass_rna_wgs_pairs_myc_conditions,
#       file = paste0(base_data_path, "commpass_paired_wgs_rna_conditions.txt"),
#       sep = "\t",
#       col.names = T)

# Run the DESeq2 workflow
#myc_vs_none <- get_deseq2_diff_expr(counts_file_path = paste0(base_data_path, "commpass_paired_wgs_rna_raw_counts.txt"),
#                                     condition_file_path = paste0(base_data_path, "commpass_paired_wgs_rna_conditions.txt"),
#                                     gene_universe = gene_body_hg38,
#                                     gene_symbol_column = NULL,
#                                     ensembl_id_column = "Gene",
#                                     formula_string = "myc ~ none",
#                                     min_transcripts = 10)
#saveRDS(object = myc_vs_none, file = paste0(base_data_path, "commpass_paired_wgs_rna_myc_deg.rds"))
myc_vs_none <- readRDS(paste0(base_data_path, "commpass_paired_wgs_rna_myc_deg.rds"))


myc_expr_dt <- myc_vs_none$deseq2_tpm_dt %>%
  dplyr::filter(gene == "MYC") %>%
  reshape2::melt(id.vars = "gene",
                 variable.name = "sample_short_id",
                 value.name = "expr") %>%
  dplyr::left_join(y = commpass_wgs_rna_pairs_myc_anno %>%
                         dplyr::select(sample_short_id, myc_trx, all_v_none),
                   by = "sample_short_id")

myc_expr_dt$myc_trx <- factor(myc_expr_dt$myc_trx,
                              levels = c("TXNDC5/TENT5C","IGH/IGK/IGL","No event"))

myc_expr_dt$all_v_none <- factor(myc_expr_dt$myc_trx,
                              levels = c("myc","none"))

myc_expr_wilcox_output <- rstatix::pairwise_wilcox_test(data = myc_expr_dt,
                                                             formula = expr ~ myc_trx,
                                                             p.adjust.method = "fdr")
myc_expr_wilcox_output$p_string <- scales::scientific(myc_expr_wilcox_output$p.adj)

myc_expr_medians <- myc_expr_dt %>%
  dplyr::group_by(myc_trx) %>%
  dplyr::reframe("median_expr" = median(log2(expr + 1)))

myc_expr_boxplots <- ggplot(myc_expr_dt) +
  geom_boxplot(aes(x = myc_trx, y = log2(expr + 1), fill = myc_trx)) +
  labs(x = NULL,
       y = "Log2(TPM + 1)") +
  ggpubr::stat_pvalue_manual(data = myc_expr_wilcox_output[2:3,],
                             label = "P={p_string}",
                             y.position = c(12,11),
                             bracket.nudge.y = 0.01,
                             size = 4.5,
                             tip.length = 0.015) +
  theme(legend.position = "none",
        panel.border = element_rect(fill = NA),
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
```

Epigenomic analysis of ZFP36L1 MER

::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code .code-overflow-wrap  code-fold="true"}
mers_overlap %>%
    dplyr::filter(mer_gene_label == "ZFP36L1") %>%
    dplyr::select(, cds_overlap_pct, intron_overlap_pct, three_prime_utr_overlap_pct) %>%
    dplyr::reframe(mean_cds = mean(cds_overlap_pct), mean_intron = mean(intron_overlap_pct),
        mean_three_prime = mean(three_prime_utr_overlap_pct))
##   mean_cds mean_intron mean_three_prime
## 1     1.95       92.21                0

# Intra-chromosomal deletions or inversions for chr14 MERs Get only the
# intra-chromosomal events
intrachrom_juncs <- gr_refactor_seqs(dt2gr(hq_total_sv_bp_dt %>%
    dplyr::filter(class != "TRA")))
# Annotate each partner junction by nearest coding gene
intrachrom_juncs$nearest_gene <- genes$gene_name[nearest(x = intrachrom_juncs, subject = gr.stripstrand(genes))]

# deletions/inversions that occur between ZFP36L1 / SERPINA9 / TCL1A and IGH
# locus Let's grab all DELs / INVs on chr14q
intrachrom_chr14q <- intrachrom_juncs %Q% (seqnames == "chr14" & start > chr_arm_lengths$q_start[14] &
    class %in% c("DEL", "INV"))

# workflow is to find all BP that are within the 'effective' range from the
# gene/MER of interest This range is slightly different for DELs vs INVs For
# DELs, first BP must be just outside gene/MER on centromeric side but can be
# variable on the IGH locus side due to number of SEs at that region For INVs,
# fist BP can be on either side of gene/MER

# start of IGH region (using beginning of IGHA2) chr14:105583731 ZFP36L1
# chr14:68787662-68793067 SERPINA9 chr14:94462721-94476197 TCL1A
# chr14:95709947-95714125

#################### D E L s #################### ZFP36L1
zfp36l1_final_dels <- data.table()
zfp36l1_dels_end1 <- gr.findoverlaps(query = parse.gr(paste0("chr14:68793067-", 68793067 +
    2500000)), subject = intrachrom_chr14q %Q% (class == "DEL"), scol = c("class",
    "patient", "sample", "sv_id", "bp_order", "nearest_gene"))

# loop through all the deletion BPs that fall in the effective range use these
# to find the corresponding BP and check if in the IGH region
for (i in seq(along = zfp36l1_dels_end1$sv_id)) {
    zfp36l1_dels_end2 <- intrachrom_juncs %Q% (sv_id == zfp36l1_dels_end1$sv_id[i]) %Q%
        (bp_order != zfp36l1_dels_end1$bp_order[i])

    # If in the IGH range, keep the SV ID
    if (length(zfp36l1_dels_end2) > 0) {
        if (gr.in(query = zfp36l1_dels_end2, igh_trx_expected_range)) {
            zfp36l1_final_dels <- rrbind(zfp36l1_final_dels, gr2dt(intrachrom_juncs %Q%
                (sv_id == zfp36l1_dels_end1$sv_id[i])))
        }
    }
}
message("Intra-chromosomal deletions downstream of ZFP36L1 to near IGH region:")
## Intra-chromosomal deletions downstream of ZFP36L1 to near IGH region:
zfp36l1_final_dels %>%
    dplyr::select(seqnames, start, end, strand, class, support, patient, sample,
        bp_order)
##   seqnames     start       end strand class                    support
## 1    chr14  68795182  68795182      +   DEL       manta,delly,igcaller
## 2    chr14 105859665 105859665      -   DEL       manta,delly,igcaller
## 3    chr14  68795184  68795184      +   DEL manta,delly,svaba,igcaller
## 4    chr14 105859690 105859690      -   DEL manta,delly,svaba,igcaller
##       patient       sample bp_order
## 1 60828-DNA-N 60828-DNA-T2        1
## 2 60828-DNA-N 60828-DNA-T2        2
## 3 60828-DNA-N  60828-DNA-T        1
## 4 60828-DNA-N  60828-DNA-T        2

#################### I N V s #################### ZFP36L1
zfp36l1_final_invs <- data.table()
zfp36l1_invs_end1 <- gr.findoverlaps(query = grbind(parse.gr(paste0("chr14:68793067-",
    68793067 + 2500000)), parse.gr(paste0("chr14:", 68787662 - 2500000, "-68787662"))),
    subject = intrachrom_chr14q %Q% (class == "INV"), scol = c("class", "patient",
        "sample", "sv_id", "bp_order", "nearest_gene"))

for (i in seq(along = zfp36l1_invs_end1$sv_id)) {
    zfp36l1_invs_end2 <- intrachrom_juncs %Q% (sv_id == zfp36l1_invs_end1$sv_id[i]) %Q%
        (bp_order != zfp36l1_invs_end1$bp_order[i])

    # If in the IGH range, keep the SV ID
    if (length(zfp36l1_invs_end2) > 0) {

        if (gr.in(query = zfp36l1_invs_end2, igh_trx_expected_range)) {
            zfp36l1_final_invs <- rrbind(zfp36l1_final_invs, gr2dt(intrachrom_juncs %Q%
                (sv_id == zfp36l1_invs_end1$sv_id[i])))
        }
    }
}
message("Intra-chromosomal inversions downstream of ZFP36L1 to near IGH region")
## Intra-chromosomal inversions downstream of ZFP36L1 to near IGH region
zfp36l1_final_dels %>%
    dplyr::select(seqnames, start, end, strand, class, support, patient, sample,
        bp_order)
##   seqnames     start       end strand class                    support
## 1    chr14  68795182  68795182      +   DEL       manta,delly,igcaller
## 2    chr14 105859665 105859665      -   DEL       manta,delly,igcaller
## 3    chr14  68795184  68795184      +   DEL manta,delly,svaba,igcaller
## 4    chr14 105859690 105859690      -   DEL manta,delly,svaba,igcaller
##       patient       sample bp_order
## 1 60828-DNA-N 60828-DNA-T2        1
## 2 60828-DNA-N 60828-DNA-T2        2
## 3 60828-DNA-N  60828-DNA-T        1
## 4 60828-DNA-N  60828-DNA-T        2

# Highly recurrent SNV sites first check if their are duplicated mutations
# patients
muts_per_mer %>%
    dplyr::filter(mut_uniq_id == "snv_er177") %>%
    dplyr::select(seqnames, start, Reference_Allele, Matched_Norm_Sample_Barcode) %>%
    duplicated() %>%
    sum()
## [1] 0

zfp36l1_snv_dups <- duplicated(muts_per_mer %>%
    dplyr::filter(mut_uniq_id == "snv_er177") %>%
    dplyr::select(seqnames, start, Reference_Allele))
zfp36l1_snv_dup_sites_start <- muts_per_mer[mut_uniq_id == "snv_er177"][zfp36l1_snv_dups,
    .(start)]
zfp36l1_snv_foci_counts <- muts_per_mer %>%
    dplyr::filter(start %in% zfp36l1_snv_dup_sites_start$start) %>%
    dplyr::group_by(seqnames, start) %>%
```

Compare Chromatin states between the E/SE groups

::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code .code-overflow-wrap  code-fold="true"}
# Look at Enhancer signal
enhancer_epilogo_group <- e_se_mer_epilogos_props %>%
    dplyr::filter(state %in% c("Strong Enhancer1", "Strong Enhancer2", "Weak Enhancer")) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(state_group = "Enhancer") %>%
    dplyr::group_by(e_se_group, state_group, roi) %>%
    dplyr::reframe(group_state_sum_prop = sum(prop))

enhancer_epilogo_group_mean <- enhancer_epilogo_group %>%
    dplyr::group_by(e_se_group, state_group) %>%
    dplyr::reframe(mean_group_state_sum_prop = mean(group_state_sum_prop))
print("Enhancer states proportion")
## [1] "Enhancer states proportion"
enhancer_epilogo_group_mean
## # A tibble: 3 × 3
##   e_se_group state_group mean_group_state_sum_prop
##   <chr>      <chr>                           <dbl>
## 1 GCB        Enhancer                        0.230
## 2 GCB,PC     Enhancer                        0.515
## 3 PC         Enhancer                        0.153

# Use Dunn test to perform pairwise Kruskal-Wallis test of means
enhancer_epilogo_group_pval <- rstatix::dunn_test(data = enhancer_epilogo_group,
    formula = group_state_sum_prop ~ e_se_group)
enhancer_epilogo_group_pval$p.adj <- round(x = enhancer_epilogo_group_pval$p.adj,
    digits = 3)

enhancer_epilogo_group_plot <- ggplot(enhancer_epilogo_group_mean) + geom_col(aes(x = e_se_group,
    y = mean_group_state_sum_prop, fill = e_se_group)) + labs(x = NULL, y = "Mean Proportion") +
    scale_fill_manual(name = "E/SE-MER\nGroup", values = c("royalblue3", "#540046",
        "orangered3")) + scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 0.73)) + theme(panel.border = element_rect(fill = "transparent"),
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5), panel.background = element_rect(fill = ggplot2::alpha(colour = "#ffcd17",
        alpha = 0.15)), panel.grid = element_blank(), legend.position = "none") +
    ggpubr::stat_pvalue_manual(data = enhancer_epilogo_group_pval[c(1, 3), ], label = "P={p.adj}",
        y.position = c(0.62, 0.65), bracket.nudge.y = 0.01, size = 4.5, tip.length = 0.015) +
    ggpubr::stat_pvalue_manual(data = enhancer_epilogo_group_pval[2, ], label = "{p.adj.signif}",
        y.position = 0.57, bracket.nudge.y = 0.01, size = 4.5, tip.length = 0.015)

# Look at Transcription signal
transcription_epilogo_group <- e_se_mer_epilogos_props %>%
    dplyr::filter(state %in% c("Transcription Transition", "Transcription Elongation",
        "Weak Transcription")) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(state_group = "Transcription") %>%
    dplyr::group_by(e_se_group, state_group, roi) %>%
    dplyr::reframe(group_state_sum_prop = sum(prop))

transcription_epilogo_group_mean <- transcription_epilogo_group %>%
    dplyr::group_by(e_se_group, state_group) %>%
    dplyr::reframe(mean_group_state_sum_prop = mean(group_state_sum_prop))

print("Transcription states proportion")
## [1] "Transcription states proportion"
transcription_epilogo_group_mean
## # A tibble: 3 × 3
##   e_se_group state_group   mean_group_state_sum_prop
##   <chr>      <chr>                             <dbl>
## 1 GCB        Transcription                    0.0742
## 2 GCB,PC     Transcription                    0.121 
## 3 PC         Transcription                    0.434

# Use Dunn test to perform pairwise Kruskal-Wallis test of means

    formula = group_state_sum_prop ~ e_se_group)

    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5), panel.background = element_rect(fill = ggplot2::alpha(colour = "#4fe609",
        alpha = 0.15)), panel.grid = element_blank(), legend.position = "none") +
```

```{.r .cell-code .code-overflow-wrap  code-fold="true"}
# More focused on gene

ideogram_track_txndc5 <- geom_single_chrom_ideogrom(window_of_interest = txndc5_window,

## Scale for x is already present.

scale_track_txndc5 <- geom_locus_scale(window_of_interest = txndc5_window, flank = 0)

gene_track_txndc5 <- geom_gene_track(window_of_interest = txndc5_window, flank = 0,
    genes_of_interest = c("TXNDC5", "BMP6", "BLOC1S5-TXNDC5"), gene_name_side = "three_prime",
    gene_label_nudge = 1000000, gene_label_size = 3)

enhancer_track_txndc5 <- geom_bed_track(data = complete_ee, window_of_interest = txndc5_window,
    flank = 0, aes_y_column = "tissue", aes_fill_column = "enhancer_type", x_axis_theme = "blank",

    theme(legend.position = "none")

mer_track_txndc5 <- geom_bed_track(data = mers_gr, window_of_interest = txndc5_window,
    flank = 0, aes_y_column = "mer_type", aes_fill_column = "mer_type", aes_fill_colors = c("black",
        "#FB832D"), x_axis_theme = "blank") + labs(y = "MERs") + theme(legend.position = "none") +
    theme(legend.position = "none")

gro_seq_antisense_track_txndc5 <- geom_ridge_track(data = lp1_gro_seq_plus_hg38,
    window_of_interest = txndc5_window, flank = 0, aes_y_column = "score", fill_color = "darkred",
    x_axis_theme = "blank") + ylab(" \n \nGRO-Seq") + scale_y_continuous(breaks = breaks_minus_one)
## Scale for y is already present.
## Adding another scale for y, which will replace the existing scale.

gro_seq_sense_track_txndc5 <- geom_ridge_track(data = lp1_gro_seq_minus_hg38, window_of_interest = txndc5_window,
    flank = 0, aes_y_column = "score", fill_color = "dodgerblue", x_axis_theme = "blank") +
    ylab(NULL) + scale_y_continuous(breaks = breaks_minus_one)
## Scale for y is already present.
## Adding another scale for y, which will replace the existing scale.

snv_track_txndc5 <- geom_count_mutations(data = snv_muts_hq_dt, window_of_interest = txndc5_window,
    flank = 0, aes_y_column = "Reference_Allele", aes_fill_color = "#191970", x_axis_theme = "blank") +
    ylab("SNVs") + scale_y_continuous(breaks = breaks_minus_one)
## Scale for y is already present.
## Adding another scale for y, which will replace the existing scale.

snv_vaf_track_txndc5 <- geom_vaf_landscape(data = snv_muts_hq_dt, window_of_interest = txndc5_window,
    flank = 0, contrast = F, x_axis_theme = "blank") + ylab("SNV\nVAF") + ggplot2::scale_color_gradientn(name = "Density",
    colours = paletteer_c("grDevices::GnBu", 15)[1:12]) + theme(legend.position = "none") +
    scale_y_continuous(breaks = breaks_minus_one)
## Scale for colour is already present.
## Adding another scale for colour, which will replace the existing scale.
## Scale for y is already present.
## Adding another scale for y, which will replace the existing scale.

indel_track_txndc5 <- geom_count_mutations(data = indel_muts_hq_dt, window_of_interest = txndc5_window,
    flank = 0, aes_y_column = "Reference_Allele", aes_fill_color = "#EEA9B8", x_axis_theme = "blank") +
    ylab("InDels") + scale_y_continuous(breaks = breaks_minus_one)
## Scale for y is already present.
## Adding another scale for y, which will replace the existing scale.

indel_vaf_track_txndc5 <- geom_vaf_landscape(data = indel_muts_hq_dt, window_of_interest = txndc5_window,
    flank = 0, contrast = F, x_axis_theme = "blank") + ggplot2::scale_color_gradientn(name = "Density",
    colours = rev(paletteer_c("grDevices::Lajolla", 15)[3:14])) + ylab("InDel\nVAF") +
    theme(legend.position = "none") + scale_y_continuous(breaks = breaks_minus_one)
## Scale for colour is already present.
## Adding another scale for colour, which will replace the existing scale.
## Scale for y is already present.
## Adding another scale for y, which will replace the existing scale.

epilogos_track_txndc5 <- geom_epilogo_col(data = mm_epilogos, window_of_interest = txndc5_window,
    flank = 0, x_axis_theme = "blank", contrast = F) + theme(legend.position = "none")
## Warning in dt2gr(data): Warning: Coercing to GRanges via non-standard columns

breakpoint_track_txndc5 <- geom_sv_breakpoints(data = hq_total_sv_bp_dt, window_of_interest = txndc5_window,
    flank = 0, aes_y_column = "patient", aes_fill_column = "class", aes_shape_column = "class",
    x_axis_theme = "blank") + theme(legend.position = "none")

txndc5_locus_view <- patchwork::wrap_plots(list(ideogram_track_txndc5, scale_track_txndc5,
    breakpoint_track_txndc5, indel_track_txndc5, indel_vaf_track_txndc5, snv_track_txndc5,
    snv_vaf_track_txndc5, epilogos_track_txndc5, gro_seq_antisense_track_txndc5,
    gro_seq_sense_track_txndc5, enhancer_track_txndc5, mer_track_txndc5, gene_track_txndc5),
    ncol = 1, heights = c(0.01, 0.015, 0.02, 0.02, 0.02, 0.02, 0.02, 0.02, 0.02,
        0.02, 0.015, 0.015, 0.025))
```
